utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: The train’s arrived, thank God. What’s the time?

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: It will soon be two. [Blows out candle] It is light already.

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: How much was the train late? Two hours at least. [Yawns and stretches himself] I have made a rotten mess of it! I came here on purpose to meet them at the station, and then overslept myself... in my chair. It’s a pity. I wish you’d wakened me.

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: I thought you’d gone away. [Listening] I think I hear them coming.

beat_states.desire_state: to be helpful and attentive, to share in the excitement of the family's return
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to elevate herself beyond her servant status through proximity to the drama and emotion of the household
beat_states.obstacle: she is merely a maid with limited agency; Lopakhin subtly reproaches her for not waking him

affect_state.rationale: Mildly positive anticipation about the family's return, but slightly anxious from Lopakhin's implicit blame. She is alert, listening for sounds of arrival, in a state of eager uncertainty.

social_state.rationale: She is subordinate to Lopakhin and deferential, but warmly engaged — eager to participate in the social event of the homecoming.

known_facts: It is nearly two o'clock in the morning
The train has arrived
Lopakhin was sleeping in the chair
She thought he had already left
hidden_secrets: Her emotional excitability and romantic nature (which will emerge more fully later)
false_beliefs: She believed Lopakhin had already gone to the station
epistemic_state.rationale: Dunyasha has been keeping vigil, aware of the time and light. Her false belief that Lopakhin left is genuine and serves as her defense for not waking him.

beat_states.defense_state: deflecting responsibility by explaining she assumed he had left
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she is nervous and excited about the arrivals but must maintain a composed servant's demeanor
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Dunyasha may have noticed Lopakhin sleeping but chose not to wake him out of timidity or social deference rather than genuinely believing he had left.

beat_states.desire_state: to present himself as someone who cared enough to meet the family at the station, and to mitigate his failure of falling asleep
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to prove his worth and legitimacy to Ranevskaya and the landed class he came from beneath — to transcend his peasant origins
beat_states.obstacle: he overslept and missed the arrival, undermining his careful self-presentation as a reliable, devoted figure

affect_state.rationale: Frustrated and embarrassed at himself for oversleeping. The yawning suggests residual sleepiness but he is agitated by his failure. He feels out of control of the impression he will make.

social_state.rationale: He is materially superior to Dunyasha (a servant) but socially anxious about the returning family. He is warm but slightly self-absorbed, mildly blaming Dunyasha for not waking him.

known_facts: The train has arrived
It was at least two hours late
He came specifically to meet the family at the station
He fell asleep in the chair and missed them
Ranevskaya has been away for five years
hidden_secrets: His deep need for Ranevskaya's approval and recognition stems from his peasant childhood on this estate
He likely already has a business proposition in mind regarding the cherry orchard
false_beliefs: That meeting them at the station would have significantly changed how they perceive him
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin is orienting himself in time and space, anxiously calculating how badly he's botched the welcome. His knowledge of the train schedule and his deliberate journey suggest careful planning now ruined.

beat_states.defense_state: self-deprecation as preemptive defense against judgment — calling it 'a rotten mess' before anyone else can criticize him
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he is a successful businessman who still anxiously seeks approval from the aristocratic family, revealing his deep insecurity about his origins despite his material power
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lopakhin may have unconsciously allowed himself to fall asleep as a form of self-sabotage — his ambivalence about facing Ranevskaya and the complicated emotions she stirs may have produced avoidance.

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: [Listens] No.... They’ve got to collect their luggage and so on.... [Pause] Lubov Andreyevna has been living abroad for five years; I don’t know what she’ll be like now.... She’s a good sort--an easy, simple person. I remember when I was a boy of fifteen, my father, who is dead--he used to keep a shop in the village here--hit me on the face with his fist, and my nose bled.... We had gone into the yard together for something or other, and he was a little drunk. Lubov Andreyevna, as I remember her now, was still young, and very thin, and she took me to the washstand here in this very room, the nursery. She said, “Don’t cry, little man, it’ll be all right in time for your wedding.” [Pause] “Little man”.... My father was a peasant, it’s true, but here I am in a white waistcoat and yellow shoes... a pearl out of an oyster. I’m rich now, with lots of money, but just think about it and examine me, and you’ll find I’m still a peasant down to the marrow of my bones. [Turns over the pages of his book] Here I’ve been reading this book, but I understood nothing. I read and fell asleep. [Pause.]

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: The dogs didn’t sleep all night; they know that they’re coming.

beat_states.desire_state: to participate in the excitement of the family's return and connect with Lopakhin
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to be seen and valued as more than a servant — to matter in this household's emotional life
beat_states.obstacle: Lopakhin is absorbed in his own interior monologue and barely registers her presence

affect_state.rationale: She is excited and slightly nervous about the arrival; her comment is eager but tentative. She senses Lopakhin's emotional rawness and steps gently around it.

social_state.rationale: As a maid speaking to a wealthy merchant, she is clearly subordinate. Her warmth is genuine — the detail about the dogs is affectionate and shows her attunement to the household's emotional rhythms.

known_facts: The family is arriving imminently
The dogs were restless all night, sensing the return
Lopakhin has been waiting and is emotionally stirred
hidden_secrets: She may already be developing romantic feelings for Yasha or Yepikhodov, which will emerge later
epistemic_state.rationale: Dunyasha's knowledge is practical and intuitive — she reads the household's mood through its animals. She does not yet reveal deeper personal concerns.

beat_states.defense_state: deflecting from the heaviness of Lopakhin's confession by offering a lighter, simpler observation
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She wants to engage meaningfully with Lopakhin but can only offer a servant's observation about dogs, reinforcing the very class gap she wishes to transcend
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Dunyasha's line about the dogs could be read as a subtle way of saying 'I too have been awake all night waiting' — making a bid for Lopakhin's attention and shared intimacy in the vigil.

beat_states.desire_state: to make sense of his own identity and worthiness before Lubov Andreyevna's return
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to transcend his peasant origins and be recognized as a legitimate, worthy person
beat_states.obstacle: his deep-seated class shame and the indelible memory of his peasant upbringing that no wealth can erase

affect_state.rationale: The memory of his father's violence and Lubov's tenderness opens deep emotional wounds; he is anxious about the reunion and uncertain who he really is. The pauses reveal unresolved grief and self-doubt beneath outward composure.

social_state.rationale: He has economic power but feels socially inferior to the family he's waiting for. His warmth toward Lubov's memory is genuine — she is the one person from the gentry who showed him kindness.

known_facts: Lubov Andreyevna has lived abroad for five years
The train has arrived
He is now wealthy
His father was a peasant shopkeeper who beat him
Lubov comforted him as a child in this very nursery
hidden_secrets: He may harbor romantic or idealized feelings toward Lubov that he cannot fully articulate
His reading failure and falling asleep with the book shames him — evidence he cannot self-improve
false_beliefs: That his wealth and appearance might eventually override his peasant identity
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin has clear factual knowledge of circumstances but is deeply uncertain about his own inner nature and how others truly see him. The book detail reveals his awareness of the gap between aspiration and reality.

beat_states.defense_state: self-deprecation as preemptive armor — exposing his lowliness before anyone else can
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He simultaneously boasts of his wealth ('white waistcoat and yellow shoes') and insists he is still fundamentally a peasant; he craves validation from the aristocratic world while disclaiming any right to it
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lopakhin's monologue could be read less as genuine vulnerability and more as a rehearsed performance — testing how his self-narrative sounds aloud before Lubov arrives, strategically positioning himself as humble before proposing his business plan.

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: What’s up with you, Dunyasha...?

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: My hands are shaking. I shall faint.

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: You’re too sensitive, Dunyasha. You dress just like a lady, and you do your hair like one too. You oughtn’t. You should know your place.

beat_states.desire_state: to have her emotional state acknowledged and to be seen as a person of refined sensibility
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to transcend her servant status and be recognized as someone with the feelings and worth of a lady
beat_states.obstacle: Lopakhin dismisses her emotional display as pretension rather than genuine feeling, reinforcing her servant status

affect_state.rationale: Highly aroused and genuinely nervous about the family's return; low control as she feels overwhelmed; high vulnerability both because the emotion is real and because Lopakhin's correction stings

social_state.rationale: Low status as a servant being corrected; her warmth comes from eagerness and openness, seeking connection and recognition

known_facts: The family is about to arrive
She has dressed and styled herself carefully
She is physically affected by the anticipation
hidden_secrets: She aspires to be more than a servant and cultivates genteel manners deliberately
She may already be emotionally entangled with Yasha or Yepikhodov
false_beliefs: That performing sensitivity and dressing well can genuinely change her social position
That others will see her refined behavior as natural rather than affected
epistemic_state.rationale: Dunyasha is caught between genuine feeling and social aspiration; she doesn't fully understand how transparent her pretensions are to others

beat_states.defense_state: dramatization — performing her nervousness theatrically to elevate herself above her station
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She genuinely is overcome with emotion, yet she also performs and amplifies that emotion as proof of her refinement — the real and the performed are entangled
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Dunyasha's physical symptoms may be entirely genuine and unperformed — she may simply be an anxious person whom Lopakhin misreads as affected

beat_states.desire_state: to settle Dunyasha down and restore proper order while they wait
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to prove himself worthy and respectable despite his peasant origins — maintaining social boundaries reinforces his own precarious sense of place
beat_states.obstacle: Dunyasha's emotional display disrupts the composed atmosphere he wants to maintain before the family's arrival

affect_state.rationale: Mildly irritated but composed; his correction of Dunyasha gives him a momentary sense of authority, though his own nervousness about Ranevskaya's return simmers underneath

social_state.rationale: Asserts paternal/superior status over Dunyasha as a maid, but his warmth is minimal — he's lecturing rather than comforting, creating distance

known_facts: Dunyasha is a servant who dresses and acts above her station
The family is arriving imminently
Social boundaries matter in this household
hidden_secrets: His own class anxiety — he is deeply insecure about his peasant origins despite his wealth
He recognizes in Dunyasha's pretensions a mirror of his own
false_beliefs: That he has the moral authority to correct someone else's class performance
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin sees clearly what Dunyasha is doing but is blind to his own parallel behavior — a classic Chekhovian irony

beat_states.defense_state: projection — policing Dunyasha's class performance to manage his own anxiety about class boundaries
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He himself is a peasant's son playing at being a gentleman, yet he scolds Dunyasha for doing exactly the same thing — dressing above her station
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lopakhin may genuinely care about Dunyasha and is trying to protect her from embarrassment when the family arrives

utterances.speaker: EPIKHODOV
utterances.text: [Enters with a bouquet. He wears a short jacket and brilliantly polished boots which squeak audibly. He drops the bouquet as he enters, then picks it up] The gardener sent these; says they’re to go into the dining-room. [Gives the bouquet to DUNYASHA.]

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: And you’ll bring me some kvass.

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: Very well. [Exit.]

beat_states.desire_state: to obey promptly and exit the slightly embarrassing situation after her earlier confession of excitement
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to rise above her station and be seen as refined and sensitive, not merely a servant
beat_states.obstacle: she is bound by her role to take orders and cannot linger or express herself freely

affect_state.rationale: Slightly flustered from the previous beat where she revealed her emotional state. The arrival of Epikhodov and Lopakhin's command give her a task to anchor to, reducing her agitation.

social_state.rationale: Lowest status in the room — takes orders from Lopakhin, receives the bouquet from Epikhodov. Warm enough to comply without resentment.

known_facts: The family is arriving
Lopakhin wants kvass
The flowers are for the dining room
hidden_secrets: Her heart is pounding with excitement about the arrival, which she shared with Lopakhin
false_beliefs: That her emotional sensitivity makes her more like the gentry than the other servants
epistemic_state.rationale: Dunyasha knows her duties but her inner world is preoccupied with romantic and social aspirations beyond her station.

beat_states.defense_state: retreating into dutiful service to recover composure after her earlier emotional outburst to Lopakhin
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: wants to be treated as a delicate person of feeling but must perform as an obedient maid
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: She may already be registering Epikhodov's attention and beginning to calculate her romantic options

beat_states.desire_state: to fulfill his errand competently and be noticed as a capable, dignified person
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to be taken seriously and respected despite his chronic bad luck and clumsiness
beat_states.obstacle: his own physical awkwardness betrays him — he drops the bouquet immediately upon entering, undermining the dignity he craves

affect_state.rationale: The dropped bouquet is a small humiliation; he recovers quickly but the squeaking boots and fumble reveal chronic low control over his environment. He's mildly embarrassed but pushes through with formal speech.

social_state.rationale: Low-status clerk delivering flowers on behalf of the gardener — a servant's errand. He's polite and dutiful but commands no authority in this room.

known_facts: The gardener sent flowers for the dining room
The family is expected to arrive
Dunyasha is present
hidden_secrets: His romantic interest in Dunyasha (developing)
false_beliefs: That his polished boots and formal manner project an impressive image
epistemic_state.rationale: Epikhodov knows his task and executes it, but has limited self-awareness about how his clumsiness reads to others.

beat_states.defense_state: overcompensation through formal speech and meticulous appearance (polished boots, careful diction)
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: desperately wants to appear competent and polished while his body constantly betrays incompetence
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: He may have deliberately sought this errand to be near Dunyasha, using the flowers as a pretext

beat_states.desire_state: to dismiss this interruption and maintain focus on the arrival of the family
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to prove his worth to Ranevskaya and transcend his peasant origins through the cherry orchard plan
beat_states.obstacle: the waiting is dragging on and he's surrounded by servants rather than the people who matter to him

affect_state.rationale: Mildly impatient. He doesn't engage with Epikhodov at all — barely registers him. The kvass request is a way to manage the waiting and assert normalcy.

social_state.rationale: He treats the servants transactionally — no warmth, just commands. He occupies a middle position: higher than servants, lower than the arriving gentry.

known_facts: The train has arrived
The family will come soon
He has a business proposal for Ranevskaya about the cherry orchard
hidden_secrets: His deep emotional attachment to Ranevskaya from childhood
His anxiety about whether his proposal will be received well
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin is pragmatically aware of the situation but not fully aware of his own emotional investment in Ranevskaya's approval.

beat_states.defense_state: asserting authority through a casual command — ordering kvass to reestablish his position as someone who gives orders, not receives them
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: a self-made merchant who still feels like a peasant's son, asserting dominance over servants while anxiously awaiting approval from the gentry

utterances.speaker: EPIKHODOV
utterances.text: There’s a frost this morning--three degrees, and the cherry-trees are all in flower. I can’t approve of our climate. [Sighs] I can’t. Our climate is indisposed to favour us even this once. And, Ermolai Alexeyevitch, allow me to say to you, in addition, that I bought myself some boots two days ago, and I beg to assure you that they squeak in a perfectly unbearable manner. What shall I put on them?

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: Go away. You bore me.

utterances.speaker: EPIKHODOV
utterances.text: Some misfortune happens to me every day. But I don’t complain; I’m used to it, and I can smile. [DUNYASHA comes in and brings LOPAKHIN some kvass] I shall go. [Knocks over a chair] There.... [Triumphantly] There, you see, if I may use the word, what circumstances I am in, so to speak. It is even simply marvellous. [Exit.]

beat_states.desire_state: To be acknowledged and taken seriously by Lopakhin, to establish human connection through sharing his misfortunes
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To be seen as a person of significance despite the universe conspiring against him — to overcome his identity as 'Two-and-Twenty Troubles'
beat_states.obstacle: Lopakhin is completely uninterested and dismissive; Epikhodov's own clumsiness and rambling manner undercut his attempts at dignity

affect_state.rationale: Epikhodov lives in a state of chronic low-grade distress masked by philosophical posturing. He is bewildered by the world's hostility toward him, has no control over his own body or circumstances, and is highly vulnerable beneath his performative acceptance.

social_state.rationale: He is low-status — a clerk trying to engage his social superior who openly dismisses him. His warmth is genuine if awkward; he approaches Lopakhin without hostility, seeking connection.

known_facts: There is a frost of three degrees and the cherry trees are flowering
He bought new boots two days ago that squeak terribly
Misfortune follows him daily
Lopakhin is his social superior
hidden_secrets: He is likely here partly because Dunyasha is present — his romantic interest in her
His bouquet from the previous beat was likely intended for someone
false_beliefs: That his philosophical framing of his misfortunes makes him seem interesting or sympathetic rather than tiresome
That knocking over the chair 'triumphantly' proves his point in a way that earns respect
epistemic_state.rationale: Epikhodov has accurate observational knowledge but a deeply distorted sense of how he is perceived. He mistakes his performed suffering for charm or philosophical depth.

beat_states.defense_state: Intellectualization and philosophical framing of his suffering — transforming humiliation into a performed, almost literary identity. His 'triumphant' reaction to knocking over the chair converts shame into a proof of his thesis.
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He claims he doesn't complain and can smile, yet everything he says is a complaint. He performs acceptance of misfortune while desperately seeking sympathy for it.
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Epikhodov may be more self-aware than he appears — his 'triumphant' reaction to the knocked chair could be a deliberate clowning strategy to preempt mockery, a survival tactic rather than genuine obliviousness.

beat_states.desire_state: To be left alone so he can focus on the imminent arrival of Ranevskaya
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: Lopakhin's deepest want is to prove his worth and transcend his peasant origins — Ranevskaya's arrival is the pivotal event through which he hopes to demonstrate his transformation and present his plan for the cherry orchard
beat_states.obstacle: Epikhodov's tedious, rambling presence is wasting his time and attention during a moment of high anticipation

affect_state.rationale: Irritated but in command of this interaction. His anxiety about Ranevskaya's arrival gives him low-level negative affect, but with Epikhodov he feels completely certain of his superiority and exercises control effortlessly.

social_state.rationale: He asserts clear dominance over Epikhodov with zero warmth — 'Go away. You bore me' is as cold and dismissive as possible. His status relative to Epikhodov is unquestioned.

known_facts: Ranevskaya's train is expected imminently
He has a business proposal for the cherry orchard
Epikhodov is a hapless clerk of no consequence to him
hidden_secrets: His deep emotional attachment to Ranevskaya from childhood
His anxiety about whether he will be accepted by her as an equal
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin's knowledge state is practical and forward-looking. He is not interested in Epikhodov's world at all — his mind is entirely on the arrival.

beat_states.defense_state: Blunt, curt rejection — no engagement with the content of Epikhodov's speech, shutting down interaction immediately
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: Lopakhin, who was just moments ago anxiously reflecting on his own unworthiness and peasant background, now exercises casual dominance over a lower-status man — revealing the class hierarchy he both resents and perpetuates

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: I may confess to you, Ermolai Alexeyevitch, that Epikhodov has proposed to me.

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: Ah!

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: I don’t know what to do about it. He’s a nice young man, but every now and again, when he begins talking, you can’t understand a word he’s saying. I think I like him. He’s madly in love with me. He’s an unlucky man; every day something happens. We tease him about it. They call him “Two-and-twenty troubles.”

beat_states.desire_state: to confide in Lopakhin and receive guidance or validation about Epikhodov's proposal
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to elevate herself beyond her station as a servant and be recognized as a woman of feeling and romantic importance
beat_states.obstacle: her own confusion about her feelings and the social gap between her and Lopakhin that makes this confession slightly inappropriate

affect_state.rationale: She is excited and flattered by the proposal but genuinely confused about her feelings. The confession itself is an act of vulnerability—sharing private romantic news with a social superior. Her mixed description of Epikhodov (nice but incomprehensible, lovable but unlucky) reveals genuine ambivalence.

social_state.rationale: She is a maid confiding in her employer's associate, which lowers her status, but she's performing warmth and intimacy to create a sense of closeness with Lopakhin, treating him as a confidant rather than a master.

known_facts: Epikhodov has formally proposed to her
Epikhodov is in love with her
Epikhodov is considered unlucky and is nicknamed 'Two-and-twenty troubles'
She finds him sometimes incomprehensible
hidden_secrets: She may be more attracted to people of higher status than Epikhodov
Her romantic sensibility may be more performative than genuine
false_beliefs: That Lopakhin is an appropriate and interested confidant for her romantic life
epistemic_state.rationale: Dunyasha has concrete knowledge of the proposal and Epikhodov's character but lacks self-knowledge about her own desires. She treats Lopakhin as a peer-confidant, misreading the social dynamic.

beat_states.defense_state: deflecting her own romantic uncertainty by cataloguing Epikhodov's faults and ridiculousness
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she says she doesn't know what to do but simultaneously enjoys the attention and status of being desired; she mocks him while admitting she likes him
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Dunyasha could be deliberately performing romantic distress to make herself seem more interesting and refined to Lopakhin, using the confession as flirtation rather than genuine seeking of advice.

beat_states.desire_state: to politely acknowledge Dunyasha's confession without getting drawn into servant gossip while waiting for the family's arrival
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to secure his social and economic transformation by persuading Ranevskaya about the cherry orchard plan—Dunyasha's love life is irrelevant to his mission
beat_states.obstacle: Dunyasha is pulling him into an intimate conversation he has no investment in, wasting time before the crucial arrival

affect_state.rationale: He is emotionally uninvested in this conversation. His 'Ah!' is perfunctory—neither warm nor cold. His arousal is low because this topic doesn't engage him. He maintains control by refusing to be drawn in.

social_state.rationale: Lopakhin holds higher social status than Dunyasha and his minimal response reinforces the distance. He's not unkind but shows no real warmth or interest in her romantic dilemma.

known_facts: Epikhodov has proposed to Dunyasha
Epikhodov is considered clumsy and unlucky
The family is due to arrive imminently
hidden_secrets: His mind is preoccupied with his business proposal for the cherry orchard
He may be anxious about his own unresolved feelings toward Ranevskaya
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin absorbs the information passively. His mind is elsewhere—on the arriving family and his plans. He has no stake in this knowledge.

beat_states.defense_state: minimal engagement—his single 'Ah!' functions as a polite wall to avoid deeper involvement
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: as a man of peasant origin he might naturally empathize with servant concerns, but his ambitions now place him psychologically above such matters
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lopakhin's 'Ah!' could contain a moment of genuine surprise or wistfulness—perhaps briefly reminded of his own romantic inadequacies or his failure to propose to Varya.

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: [Listens] There they come, I think.

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: They’re coming! What’s the matter with me? I’m cold all over.

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: There they are, right enough. Let’s go and meet them. Will she know me? We haven’t seen each other for five years.

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: [Excited] I shall faint in a minute.... Oh, I’m fainting! [Two carriages are heard driving up to the house. LOPAKHIN and DUNYASHA quickly go out. The stage is empty. A noise begins in the next room. FIERS, leaning on a stick, walks quickly across the stage; he has just been to meet LUBOV ANDREYEVNA. He wears an old-fashioned livery and a tall hat. He is saying something to himself, but not a word of it can be made out. The noise behind the stage gets louder and louder. A voice is heard: “Let’s go in there.” Enter LUBOV ANDREYEVNA, ANYA, and CHARLOTTA IVANOVNA with a little dog on a chain, and all dressed in travelling clothes, VARYA in a long coat and with a kerchief on her head. GAEV, SIMEONOV-PISCHIN, LOPAKHIN, DUNYASHA with a parcel and an umbrella, and a servant with luggage--all cross the room.]

beat_states.desire_state: To experience and perform the emotional intensity of this dramatic moment — to feel important and noticed
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To be seen as a refined, sensitive person of consequence rather than a mere servant
beat_states.obstacle: Her actual low social position and the fact that no one is particularly focused on her emotional state

affect_state.rationale: Overwhelmed by arousal and excitement, she has lost control of her physical and emotional state — or is performing that loss of control. The moment is thrilling but destabilizing for her.

social_state.rationale: She is a maid, clearly subordinate, but her emotional expressiveness is a bid for attention and connection. She performs warmth and sensitivity as markers of gentility.

known_facts: The family is arriving
Epikhodov has proposed to her
She has been away from the family for five years like everyone else on the estate
hidden_secrets: Her exaggerated emotional responses are partly performative — she models herself on the genteel women she serves
false_beliefs: She believes her heightened emotional sensitivity proves she is a person of refinement rather than a simple maid
epistemic_state.rationale: Dunyasha knows the surface facts but her self-perception is filtered through romantic aspirations that distort her self-understanding.

beat_states.defense_state: Performing exaggerated physical symptoms (cold, fainting) to externalize and legitimize her inner excitement
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She is a household servant with no real personal stake in this arrival, yet she experiences (or performs) the most extreme emotional reaction of anyone present
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Dunyasha's physical symptoms could be read as genuinely overwhelming anxiety — perhaps she fears changes in household dynamics that could affect her position or her relationship with Epikhodov.

beat_states.desire_state: To be recognized and accepted by Lubov Andreyevna upon her return
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To prove his worth and transcend his peasant origins by gaining the respect and intimacy of the landowning class
beat_states.obstacle: Five years of separation and the uncertainty of whether she will remember him or acknowledge him warmly

affect_state.rationale: Excited and hopeful at the arrival but deeply uncertain about whether Lubov will know him or treat him warmly. His question 'Will she know me?' reveals naked vulnerability beneath his confident exterior.

social_state.rationale: Despite his wealth, he positions himself as a subordinate greeting a returning mistress. His warmth is genuine — he has deep affection and gratitude toward Lubov's family.

known_facts: The carriages are arriving
He hasn't seen Lubov Andreyevna in five years
He was once a peasant boy on this estate and she was kind to him
The estate is in financial trouble
hidden_secrets: He harbors a plan to save/acquire the cherry orchard that will fundamentally alter the family's relationship to their land
His emotional attachment to Lubov is deeper and more complex than he lets on
false_beliefs: He may believe that proving his success will earn him full acceptance into this world
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin knows the practical situation well but is uncertain about the emotional dynamics of the reunion.

beat_states.defense_state: Deflecting his anxiety into purposeful action — rushing to meet them rather than sitting with his nervousness
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He is a successful, powerful businessman yet is reduced to the anxious vulnerability of a child hoping to be recognized by a surrogate maternal figure
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lopakhin's eagerness could be read as primarily strategic — wanting to be the first to greet her in order to establish influence before presenting his business proposal about the orchard.

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: Let’s come through here. Do you remember what this room is, mother?

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: [Joyfully, through her tears] The nursery!

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: How cold it is! My hands are quite numb. [To LUBOV ANDREYEVNA] Your rooms, the white one and the violet one, are just as they used to be, mother.

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: My dear nursery, oh, you beautiful room.... I used to sleep here when I was a baby. [Weeps] And here I am like a little girl again. [Kisses her brother, VARYA, then her brother again] And Varya is just as she used to be, just like a nun. And I knew Dunyasha. [Kisses her.]

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: The train was two hours late. There now; how’s that for punctuality?

beat_states.desire_state: to guide her mother back into the emotional sanctuary of their home and rekindle her connection to this place
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to restore her mother's bond to the family and the estate so that everything can be saved
beat_states.obstacle: uncertainty about whether the homecoming can actually heal what has been broken

affect_state.rationale: Anya is tender and eager, leading her mother through the house with gentle excitement, but underneath she knows the gravity of their financial situation

social_state.rationale: She positions herself as caretaker-daughter, deferring to her mother's authority while radiating filial warmth

known_facts: The estate is in financial peril
Her mother has been away for years
This room is the nursery
hidden_secrets: She may already know more about the dire financial situation than she lets on in this moment
false_beliefs: That bringing mother home may be enough to fix things
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya knows the practical reality but chooses to lead with emotional reconnection first

beat_states.psychological_contradiction: hopeful excitement about reunion while carrying anxiety about the estate's future
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Anya may be performing cheerfulness to manage her mother's fragile emotional state

beat_states.desire_state: to deflect from the overwhelming emotion in the room and reassert a sense of normalcy and conversational control
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve his dignity and the appearance of order in a world that is slipping away from him
beat_states.obstacle: the room is flooded with his sister's tears and kisses, leaving him emotionally exposed and without a comfortable role to play

affect_state.rationale: Gaev is uncomfortable with the depth of feeling in the room. His flat, slightly irritable remark about the train reveals someone who cannot metabolize emotion and must convert it into mundane complaint.

social_state.rationale: As the brother and nominal male head of household he has some status, but his irrelevant comment makes him seem slightly peripheral and out of touch

known_facts: The train was two hours late
His sister has returned after years abroad
The estate is in financial trouble
hidden_secrets: His own ineffectuality in managing the estate's crisis
His habit of making speeches to furniture to avoid real problems
false_beliefs: That maintaining composure and citing facts constitutes handling the situation
epistemic_state.rationale: Gaev knows the facts but avoids their emotional and practical implications through habitual deflection

beat_states.defense_state: intellectualization and trivial complaint — he retreats to a banal observation about train schedules to avoid emotional engagement
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he has just been kissed by his weeping sister and is clearly moved, yet immediately pivots to a complaint about punctuality as if nothing emotional is happening
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Gaev's comment about the train could be a gentle attempt at humor to lighten the emotional intensity, rather than pure avoidance

beat_states.desire_state: to dissolve into the overwhelming emotion of homecoming and recover the innocence and safety of her childhood
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to recapture a lost paradise — the emotional world of her past before grief and ruin
beat_states.obstacle: the nursery triggers grief as much as joy; the passage of time and everything she has lost stands between her and the comfort she seeks

affect_state.rationale: She is overwhelmed — crying through joy, kissing everyone compulsively. Her emotions are ungovernable. The nursery cracks her open completely.

social_state.rationale: As the returning matriarch she holds natural status, but she exercises it entirely through lavish affection rather than authority

known_facts: She has been away for years
This is the nursery where she slept as a child
Her children and household are here
hidden_secrets: The full extent of her financial recklessness abroad
Her ongoing attachment to the lover in Paris
false_beliefs: That this emotional return can somehow restore what has been lost
That things are 'just as they used to be'
epistemic_state.rationale: Lubov is willfully refusing to engage with present reality, choosing to inhabit the emotional past

beat_states.defense_state: regression — she reverts to childlike behavior to avoid confronting the adult reality of her return
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: ecstatic joy and deep grief are simultaneous; she weeps and kisses, unable to separate homecoming from mourning

beat_states.desire_state: to demonstrate that she has faithfully maintained the household and deserves her mother's approval and love
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to secure her place and purpose within the family by being indispensable
beat_states.obstacle: her mother's emotional flood is directed everywhere at once, making it hard for Varya to receive specific recognition; the cold and practical details feel inadequate next to Lubov's operatic emotion

affect_state.rationale: She is emotionally stirred but contains herself, presenting practical information. Her complaint about the cold is a displaced expression of her own emotional exposure.

social_state.rationale: She occupies the adopted daughter / household manager role — dutiful, warm but subordinate, seeking validation through service

known_facts: The rooms have been preserved as they were
The estate is in financial danger
She has been running the household in Lubov's absence
hidden_secrets: Her unresolved hope that Lopakhin will propose
Her deep anxiety about her own future if the estate is sold
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya knows the practical reality better than anyone but suppresses it in this emotional moment

beat_states.defense_state: practicality as emotional armor — she reports on rooms and temperature rather than expressing her own feelings
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: deeply moved by her mother's return but channels all emotion into caretaking details, as if she doesn't feel permitted to simply feel
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: The 'nun' comparison from Lubov may sting Varya more than she shows, touching her fear of being forever unmarried

utterances.speaker: CHARLOTTA
utterances.text: [To PISCHIN] My dog eats nuts too.

utterances.speaker: PISCHIN
utterances.text: [Astonished] To think of that, now!

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: We did have to wait for you!

beat_states.desire_state: to insert herself into conversation and attract attention through eccentric behavior
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to establish identity and belonging in a world where she feels fundamentally rootless and unknown
beat_states.obstacle: she is a peripheral figure in this household, a governess with no real family or place

affect_state.rationale: Charlotta delivers a bizarre, deadpan observation — her affect is deliberately flattened and controlled, masking deeper loneliness beneath performative strangeness

social_state.rationale: As governess she occupies an ambiguous social position — not quite servant, not quite family. Her odd remark keeps others at arm's length while demanding notice

known_facts: she is a governess in this household
she has a dog
the family has just arrived home
hidden_secrets: she knows almost nothing about her own origins or parents
epistemic_state.rationale: Charlotta's fundamental epistemic condition is not knowing who she is — her non-sequitur about the dog reflects her habit of deflecting from deeper unknowing

beat_states.defense_state: deflection through eccentricity — uses absurd non-sequiturs to avoid genuine emotional engagement
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: craves connection and recognition but only knows how to attract attention through oddity and performance
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: She may be genuinely trying to make friendly small talk with Pischin, simply lacking social graces rather than performing eccentricity

beat_states.desire_state: to be noticed and acknowledged by the returning family, to express that their absence affected her
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to be recognized as significant and elevated beyond her servant status
beat_states.obstacle: she is a maid and easily overlooked in the excitement of the homecoming

affect_state.rationale: Dunyasha is excited by the homecoming but also slightly aggrieved — she wants to convey that the waiting was hard, implying she deserves sympathy and attention

social_state.rationale: As a maid she is low status but pushes for intimacy and warmth, trying to close the gap between servant and family through emotional expressiveness

known_facts: the family has been away for years
she has been waiting up for their arrival
Epikhodov has proposed to her
hidden_secrets: Epikhodov's proposal and her own romantic agitation
false_beliefs: she may believe her emotional displays elevate her in others' eyes rather than marking her as a servant overstepping
epistemic_state.rationale: Dunyasha knows the practical details of the household but misreads social boundaries, believing emotional intensity signals refinement

beat_states.defense_state: mild guilt-tripping to assert her importance and emotional claim on the household
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: wants to be seen as refined and emotionally sensitive but her complaint reveals her subordinate position of having to wait
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Her remark may be purely practical and good-natured rather than a bid for attention — simply expressing shared relief that the wait is over

beat_states.desire_state: to be agreeable and participate in the social moment of the homecoming
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to maintain social connections and goodwill that might lead to financial assistance
beat_states.obstacle: Charlotta's remark is so absurd it catches him off guard, leaving him momentarily without a real conversational foothold

affect_state.rationale: Pischin's astonishment is performative and slightly bewildered — he is a man who bounces along the surface of social situations, genuinely surprised by everything because he processes little deeply

social_state.rationale: Pischin is perpetually warm and deferential, always eager to be liked and included; his 'astonished' response is a way of validating Charlotta and keeping rapport

known_facts: the family has returned
he is here likely to ask for money
Charlotta's dog apparently eats nuts
hidden_secrets: he is in severe financial difficulty and needs to borrow money
false_beliefs: he may believe his perpetual optimism about money turning up is warranted
epistemic_state.rationale: Pischin operates with a cheerful obliviousness, always expecting things will work out despite evidence to the contrary

beat_states.defense_state: performing astonishment to mask intellectual emptiness — reflexive agreeableness
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: presents himself as genuinely amazed by trivia while his real preoccupation is always financial desperation
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: His astonishment could be genuine — he may truly be a man who finds wonder in small things, reflecting an innocent rather than calculated nature

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: I didn’t get any sleep for four nights on the journey.... I’m awfully cold.

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: You went away during Lent, when it was snowing and frosty, but now? Darling! [Laughs and kisses her] We did have to wait for you, my joy, my pet.... I must tell you at once, I can’t bear to wait a minute.

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: [Tired] Something else now...?

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: The clerk, Epikhodov, proposed to me after Easter.

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: Always the same.... [Puts her hair straight] I’ve lost all my hairpins.... [She is very tired, and even staggers as she walks.]

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: I don’t know what to think about it. He loves me, he loves me so much!

beat_states.desire_state: to rest, to be left alone, to recover from the exhausting journey
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to find stability and peace in a world that is shifting beneath her family
beat_states.obstacle: Dunyasha's excited chatter demands emotional energy Anya doesn't have

affect_state.rationale: Physically depleted after four sleepless nights, cold, staggering — her body is failing her. The weary 'something else now?' and 'always the same' reveal low-level irritation layered over deep fatigue. She is too spent to maintain composure or interest.

social_state.rationale: As the young mistress she holds higher status than Dunyasha, but she's too exhausted to exercise it warmly or firmly. Her responses are minimal and slightly dismissive, though not unkind — just depleted.

known_facts: She has just returned from Paris after a difficult journey
The family's financial situation is precarious
Dunyasha always has gossip and drama
hidden_secrets: The full weight of what she witnessed of her mother's situation in Paris
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya carries knowledge of the family's troubles from the journey but is too tired to process or share anything. Her 'always the same' suggests she knows Dunyasha's pattern of breathless revelations.

beat_states.defense_state: withdrawal — retreating into physical exhaustion as a shield against engagement
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She wants to be home and feel welcomed, but the reality of home immediately imposes its trivial burdens on her
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Her 'always the same' could carry a deeper melancholy — a recognition that while she has been through transformative experiences abroad, nothing at home has changed, foreshadowing her growing disconnection from this world.

beat_states.desire_state: to share her news about Epikhodov's proposal and receive validation and attention from Anya
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to elevate herself beyond her servant status through romantic significance and emotional connection with her betters
beat_states.obstacle: Anya is physically exhausted and emotionally unavailable, unable to give the excited response Dunyasha craves

affect_state.rationale: Genuinely excited and emotionally overflowing — the laughter, kissing, the 'I can't bear to wait a minute' all signal high positive arousal. But underneath there's real uncertainty about the proposal and a need for someone to help her process it.

social_state.rationale: As a servant she is below Anya, but she treats her with intimate familiarity — 'my joy, my pet' — almost as a sister or confidante, which reveals her aspiration to close the social gap through emotional closeness.

known_facts: Epikhodov proposed to her after Easter
Epikhodov loves her deeply
Anya has been away since Lent
hidden_secrets: Her own class aspirations masked as romantic confusion
false_beliefs: That Anya is available and interested in hearing her romantic news right now
That her romantic dilemma carries the same weight for others as it does for her
epistemic_state.rationale: Dunyasha is genuinely uncertain about Epikhodov but also oblivious to Anya's state — she's so consumed by her own emotional world that she misreads the room entirely.

beat_states.defense_state: manic enthusiasm — using breathless excitement to override any signal that Anya isn't interested
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She says she doesn't know what to think about Epikhodov's proposal, yet she's bursting to tell it — she wants the drama of being pursued more than she wants advice
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Dunyasha's effusiveness could be partly performative — she may be imitating the emotional register of the gentry women she serves, playing at being a romantic heroine rather than genuinely overwhelmed.

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: [Looks into her room; in a gentle voice] My room, my windows, as if I’d never gone away. I’m at home! To-morrow morning I’ll get up and have a run in the garden....Oh, if I could only get to sleep! I didn’t sleep the whole journey, I was so bothered.

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: Peter Sergeyevitch came two days ago.

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: [Joyfully] Peter!

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: He sleeps in the bath-house, he lives there. He said he was afraid he’d be in the way. [Looks at her pocket-watch] I ought to wake him, but Barbara Mihailovna told me not to. “Don’t wake him,” she said.

beat_states.desire_state: to reclaim the comfort and safety of home and finally rest
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve the innocence and security of her childhood world (the cherry orchard, the estate, the family)
beat_states.obstacle: physical exhaustion and emotional overstimulation from the journey prevent her from settling into peace; also the excitement about Petya disrupts her attempt to rest

affect_state.rationale: Warm nostalgia and joy at being home, spiking further at Petya's name. But exhaustion and sleeplessness leave her physically and emotionally unguarded. She cannot control her own body's need for rest.

social_state.rationale: As daughter of the estate she holds social rank over Dunyasha, but her manner is gentle and intimate, treating Dunyasha as a confidante rather than a servant.

known_facts: She has returned home after a long journey
She hasn't slept in four nights
Her room is unchanged
Peter Sergeyevitch (Trofimov) has arrived at the estate
hidden_secrets: Her growing romantic feelings for Trofimov
The depth of her anxiety about the family's financial ruin
false_beliefs: That returning home means things can go back to how they were
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya clings to the illusion that her childhood room and garden represent stability, not yet fully confronting that the estate is about to be lost. Her joyful exclamation at Petya's name reveals feelings she may not fully acknowledge.

beat_states.defense_state: regression — retreating into childlike wonder at her room to ward off the anxieties of the journey and the family's financial crisis
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she desperately wants sleep and rest but is simultaneously electrified by the news of Petya's arrival, pulling her toward wakefulness
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: The joy at Petya's name could be purely platonic — excitement at seeing her late brother's former tutor who represents intellectual stimulation rather than romance.

beat_states.desire_state: to share gossip and be treated as an intimate equal by Anya
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to transcend her servant status and be recognized as a person of feeling and consequence
beat_states.obstacle: her servant role limits how much she can insert herself; Varya's authority constrains her actions (told not to wake Petya)

affect_state.rationale: Pleased to have interesting news to deliver and to see Anya's excited reaction. Moderately aroused by the social exchange. Some vulnerability in her subordinate position but she navigates it with practiced ease.

social_state.rationale: Servant status below Anya, but the late-night intimacy and shared girlish confidence creates genuine warmth between them.

known_facts: Trofimov arrived two days ago
Trofimov is sleeping in the bath-house
Varya instructed her not to wake him
Trofimov said he didn't want to be in the way
hidden_secrets: Her own romantic preoccupations (with Yepikhodov/Yasha) which color how she reads others' excitement
epistemic_state.rationale: Dunyasha is a reliable source of household intelligence. She knows the practical arrangements and social dynamics. She strategically drops Petya's name knowing it will provoke a reaction from Anya.

beat_states.defense_state: performing compliance — citing Varya's instructions to deflect responsibility for not having woken Petya
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she wants to be Anya's confidante and equal but simultaneously defers to the household hierarchy by citing Varya's orders
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Dunyasha may be entirely innocent in mentioning Petya — simply reporting household news without any strategic awareness of Anya's feelings.

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: Dunyasha, some coffee, quick. Mother wants some.

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: This minute. [Exit.]

beat_states.desire_state: to fulfill the order quickly and prove herself a responsive, valued servant
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to rise above her station and be recognized as more than a simple maid
beat_states.obstacle: she is constrained by her servant role and must suppress her own excitement and personal feelings to attend to duties

affect_state.rationale: Dunyasha is compliant but internally agitated; she has limited control over her situation and is emotionally stirred by the night's events, though the task itself is straightforward

social_state.rationale: She is clearly subordinate to Varya, responding with deference and immediacy; her warmth is present in her willingness and eagerness to serve

known_facts: Varya wants coffee prepared for Lyubov immediately
The family has just arrived from a long journey
Yepikhodov has proposed to her
hidden_secrets: She is emotionally preoccupied with Yepikhodov's proposal and her own romantic aspirations
false_beliefs: She may believe she has become as refined as a lady
epistemic_state.rationale: Dunyasha knows the domestic routines and has personal knowledge she has been bursting to share all night, but in this moment duty takes precedence

beat_states.defense_state: eager obedience masks her desire for personal recognition and romantic preoccupations
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she performs the dutiful servant while internally she is buzzing with her own emotional drama—her feelings about Yepikhodov's proposal and the excitement of the family's return
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Dunyasha's quick exit may also be relief at escaping the emotionally charged room to collect herself

beat_states.desire_state: to ensure her mother's comfort is attended to immediately upon arrival
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to hold the household and family together through responsible caretaking
beat_states.obstacle: she cannot personally attend to everything at once; she must delegate while managing the emotional chaos of the homecoming

affect_state.rationale: Varya is in her element giving orders and managing domestic affairs, which provides a sense of control, but underlying anxiety about the family's situation keeps her arousal elevated and valence only slightly positive

social_state.rationale: She speaks to Dunyasha with clear hierarchical authority as the household manager; the instruction is brisk and functional rather than warm, though not unkind

known_facts: Lyubov has just arrived home after a long journey
Mother wants coffee
She is responsible for the household's daily functioning
The estate is in financial danger
hidden_secrets: She harbors deep anxiety about the estate's impending auction
She hopes Lopakhin will propose to her
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya is the most practically informed member of the household about its day-to-day operations and its financial peril

beat_states.defense_state: displacement into practical tasks to avoid confronting emotional overwhelm of the family's return and the estate's precarious situation
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she performs calm authority while internally anxious about whether she can keep everything from falling apart
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Varya may also be subtly performing competence for her mother's benefit, demonstrating that the household has been well-managed in her absence

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: Well, you’ve come, glory be to God. Home again. [Caressing her] My darling is back again! My pretty one is back again!

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: I did have an awful time, I tell you.

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: I can just imagine it!

beat_states.desire_state: to be comforted and to unburden herself of the stress of the journey
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to return home and recover the security and innocence of childhood
beat_states.obstacle: she is exhausted, cold, and carrying unspoken knowledge about her mother's dire situation in Paris

affect_state.rationale: Anya is depleted — physically exhausted and emotionally raw. The warmth of homecoming provides slight positive valence, but she's fundamentally overwhelmed and uncertain about the future. High vulnerability as she lets herself be the child again.

social_state.rationale: She accepts the role of the younger sister being fussed over — submitting to Varya's caretaking. Warm and receptive but yielding status.

known_facts: The journey was grueling
Her mother's life in Paris was chaotic and financially irresponsible
She is home at last
The estate is beloved to her
hidden_secrets: The full extent of Ranevskaya's spending and her relationship with the man in Paris
How dire she perceives the financial situation to be
false_beliefs: That being home might restore things to how they were
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya has seen things in Paris that have shaken her but she withholds the specifics for now, letting herself sink into the comfort of arrival.

beat_states.defense_state: minimizing the deeper troubles by framing suffering as physical discomfort ('awful time') rather than revealing the full emotional and financial picture
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She wants the comfort of home and Varya's warmth, but she carries disturbing knowledge about her mother's recklessness that she hasn't yet shared
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Anya's 'I did have an awful time' could be a tentative opening toward revealing the Paris situation, testing whether this is the moment to disclose — rather than pure complaint

beat_states.desire_state: to physically and emotionally reconnect with Anya and reassert her role as caretaker
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to hold the family together and maintain order in the absence of stable authority
beat_states.obstacle: Anya has been away for months and Varya has been carrying the household burden alone; she cannot yet share the full weight of what's been happening

affect_state.rationale: The reunion brings genuine warmth and relief, but underneath the effusive greeting is deep uncertainty about the estate's future and whether anything has been resolved. She overcompensates with affection.

social_state.rationale: Varya positions herself as the older, nurturing sister — almost maternal — but her status is complicated by her ambiguous position as adopted daughter and de facto housekeeper. Warmth is at peak.

known_facts: Anya has returned from Paris with Ranevskaya
The estate is in severe financial danger
She has been managing the household alone
Anya's journey was difficult
hidden_secrets: The depth of her despair about the estate's impending loss
Her private anguish about whether Lopakhin will ever propose
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya knows the practical situation intimately but hasn't yet learned what happened in Paris or whether any financial solution was found. She delays asking.

beat_states.defense_state: channeling anxiety about the estate into maternal affection — displacing worry into tenderness
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: genuinely overjoyed at Anya's return while simultaneously dreading what Anya may or may not have accomplished regarding the family's financial crisis
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Varya's effusiveness could be read as a deliberate performance to delay confronting the financial reality — keeping things light before the inevitable reckoning

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: I went away in Holy Week; it was very cold then. Charlotta talked the whole way and would go on performing her tricks. Why did you tie Charlotta on to me?

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: You couldn’t go alone, darling, at seventeen!

beat_states.desire_state: to unburden herself of the travel's discomforts and subtly reproach Varya for the arrangement
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to be seen and cared for as she transitions from childhood into an uncertain adult world
beat_states.obstacle: Varya's protective authority and the fait accompli of the travel arrangements

affect_state.rationale: Mildly negative — recalling cold, tedious travel. Moderate arousal as she builds to a pointed question. She felt powerless over the arrangements and is still processing the ordeal.

social_state.rationale: She positions herself as the younger, put-upon sister, but the complaint is intimate and familiar — she trusts Varya enough to grumble openly.

known_facts: The journey was cold and long
Charlotta performed tricks the entire way
She was sent with Charlotta as a chaperone
She was seventeen at the time of departure
hidden_secrets: Deeper distress about what she found in Paris — mother's situation, the state of affairs
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya knows the surface facts of the trip but may not yet fully grasp the gravity of the family's financial ruin; she displaces her anxiety onto Charlotta.

beat_states.defense_state: deflection — channeling deeper anxieties about the trip and what she witnessed in Paris onto the minor irritation of Charlotta
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She is relieved to be home yet compelled to revisit the hardships of the journey, suggesting unprocessed distress beneath the complaint
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: The Charlotta complaint could also be a deflection from needing to tell Varya the bad news about their mother and the estate — she stalls by talking about trivialities.

beat_states.desire_state: to justify her protective decision and maintain her role as responsible caretaker
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to hold the family together through maternal self-sacrifice and practical management
beat_states.obstacle: Anya's reproach challenges her authority and competence as surrogate mother

affect_state.rationale: Slightly positive — still in the glow of reunion. High certainty about her reasoning. She feels in control of this particular exchange, though underlying anxiety about the estate persists.

social_state.rationale: She speaks from the elder-sister/surrogate-mother position with affectionate authority. 'Darling' softens the firmness of her justification.

known_facts: Anya was seventeen when she left
A young girl cannot travel alone to Paris
Charlotta was the available chaperone
She has been managing the estate in everyone's absence
hidden_secrets: The depth of her worry about the estate's financial collapse while everyone was away
false_beliefs: That her practical arrangements were adequate — she may not realize how taxing Charlotta was as a companion
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya operates from a strong sense of duty and propriety; she knows the practical constraints but may underestimate the emotional toll on Anya.

beat_states.defense_state: rationalization — framing the decision as the only sensible option given Anya's age
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She wants Anya to feel loved and welcomed home, yet she cannot help asserting her authority even in this tender reunion moment
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Varya's quick justification might also mask guilt — she sent Anya on a difficult errand (retrieving their mother) that perhaps she herself should have undertaken.

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: We went to Paris; it’s cold there and snowing. I talk French perfectly horribly. My mother lives on the fifth floor. I go to her, and find her there with various Frenchmen, women, an old abbé with a book, and everything in tobacco smoke and with no comfort at all. I suddenly became very sorry for mother--so sorry that I took her head in my arms and hugged her and wouldn’t let her go. Then mother started hugging me and crying....

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: [Weeping] Don’t say any more, don’t say any more....

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: She’s already sold her villa near Mentone; she’s nothing left, nothing. And I haven’t a copeck left either; we only just managed to get here. And mother won’t understand! We had dinner at a station; she asked for all the expensive things, and tipped the waiters one rouble each. And Charlotta too. Yasha wants his share too--it’s too bad. Mother’s got a footman now, Yasha; we’ve brought him here.

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: I saw the wretch.

beat_states.desire_state: to unburden herself of the overwhelming truth about their mother's financial and emotional ruin
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to find security and a sense of home after witnessing the collapse of everything she thought was stable
beat_states.obstacle: the pain of the revelations and Varya's distress make it hard to continue, yet she feels compelled to tell the full truth

affect_state.rationale: Anya has seen the worst — the poverty, the denial, the reckless spending — and is emotionally raw from both pity for her mother and fear for the future. Her certainty is high because she has witnessed it firsthand, but control is low because she cannot change any of it.

social_state.rationale: Anya positions herself below Varya as the returning younger sister seeking guidance, but her warmth is complicated by frustration and exhaustion. She trusts Varya enough to confide fully.

known_facts: Lyubov lives in a fifth-floor Paris flat surrounded by hangers-on
The Mentone villa has been sold
They have no money left
Mother continues to spend recklessly — expensive dinners, large tips
Yasha has been brought back as a footman
Charlotta also demands money
hidden_secrets: The depth of her own terror about the family's future
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya is the most informed character in this moment about the true state of affairs abroad. She holds no false beliefs — she has seen it all with clear young eyes.

beat_states.defense_state: channeling overwhelming emotion into pragmatic reportage — listing facts to manage the horror of what she witnessed
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she is desperate to be comforted as a child returning home, yet she has been forced into the adult role of clear-eyed witness to her mother's self-destruction

beat_states.desire_state: to stop the flood of devastating news and protect herself from the full weight of what she already suspects
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to hold the household and family together through order, faith, and self-sacrifice
beat_states.obstacle: the truth Anya is revealing confirms that her years of sacrifice and stewardship may have been futile — she cannot control what happens abroad

affect_state.rationale: Varya is overwhelmed. She has managed the estate under dire conditions and now learns that things abroad are even worse than she feared. Her weeping reflects genuine collapse of composure. Her final line about Yasha ('I saw the wretch') is a brief reassertion of judgment — a small grab for control through contempt.

social_state.rationale: Varya holds slight elder-sister authority but is being subordinated by the information Anya controls. Her warmth toward Anya is high — she is protective — but her contempt toward Yasha reasserts her moral status.

known_facts: The estate is heavily in debt
Mother has been spending recklessly
The Mentone villa is sold
They are essentially penniless
Yasha has arrived with the family
hidden_secrets: Her own fears about losing the estate and her place in the world
Her uncertain relationship with Lopakhin and whether marriage will save her
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya already suspected much of this but now has it confirmed. She has no false beliefs — if anything she is the family realist — but she may underestimate just how impossible the situation is.

beat_states.defense_state: weeping as emotional overflow that also serves to halt Anya's testimony — using distress to signal 'I can't bear more'
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she desperately needs to know the full truth to manage the crisis, yet she begs Anya to stop telling it because the reality is unbearable
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Varya's 'Don't say any more' could also be read as protective of Anya — trying to spare the younger girl from reliving the trauma — rather than purely self-protective.

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: How’s business? Has the interest been paid?

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: Not much chance of that.

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: Oh God, oh God...

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: The place will be sold in August.

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: O God....

beat_states.desire_state: to learn the truth about the estate's financial situation and find reassurance
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve the family home and the sense of belonging and security it represents
beat_states.obstacle: the devastating reality that the estate is beyond saving — the interest is unpaid and the sale is imminent

affect_state.rationale: Anya moves from anxious inquiry to near-despair in seconds. Her repeated 'Oh God' signals rising distress, helplessness, and an inability to mount any practical response. She is fully exposed emotionally.

social_state.rationale: Anya is the younger sister seeking information from the more practically responsible Varya; she is in a dependent, lower-status position here, though their bond remains warm.

known_facts: The interest on the estate's debts has not been paid
The estate will be sold at auction in August
The family's financial situation is dire
hidden_secrets: She may already have sensed the hopelessness during the trip to Paris with her mother's extravagant spending
false_beliefs: Some residual hope that the situation might not be as bad as feared
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya's questions suggest she partially knew the answer but needed confirmation. The Paris trip likely already showed her the family's financial recklessness, but she arrives still clinging to a sliver of hope.

beat_states.defense_state: invocation — calling on God as a reflexive emotional discharge to avoid confronting the full weight of the news
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she asks the question directly, showing she already suspects the worst, yet each answer still hits her as if she hoped for something different
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Anya may be performing distress partly to signal solidarity with Varya's burden, not only reacting to new information

beat_states.desire_state: to convey the brutal truth plainly and share the burden she has been carrying alone
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to hold the household and family together through practical management despite impossible circumstances
beat_states.obstacle: the financial reality is beyond her power to fix; delivering the news also means hurting Anya

affect_state.rationale: Varya is resigned and weary rather than panicked. She has lived with this knowledge for months. Her low arousal and high certainty reflect someone who has already processed the grief and now delivers facts with exhausted clarity.

social_state.rationale: Varya occupies the role of the responsible elder sibling / estate manager. She holds informational authority here but remains emotionally warm toward Anya, even as she delivers devastating news.

known_facts: The interest on the mortgage has not been paid
The estate is scheduled for auction in August
The family has no viable financial plan to prevent the sale
hidden_secrets: The depth of her own despair and exhaustion from managing the estate alone
Her possible resentment toward Ranevskaya's financial irresponsibility
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya is the most informed character about the estate's practical situation. She has no illusions and harbors no false beliefs about the financial reality.

beat_states.defense_state: stoic minimization — she delivers catastrophic news in flat, understated phrases ('Not much chance of that') to maintain composure
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she wants to protect Anya but also needs Anya to understand the severity; delivering the blow is both an act of care and of ruthless honesty
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Varya's bluntness could also be a tactic to mobilize Anya — shocking her into action or into pressuring Ranevskaya

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: [Looks in at the door and moos] Moo!... [Exit.]

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: [Through her tears] I’d like to.... [Shakes her fist.]

beat_states.desire_state: to disrupt Varya's serious emotional moment and get a reaction from her
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to prove his worth and transcend his peasant origins, while maintaining emotional distance from genuine intimacy
beat_states.obstacle: the weight of expectation around his relationship with Varya and the seriousness of the family's financial crisis

affect_state.rationale: He's enjoying the mischief, feeling in control of the interaction. The mooing is deliberately absurd — he chooses the moment of Varya's vulnerability to interject, keeping himself emotionally safe while asserting his presence.

social_state.rationale: The mooing is a deliberate lowering of social register — playing the peasant/animal — but it's also an assertion of power because he controls the tone. Warmth is low because the gesture is dismissive of the emotional gravity in the room.

known_facts: Varya and Anya are having a private emotional conversation
The family is in financial distress
There is an expectation that he and Varya will marry
He has a practical solution for the cherry orchard
hidden_secrets: He cannot bring himself to propose to Varya
His disruption masks his discomfort with emotional closeness
false_beliefs: He may believe his clowning is harmless or even endearing
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin is aware of the dynamics around him but uses strategic obliviousness as a tool. He knows more than he lets on about the estate's future.

beat_states.defense_state: deflection through clowning — uses humor to avoid the vulnerability of genuine engagement with Varya
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he is drawn to Varya and the family yet compulsively sabotages intimacy through mockery
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: The mooing could be a genuinely affectionate, awkward attempt at humor from a man who simply doesn't know how to enter an intimate conversation — bumbling rather than strategic.

beat_states.desire_state: to be taken seriously and to have Lopakhin engage with her genuinely rather than mock her
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to hold the family and estate together through duty, self-sacrifice, and the security that marriage to Lopakhin would provide
beat_states.obstacle: Lopakhin's refusal to engage sincerely and her own inability to express her feelings directly

affect_state.rationale: She is already in tears from the preceding conversation about the family's troubles. Lopakhin's mockery hits precisely where she is most exposed — her hope for a proposal. The tears and the shaking fist coexist, revealing someone overwhelmed and humiliated.

social_state.rationale: She has been made to feel small by Lopakhin's casual cruelty. Her warmth drops as she responds with hostility, but this is reactive — she is the one left exposed while he exits.

known_facts: Lopakhin has not proposed despite everyone's expectation
The estate is in severe financial trouble
She bears the burden of managing the household
Others gossip about her and Lopakhin
hidden_secrets: She deeply wants Lopakhin to propose
She fears she is unlovable or unworthy of genuine affection
false_beliefs: She may still believe a proposal from Lopakhin is possible if circumstances align
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya knows the situation intellectually but clings to hope. Each of Lopakhin's deflections erodes her belief but doesn't fully extinguish it.

beat_states.defense_state: channeling emotional pain into aggression — converting vulnerability into a fist-shake to preserve dignity
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she is deeply hurt by and attracted to the man she threatens; her anger is proportional to her longing
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Varya's fist-shake could be more performative and affectionate than genuinely aggressive — a habitual 'oh you!' response that masks comfort with their dynamic.

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: [Embraces VARYA, softly] Varya, has he proposed to you? [VARYA shakes head] But he loves you.... Why don’t you make up your minds? Why do you keep on waiting?

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: I think that it will all come to nothing. He’s a busy man. I’m not his affair... he pays no attention to me. Bless the man, I don’t want to see him.... But everybody talks about our marriage, everybody congratulates me, and there’s nothing in it at all, it’s all like a dream. [In another tone] You’ve got a brooch like a bee.

utterances.speaker: ANYA
