utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: What are we to do, little mother? It’s the will of God.

utterances.speaker: TROFIMOV
utterances.text: [Softly, through his tears] It’s all right, it’s all right.

beat_states.desire_state: to stop Lubov's emotional breakdown before it overwhelms everyone
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve dignity and the illusion that everything can remain as it was
beat_states.obstacle: Lubov's grief is already erupting and he has no real tools to contain it

affect_state.rationale: Confused and distressed by the sudden emotional intensity; his stage direction 'confused' reveals he is out of his depth, anxious, and unable to manage the situation

social_state.rationale: Attempts to assert elder-brother authority but does so clumsily; there is warmth underneath his awkward restraint

known_facts: Grisha drowned years ago
Trofimov was Grisha's tutor
Trofimov's presence triggers Lubov's grief
hidden_secrets: his own inability to handle deep emotion
false_beliefs: that telling Lubov to stop will actually help
epistemic_state.rationale: He understands the cause of her pain but not how to address it

beat_states.defense_state: intellectualization — reduces the emotional crisis to a matter of 'enough'
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: wants to comfort his sister but his method of shutting down emotion is the opposite of comfort
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: He may also be embarrassed by the public display of grief and trying to preserve family decorum

beat_states.desire_state: to mourn her dead son openly, to be with the memory of Grisha
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to reconnect with the lost world of love and innocence that the cherry orchard represents — Grisha is the most painful embodiment of that loss
beat_states.obstacle: the irreversibility of death; there is no resolution possible, only grief

affect_state.rationale: Completely broken open by grief; the repetition of 'Grisha' three times shows she is beyond social composure, fully surrendered to anguish. High certainty because the loss is absolute and undeniable.

social_state.rationale: Normally the matriarch, she is now utterly exposed and dependent on others' care; the warmth is directed inward toward the memory of her son

known_facts: Grisha drowned at age seven
Trofimov was his tutor
seeing Trofimov brings Grisha's death flooding back
hidden_secrets: she blames herself for Grisha's death
she fled Russia partly to escape this grief
epistemic_state.rationale: This is raw, unmediated truth — she knows exactly what she has lost and why she is in pain

beat_states.defense_state: none — defenses have collapsed; she is in unmediated grief
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she has returned 'home' seeking comfort but the home is the very place that holds her deepest wound

beat_states.desire_state: to offer comfort and be forgiven for triggering this grief by his very presence
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to be accepted as someone who matters, who has grown beyond being 'just the tutor' — to connect meaningfully with this family
beat_states.obstacle: his presence is the trigger; the more he tries to help, the more he reminds Lubov of Grisha's death

affect_state.rationale: Crying himself, feeling guilt and sorrow; his soft, tearful consolation reveals he is deeply affected but trying to hold steady for Lubov's sake

social_state.rationale: Low status — young, poor, the former tutor who has just been reproached by Varya — but genuinely warm and empathetic in his attempt to comfort

known_facts: Grisha drowned while in his care or nearby
his appearance reminds Lubov of Grisha
Varya warned him to wait
hidden_secrets: possible survivor's guilt about Grisha's death
false_beliefs: that saying 'it's all right' can meaningfully help
epistemic_state.rationale: He understands the dynamics at play but underestimates the depth of the wound his presence reopens

beat_states.defense_state: minimization — 'it's all right' attempts to contain an uncontainable grief
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he weeps through his own grief while trying to reassure others that everything is fine
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: His tears may also stem from his own unresolved grief over Grisha, not just empathy for Lubov

beat_states.desire_state: to protect her mother from this pain, and to manage the situation she warned against
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to hold the family together through practical devotion and self-sacrifice
beat_states.obstacle: she cannot undo Trofimov's appearance or erase Lubov's grief; she is powerless despite having foreseen this

affect_state.rationale: Weeping openly, overwhelmed by her mother's pain and her own helplessness; the reproach to Trofimov is a desperate attempt to regain some control

social_state.rationale: Subordinate but deeply caring; addresses Lubov as 'little mother' with tenderness while scolding Trofimov from a position of domestic authority

known_facts: Trofimov's presence would trigger Lubov
she had asked Trofimov to wait until tomorrow
Grisha's death is the source of this grief
hidden_secrets: her own deep grief over the half-brother she lost
epistemic_state.rationale: She correctly predicted this outcome and is frustrated that her warning was ignored

beat_states.defense_state: displacement — channels her own grief into blame toward Trofimov and appeal to God's will
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: wants to be the strong caretaker but is weeping and just as overwhelmed as her mother

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: [Still weeping] My boy’s dead; he was drowned. Why? Why, my friend? [Softly] Anya’s asleep in there. I am speaking so loudly, making such a noise.... Well, Peter? What’s made you look so bad? Why have you grown so old?

utterances.speaker: TROFIMOV
utterances.text: In the train an old woman called me a decayed gentleman.

beat_states.desire_state: to process her grief over Grisha's death while reaching out to Trofimov as a living connection to her lost son
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to recover what has been lost — her home, her innocence, her family wholeness
beat_states.obstacle: the overwhelming grief that Trofimov's presence triggers, reminding her of Grisha's drowning

affect_state.rationale: Deep grief is erupting — the existential 'why' of her son's death — but she self-corrects, noting Anya is sleeping, and redirects to Trofimov with a softer, almost maternal curiosity about his aging, showing her attempt to regain composure

social_state.rationale: She maintains the role of the elder, the mistress of the house, even in grief; her warmth toward Trofimov is genuine — he was Grisha's tutor and thus a surrogate connection to her dead child

known_facts: Grisha drowned as a child
Trofimov was Grisha's tutor
Trofimov has visibly aged and looks worn
Anya is asleep nearby
hidden_secrets: the full weight of guilt she carries about Grisha's death and her subsequent flight to Paris
epistemic_state.rationale: She is fully aware of the facts but cannot reconcile the senselessness of her son's death; her question 'why?' is existential, not informational

beat_states.defense_state: deflecting from the depth of her pain by shifting attention to Trofimov's appearance
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she is simultaneously devastated by Grisha's memory and trying to be warm and maternal toward Trofimov; she weeps but then pivots to gentle teasing
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: She may be unconsciously testing whether Trofimov can handle her grief — whether he is strong enough to be a surrogate for the lost son

beat_states.desire_state: to ease Lubov's pain and deflect the emotional intensity of the moment with self-deprecating humor
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to be taken seriously as a thinker and agent of change, not merely pitied as a shabby eternal student
beat_states.obstacle: Lubov's grief makes a serious philosophical response inappropriate; his own shabbiness and aging undercut his dignity

affect_state.rationale: He is touched by Lubov's grief and her noticing his decline, but covers his discomfort and perhaps his own sadness about his appearance and station with wry humor

social_state.rationale: He is lower status — a perpetual student, visibly aged and shabby — but warm toward Lubov; the self-deprecating joke is an offering of connection through lightness

known_facts: He was Grisha's tutor
Grisha drowned
He has aged and looks worn
An old woman on the train called him a 'decayed gentleman'
hidden_secrets: his possible guilt or complicated feelings about having been present in the household when Grisha died
epistemic_state.rationale: He knows the social reality of his decline and is aware of how others perceive him; his anecdote is evidence of this self-awareness

beat_states.defense_state: self-deprecation as armor — using humor to avoid the raw emotional weight of the moment
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he wants to be seen as a serious person with ideas, but responds to a deeply emotional moment with a self-mocking anecdote, undermining his own gravitas
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: The anecdote may be a subtle way of signaling to Lubov that he too has suffered — that the years since Grisha's death have marked him as well

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: You were quite a boy then, a nice little student, and now your hair is not at all thick and you wear spectacles. Are you really still a student? [Goes to the door.]

utterances.speaker: TROFIMOV
utterances.text: I suppose I shall always be a student.

beat_states.desire_state: to regain composure by redirecting emotional energy away from grief toward mundane observation
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to recapture the safety and warmth of the past while avoiding the pain it carries
beat_states.obstacle: the overwhelming grief triggered by Trofimov's presence, which reminds her of her drowned son

affect_state.rationale: Still raw from weeping but actively forcing herself toward lighter ground; the teasing tone is fragile, a thin veneer over deep pain. She's regaining some control but remains highly vulnerable.

social_state.rationale: She reasserts her social position as the older, maternal figure by teasing Trofimov affectionately, but the warmth is genuine — he is linked to her son and her past.

known_facts: Trofimov was her son Grisha's tutor
Grisha drowned years ago
Trofimov has aged and changed physically
Trofimov is still apparently a student
hidden_secrets: The depth of guilt she may carry about Grisha's death
Her fear that she cannot withstand the emotional weight of being home
epistemic_state.rationale: She perceives Trofimov accurately in the present but uses surface observations to avoid deeper emotional engagement.

beat_states.defense_state: displacement — channeling intense grief into light, teasing observation about Trofimov's appearance
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she has just been weeping over her dead child yet pivots to gently mocking Trofimov's aging appearance, revealing the desperate need to escape unbearable feeling
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: The teasing could also be a subtle attempt to diminish Trofimov's significance so his presence hurts less — a form of minimization rather than pure deflection.

beat_states.desire_state: to endure this awkward moment with dignity while acknowledging Lubov's pain without worsening it
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to be recognized as someone of intellectual and moral worth beyond his humble circumstances
beat_states.obstacle: Lubov's teasing subtly undermines him by highlighting his lack of worldly progress, and his connection to her grief makes him an emotionally loaded figure

affect_state.rationale: Slightly wounded by her observation but maintaining composure. His self-deprecating reply masks a resigned sadness about his own stalled life. Low arousal — he's absorbing rather than reacting.

social_state.rationale: He accepts a lower status position — the perpetual student, the tutor, the young man being assessed by a grande dame — but does so with gentle warmth and implicit solidarity with her pain.

known_facts: He was Grisha's tutor
His presence caused Lubov to weep
He has not completed his studies
He has aged noticeably
hidden_secrets: His idealistic vision for the future that he has not yet expressed
Possible guilt or complicated feelings about his connection to the dead boy
epistemic_state.rationale: He is self-aware about his situation and perceptive about the emotional dynamics, though he may not fully grasp how deeply his presence disturbs Lubov.

beat_states.defense_state: self-deprecation — preemptively accepting the diminishment to neutralize it
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he likely feels both guilt for triggering her grief and a quiet sting at being reduced to his thinning hair and perpetual studenthood, yet he absorbs it gracefully
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: His reply 'I shall always be a student' could carry a note of philosophical pride — the eternal student as idealist — rather than pure self-deprecation.

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: [Kisses her brother, then VARYA] Well, let’s go to bed.... And you’ve grown older, Leonid.

utterances.speaker: PISCHIN
utterances.text: [Follows her] Yes, we’ve got to go to bed.... Oh, my gout! I’ll stay the night here. If only, Lubov Andreyevna, my dear, you could get me 240 roubles to-morrow morning--

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: Still the same story.

utterances.speaker: PISCHIN
utterances.text: Two hundred and forty roubles... to pay the interest on the mortgage.

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: I haven’t any money, dear man.

utterances.speaker: PISCHIN
utterances.text: I’ll give it back... it’s a small sum....

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: Well, then, Leonid will give it to you.... Let him have it, Leonid.

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: By all means; hold out your hand.

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: Why not? He wants it; he’ll give it back. [LUBOV ANDREYEVNA, TROFIMOV, PISCHIN, and FIERS go out. GAEV, VARYA, and YASHA remain.]

beat_states.desire_state: to maintain his dignity and express irritation at Pischin's predictable request without causing a scene
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve the family's honor and the appearance of gentility despite decline
beat_states.obstacle: Lubov has just publicly volunteered him to give money he likely doesn't have

affect_state.rationale: irritated and cornered — his sarcasm is a defense against the humiliation of being unable to provide, and against his sister's casual assumption that he has resources

social_state.rationale: he tries to maintain status through sardonic commentary but his warmth drops as he is thrust into the role of reluctant banker for a family and circle that is collectively bankrupt

known_facts: Pischin always asks for money
the family finances are dire
Lubov has just delegated the problem to him
hidden_secrets: his own financial helplessness regarding the estate
false_beliefs: that his various schemes (the aunt, the bank) might save the cherry orchard
epistemic_state.rationale: Gaev sees Pischin's request clearly for what it is but cannot see his own financial behavior as equally deluded

beat_states.defense_state: sarcasm — 'by all means; hold out your hand' is bitter irony masking his inability to help and his resentment at being put on the spot
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he performs weary superiority over Pischin ('still the same story') while being in an equally dire or worse financial position himself
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Gaev's 'by all means; hold out your hand' could be read as genuine resigned generosity rather than sarcasm, but his earlier 'still the same story' strongly suggests irritation

beat_states.desire_state: to close the evening with warmth and generosity, deflecting from her own pain by attending to others
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to recapture the feeling of love, home, and belonging — even at the cost of self-destruction
beat_states.obstacle: she has no money and cannot fulfill Pischin's request, yet her instinct is always to give

affect_state.rationale: emotionally exhausted from earlier weeping, she now shifts into a warm, tired, slightly reckless generosity — her 'why not?' reveals a fatalistic indifference to financial reality

social_state.rationale: she performs the hostess/matriarch role with genuine warmth, kissing her brother and Varya, treating Pischin with affection even as she can't help him — her status is inherited grace rather than actual power

known_facts: she has no money
the estate is threatened
Pischin always borrows money
hidden_secrets: the depth of her financial ruin
her continued sending of money to her lover in Paris
false_beliefs: that Leonid can or will provide the money
that generosity will somehow sustain the household
epistemic_state.rationale: Lubov knows rationally she is broke but behaves as though money will appear, a deep denial that structures her entire relationship to the estate

beat_states.defense_state: generosity as avoidance — she redirects the financial burden to Gaev rather than confronting her own inability
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she is penniless and facing the loss of her estate yet casually promises money she doesn't have, performing the role of the gracious benefactress she can no longer afford to be
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: she may be subtly testing Gaev — forcing him into an uncomfortable promise to expose his own impotence

beat_states.desire_state: to secure 240 roubles to pay his mortgage interest by morning
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to survive financially day by day, always believing some miracle will save him
beat_states.obstacle: Lubov has no money and Gaev's promise is clearly sarcastic/hollow

affect_state.rationale: anxious about his debt but masking it with practiced bonhomie; his persistence despite rebuff reveals underlying desperation

social_state.rationale: he occupies the lowest social position in this exchange — the perpetual borrower, slightly comic, but his warmth is genuine and disarming

known_facts: he needs 240 roubles for mortgage interest
Lubov is also in financial trouble
he has borrowed from these people before
hidden_secrets: the full extent of his debts
false_beliefs: that something will always turn up
that this loan will solve his immediate problem
epistemic_state.rationale: Pischin is a comic mirror of the family's own denial — he lives in perpetual financial crisis but maintains an unshakeable faith that things will work out

beat_states.defense_state: minimization — he frames the sum as 'small' and promises repayment to make the request seem trivial
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he is desperate yet performs casual optimism, as though borrowing to pay mortgage interest is a minor social transaction rather than a sign of ruin

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: My sister hasn’t lost the habit of throwing money about. [To YASHA] Stand off, do; you smell of poultry.

utterances.speaker: YASHA
utterances.text: [Grins] You are just the same as ever, Leonid Andreyevitch.

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: Really? [To VARYA] What’s he saying?

beat_states.desire_state: to reassert aristocratic authority and social order in the household after the emotional upheaval of Lubov's return
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve the dignity and status of the family estate and his own identity as a gentleman
beat_states.obstacle: Yasha's insolent familiarity and the general erosion of class boundaries that signals the family's decline

affect_state.rationale: Irritated and slightly anxious — the emotional intensity of the homecoming has left him needing to reassert control through petty authority. His criticism of Lubov is a deflection from his own helplessness.

social_state.rationale: Pulling rank on Yasha with the dismissive 'Stand off' command, performing the role of master. Cold and condescending, using class disdain to establish distance.

known_facts: Lubov has been spending extravagantly abroad
Yasha is a servant who has returned from Paris with airs
The estate is in financial trouble
hidden_secrets: His own ineffectiveness in managing the estate's finances
His deep anxiety about the family's decline
false_beliefs: That asserting class distinction still carries real authority
That he understands the financial situation better than Lubov
epistemic_state.rationale: Gaev clings to old hierarchies as a way of maintaining the illusion of control. His 'What's he saying?' to Varya about Yasha's comment reveals he is genuinely unsettled by the servant's familiarity but tries to dismiss it.

beat_states.defense_state: displacement — redirecting emotional discomfort from Lubov's grief and financial recklessness onto a servant he can control
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: criticizes Lubov for throwing money about while he himself is financially impotent and has done nothing to save the estate
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Gaev's comment about Lubov could also be a genuine expression of concern masked as criticism — worry about the estate's finances coming out as snobbish judgment.

beat_states.desire_state: to assert his own sophistication and equality, refusing to be diminished by Gaev's aristocratic pretensions
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to escape the provincial servitude of his origins and live as a worldly, cosmopolitan man
beat_states.obstacle: Gaev's attempt to put him in his place as a servant; the social structure that technically still binds him

affect_state.rationale: Amused and self-assured. The grin indicates he finds Gaev's bluster more entertaining than threatening. He feels secure in his Parisian worldliness and is not genuinely wounded by the insult.

social_state.rationale: Technically low status as a servant but actively subverting it through his knowing tone. His 'You are just the same as ever' is warmly phrased but carries contempt — implying Gaev is unchanged, i.e., stuck and irrelevant.

known_facts: Gaev has always been this way — pompous and ineffectual
The family is in decline
He has seen Paris and broader life beyond this estate
hidden_secrets: His contempt for the family he serves
His desire to never return to provincial Russian life permanently
false_beliefs: That his Parisian veneer makes him genuinely superior to these people
epistemic_state.rationale: Yasha reads Gaev accurately — he knows the old man is performing authority he no longer truly possesses. His comment is calculated to land as both flattery and insult.

beat_states.defense_state: ironic deflection — using a grin and a backhanded compliment to neutralize Gaev's authority without direct confrontation
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: presents himself as above provincial life but remains a servant dependent on the very family he subtly disrespects
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Yasha's grin and comment could be genuinely affectionate rather than mocking — a servant who finds his old master endearingly familiar — but the stage direction 'grins' and the context suggest sly insolence.

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: [To YASHA] Your mother’s come from the village; she’s been sitting in the servants’ room since yesterday, and wants to see you....

utterances.speaker: YASHA
utterances.text: Bless the woman!

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: Shameless man.

utterances.speaker: YASHA
utterances.text: A lot of use there is in her coming. She might have come tomorrow just as well. [Exit.]

beat_states.desire_state: to shame Yasha into behaving with filial duty and basic decency
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to maintain moral order and responsibility in a household that is falling apart
beat_states.obstacle: Yasha's complete indifference to shame and his inflated sense of self-importance

affect_state.rationale: Varya is morally certain Yasha is wrong but frustrated by her inability to make him care. Her disgust is genuine but also masks her helplessness in a household where no one respects proper behavior.

social_state.rationale: As adopted daughter and de facto housekeeper, she holds authority over a servant like Yasha, but her warmth is entirely withdrawn — she regards him with open contempt.

known_facts: Yasha's mother has traveled from the village to see him
The mother has been waiting in the servants' room since yesterday
Yasha has not bothered to visit her
hidden_secrets: Her own deep anxiety about her future and whether she truly belongs in this family
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya knows the concrete facts of the mother's visit and uses them as moral ammunition. She has clear-eyed understanding of Yasha's character.

beat_states.defense_state: moral superiority — positioning herself as the voice of propriety
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She enforces duty on others but may feel her own devotion to the household goes unrecognized and unrewarded

beat_states.desire_state: to dismiss his mother's visit and any obligation it implies, maintaining his cultivated persona as above his origins
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to escape his peasant roots entirely and live as a worldly, sophisticated man attached to the gentry
beat_states.obstacle: Varya's moral pressure and the embarrassing reminder of his village origins

affect_state.rationale: Yasha is mildly irritated rather than deeply moved. He maintains emotional control through performative indifference. The low vulnerability reflects his successful suppression of any filial feeling, though his quick exit suggests slight discomfort under Varya's gaze.

social_state.rationale: Though a servant, Yasha performs status above his station through his Paris-acquired airs. His warmth is deeply negative — cold toward his own mother and hostile toward Varya's moralizing.

known_facts: His mother has come from the village
She has been waiting since yesterday
Varya and others judge him for not seeing her
hidden_secrets: His deep shame about his peasant origins which he masks as indifference
His calculations about attaching himself to Lubov's fortunes
false_beliefs: That his Paris veneer genuinely elevates him above his background
epistemic_state.rationale: Yasha knows the situation perfectly well — he has simply chosen not to act on it. His false belief in his own sophistication is the engine of his cruelty.

beat_states.defense_state: contemptuous dismissal — treating his mother as an inconvenience to neutralize any guilt
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He depends entirely on the patronage of a declining aristocratic family while affecting superiority over his own mother and class
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: A small possibility that Yasha's dismissiveness conceals genuine pain about his mother that he cannot afford to feel, but the text gives minimal support for this reading.

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: Mother hasn’t altered a scrap, she’s just as she always was. She’d give away everything, if the idea only entered her head.

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: Yes.... [Pause] If there’s any illness for which people offer many remedies, you may be sure that particular illness is incurable, I think. I work my brains to their hardest. I’ve several remedies, very many, and that really means I’ve none at all. It would be nice to inherit a fortune from somebody, it would be nice to marry our Anya to a rich man, it would be nice to go to Yaroslav and try my luck with my aunt the Countess. My aunt is very, very rich.

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: [Weeps] If only God helped us.

beat_states.desire_state: to convince himself and Varya that he is actively working on saving the estate
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve his dignity and the family's legacy without having to fundamentally change
beat_states.obstacle: he has no viable plan and knows it — each scheme is more fantastical than the last

affect_state.rationale: Gaev's rambling reveals deep anxiety masked by verbose philosophizing. The pause before his speech and his own admission that 'many remedies means none' betrays his awareness of futility. Low certainty and control reflect genuine helplessness beneath performed confidence.

social_state.rationale: He positions himself as the family patriarch thinking through solutions, claiming authority, but his schemes (inheritance, rich marriage, the aunt) reveal impotence. Some warmth toward Varya in sharing his thoughts, but fundamentally self-focused.

known_facts: The estate is in severe financial danger
Lubov spends money recklessly
His aunt the Countess is very rich
None of his plans are reliable
hidden_secrets: He suspects all his remedies are fantasies
He has likely already squandered resources himself
false_beliefs: That brainstorming fantastical solutions constitutes meaningful action
That the aunt might actually help despite their strained relationship
epistemic_state.rationale: Gaev's self-aware aphorism about incurable illnesses reveals he knows the truth but cannot face it directly. His plans are wishes dressed as strategies.

beat_states.defense_state: intellectualization — reframing helplessness as philosophical reflection and strategic brainstorming
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: presents himself as someone working his brains to the hardest while simultaneously confessing he has no real solution
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Gaev may be deliberately performing helplessness to lower expectations so that failure won't be blamed on him

beat_states.desire_state: to find any source of hope that the estate and family can be saved
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to hold the family together and maintain order in a disintegrating world
beat_states.obstacle: Lubov's recklessness, Gaev's impotence, and the total absence of a realistic plan

affect_state.rationale: Varya weeps openly — she has reached emotional breaking point. Her appeal to God signals she sees no earthly solution. Extremely low certainty and control; high vulnerability as she drops her practical, managing persona.

social_state.rationale: Varya is subordinate here — she opened with complaint about Lubov but is now reduced to tears, powerless. Warmth persists through genuine familial concern and shared suffering with Gaev.

known_facts: Lubov has not changed and will continue giving money away
Gaev has no real plan to save the estate
The financial situation is dire
hidden_secrets: She may fear she will lose her home and identity if the estate is sold
Her own marriage prospects (Lopakhin) remain unresolved and a source of private anxiety
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya sees the situation clearly — perhaps more clearly than anyone. She has no false beliefs, which is precisely what makes her despair so acute.

beat_states.defense_state: displacement into prayer — turning to God when human agency has failed
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she criticizes her mother's recklessness but her own appeal to God is equally passive and ineffectual
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Varya's weeping could be a tactic to shame Gaev into more decisive action rather than pure despair

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: Don’t cry. My aunt’s very rich, but she doesn’t like us. My sister, in the first place, married an advocate, not a noble.... [ANYA appears in the doorway] She not only married a man who was not a noble, but she behaved herself in a way which cannot be described as proper. She’s nice and kind and charming, and I’m very fond of her, but say what you will in her favour and you still have to admit that she’s wicked; you can feel it in her slightest movements.

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: [Whispers] Anya’s in the doorway.

beat_states.desire_state: to explain and justify the family's financial predicament by blaming Ranevskaya's choices, establishing moral authority
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve his sense of aristocratic dignity and family honor in the face of decline
beat_states.obstacle: his genuine affection for his sister contradicts his need to criticize her; Anya's unexpected presence threatens to expose his gossip

affect_state.rationale: He feels entitled and certain in his moral assessment, somewhat animated by the pleasure of holding forth, but underlying anxiety about the family's situation and unaware he's being overheard

social_state.rationale: He positions himself as the morally superior judge of his sister's behavior, claiming patriarchal authority, but his warmth is diminished by his willingness to gossip about family behind their backs

known_facts: Their aunt is rich but disapproves of the family
Ranevskaya married a non-noble advocate
Ranevskaya has behaved improperly
The family is in financial trouble
hidden_secrets: He is deeply dependent on the very sister he criticizes
His own financial irresponsibility has contributed to the estate's decline
false_beliefs: He believes he is speaking privately and unobserved
epistemic_state.rationale: Gaev is factually informed about family history but blind to his own hypocrisy and unaware Anya is listening

beat_states.defense_state: intellectualization — framing his sister's moral failings as objective analysis rather than personal judgment
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he professes deep fondness for his sister while simultaneously condemning her character as 'wicked,' revealing both love and resentment
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Gaev may be rehearsing arguments to use with the aunt, testing how to frame Ranevskaya's story to potentially win the aunt's financial help

beat_states.desire_state: to immediately stop Gaev from saying hurtful things about their mother in front of Anya
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to protect the family from harm and hold things together as the responsible caretaker

affect_state.rationale: Sharp anxiety and alarm at seeing Anya in the doorway — she knows the damage Gaev's words could do; her whisper indicates urgency and fear of the moment, low control because she can't unsay what's been said

social_state.rationale: She occupies a lower social position as adopted daughter and housekeeper, but her warmth is high — her protective instinct toward Anya drives this intervention

known_facts: Anya has appeared in the doorway and may have heard Gaev's speech
Gaev is speaking critically about Ranevskaya
Anya loves her mother deeply
hidden_secrets: She likely shares some of Gaev's frustrations about Ranevskaya's spending and behavior
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya is the most perceptive person in the room — she notices Anya before Gaev does and instantly understands the social danger

beat_states.defense_state: vigilant caretaking — constantly scanning for threats to family harmony
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she may privately agree with some of Gaev's criticisms of Ranevskaya but must suppress this to protect Anya

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: Really? [Pause] It’s curious, something’s got into my right eye... I can’t see properly out of it. And on Thursday, when I was at the District Court...

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: Why aren’t you in bed, Anya?

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: Can’t sleep. It’s no good.

beat_states.desire_state: to be allowed to remain present and process the emotional weight of what she's heard and experienced
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to find security and hope for the family's future and her own
beat_states.obstacle: Varya's insistence on sending her to bed; her own inner turmoil preventing rest

affect_state.rationale: Her simple, honest admission 'Can't sleep. It's no good' reveals high vulnerability and low control. She is openly helpless, not performing or deflecting. Uncertainty about the future keeps her aroused despite fatigue.

social_state.rationale: In the position of the youngest, accepting Varya's authority but gently resisting. Her honesty carries warmth and openness.

known_facts: The estate may be sold
Her mother has been living beyond her means
The aunt does not approve of the family
She has just returned from a difficult journey
hidden_secrets: The full depth of her fear about what will become of them all
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya has absorbed much painful information during her trip and this evening. Her inability to sleep reflects the weight of this knowledge.

beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She is exhausted from travel yet too emotionally activated to sleep — her body needs rest but her mind cannot release the anxiety

beat_states.desire_state: to deflect from the painful reality of the family's financial situation and his sister's moral history
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve his sense of dignity and the family's honor without confronting hard truths
beat_states.obstacle: the emotional weight of what he just revealed about Lyubov's past and the family's precarious position

affect_state.rationale: The 'Really?' and pause suggest he's absorbing something uncomfortable. The pivot to his eye trouble is a classic avoidance of emotional discomfort, revealing low control and moderate vulnerability beneath the deflection.

social_state.rationale: Still performing the role of family patriarch and authority figure by referencing the District Court, but his warmth is muted as he retreats into self-focus.

known_facts: The aunt disapproves of Lyubov's marriage and lifestyle
The family estate is in financial danger
He was at the District Court on Thursday
hidden_secrets: He likely knows the financial situation is more dire than he lets on
false_beliefs: That rambling about trivial matters is an adequate response to crisis
epistemic_state.rationale: Gaev possesses real knowledge of the family's predicament but characteristically avoids engaging with it directly.

beat_states.defense_state: somatization — redirecting emotional pain into a physical complaint about his eye
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: has just delivered a devastating assessment of the family's situation but immediately retreats into trivial bodily complaints and anecdote, unable to sustain the gravity of his own words
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: The eye complaint could be genuine physical discomfort rather than purely psychosomatic deflection, but the timing after painful revelations strongly suggests avoidance.

beat_states.desire_state: to impose order and care on the household by getting Anya to bed
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to maintain control and hold the family together as its practical caretaker
beat_states.obstacle: Anya's restlessness and the emotional turbulence that pervades the household

affect_state.rationale: She seizes on something she can control — getting Anya to bed — amid a conversation she cannot control. Moderate arousal from maternal vigilance, slight negative valence from worry.

social_state.rationale: Exercising her quasi-maternal authority over Anya with genuine concern, maintaining her role as the responsible elder sister figure.

known_facts: It is very late
Anya has had an exhausting journey
The family's financial situation is desperate
Gaev is rambling to avoid the subject
hidden_secrets: Her own sleeplessness and anxiety about losing the estate
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya is pragmatically aware of what's happening but chooses to focus on the immediate and manageable rather than the overwhelming.

beat_states.defense_state: displacement into caretaking — channeling her anxiety about the family crisis into managing others' physical well-being
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She herself is likely unable to sleep given the weight of the estate's problems, yet she insists Anya should be in bed
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Varya may also be cutting off Gaev's rambling deliberately, using Anya as a pretext to change the subject.

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: My darling! [Kisses ANYA’S face and hands] My child.... [Crying] You’re not my niece, you’re my angel, you’re my all.... Believe in me, believe...

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: I do believe in you, uncle. Everybody loves you and respects you... but, uncle dear, you ought to say nothing, no more than that. What were you saying just now about my mother, your own sister? Why did you say those things?

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: Yes, yes. [Covers his face with her hand] Yes, really, it was awful. Save me, my God! And only just now I made a speech before a bookcase... it’s so silly! And only when I’d finished I knew how silly it was.

beat_states.desire_state: to lovingly but firmly correct her uncle and protect her mother from his careless words
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to hold her family together and preserve the possibility of a hopeful future
beat_states.obstacle: her genuine love and respect for Gaev makes it difficult to confront him, and his emotional outpouring pressures her to reassure rather than hold firm

affect_state.rationale: Anya is relatively composed compared to Gaev. She is tender but firm, acting from a position of quiet moral authority. There's underlying anxiety about the family but she manages it through directness.

social_state.rationale: Despite being the younger family member, Anya momentarily assumes a parental role — gently admonishing Gaev. Her warmth is genuine but structured around a corrective purpose.

known_facts: Gaev said something hurtful about her mother
Gaev is loved and respected by those around him
Gaev has a habit of saying foolish things
The family needs its members to behave with more discipline
hidden_secrets: She may harbor deeper doubts about whether Gaev can actually help save the estate
false_beliefs: That a gentle reprimand will be enough to change Gaev's behavior
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya perceives Gaev's weakness clearly but still extends the benefit of the doubt, partly out of love and partly out of youthful hope.

beat_states.defense_state: cushioning criticism with affirmation — she leads with love before delivering her rebuke
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she says she believes in him while simultaneously exposing the very reason she shouldn't — his inability to control what he says
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Anya's 'I do believe in you' could be read as entirely performative — saying what Gaev needs to hear so she can deliver her real message about his behavior

beat_states.desire_state: to be absolved of his verbal indiscretions and reassured that he is still loved despite his failings
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to maintain his sense of dignity and family standing while avoiding confrontation with his own ineffectuality
beat_states.obstacle: his compulsive need to talk leads him to say inappropriate things, and Anya has just called him out on his cruel remarks about her mother

affect_state.rationale: Gaev is emotionally unraveled — crying, kissing Anya, begging for belief. His self-awareness about the bookcase speech and his remarks about his sister floods him with shame. He feels profoundly out of control of his own words and behavior.

social_state.rationale: He has lowered himself before his young niece, seeking her validation. His warmth is genuine but also desperate — he needs her to shore him up emotionally.

known_facts: He said cruel things about his own sister (Anya's mother)
He made a foolish speech to a bookcase
He recognizes his own tendency toward verbal excess
The family is in financial crisis
hidden_secrets: He knows deep down he is incapable of saving the estate despite his plans and speeches
false_beliefs: That moments of self-awareness and contrition will translate into actual behavioral change
epistemic_state.rationale: Gaev has a flickering but accurate self-knowledge — he sees his foolishness clearly in retrospect — but he chronically overestimates his capacity to reform.

beat_states.defense_state: self-deprecation as preemptive disarming — by calling himself silly first, he prevents others from judging him more harshly
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he overflows with performative love and sincerity while simultaneously revealing that he cannot control his own speech or behavior
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Gaev's emotional display could be read as partly manipulative — using tears and affection to deflect Anya's criticism rather than genuinely reckoning with it

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: Yes, uncle dear, you really ought to say less. Keep quiet, that’s all.

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: You’d be so much happier in yourself if you only kept quiet.

beat_states.desire_state: To gently reinforce Varya's admonishment and protect her uncle from himself
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To find security, love, and a sense of home — which requires the family to stop self-destructing
beat_states.obstacle: Her deep affection for Gaev makes it painful to correct him; she is young and has limited authority

affect_state.rationale: Tender and slightly sad; low arousal suggests weariness rather than anger; she feels vulnerable because she needs Gaev to be the adult but knows he cannot be

social_state.rationale: She speaks from a nominally lower status as niece but with high warmth, framing the correction as being for his own happiness

known_facts: Gaev's talk embarrasses the family
Silence would serve him better than his grandiose promises
The family financial situation is dire
hidden_secrets: She may have already lost faith that the cherry orchard can be saved
false_beliefs: That simply keeping quiet could make Gaev happier — it likely wouldn't change his fundamental nature
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya echoes Varya's practical wisdom but adds a naive belief that silence alone could bring Gaev peace

beat_states.defense_state: Reframing criticism as care — 'you'd be happier' — to avoid the discomfort of directly shaming her uncle
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She has just been comforted by Gaev and now must turn around and parent him, reversing the generational dynamic
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Anya may also be speaking to manage her own anxiety — if Gaev stops talking, she doesn't have to confront the emptiness of his promises

beat_states.desire_state: To stop Gaev from making grandiose, empty speeches that embarrass the family and raise false hopes
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To hold the family together through practical management and prevent their ruin
beat_states.obstacle: Gaev's compulsive need to speechify and his inability to recognize his own foolishness

affect_state.rationale: Weary frustration mixed with affection; she is certain Gaev's talk is counterproductive but feels limited in her actual power to change him

social_state.rationale: She takes a slightly elevated position as the sensible one correcting an elder, but softens it with 'uncle dear' to maintain warmth and respect

known_facts: Gaev's speeches are empty and lead nowhere
The family's situation requires action not words
Gaev has just been making promises about saving the estate that he cannot keep
hidden_secrets: She may secretly despair that no one in the family is capable of saving the cherry orchard
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya has a clear-eyed understanding of Gaev's character and the danger of his verbal indulgences

beat_states.defense_state: Assuming the role of practical authority figure to manage her anxiety about the family's helplessness
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She speaks with affection ('uncle dear') while essentially telling him he's harmful when he opens his mouth

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: All right, I’ll be quiet. [Kisses their hands] I’ll be quiet. But let’s talk business. On Thursday I was in the District Court, and a lot of us met there together, and we began to talk of this, that, and the other, and now I think I can arrange a loan to pay the interest into the bank.

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: If only God would help us!

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: I’ll go on Tuesday. I’ll talk with them about it again. [To VARYA] Don’t howl. [To ANYA] Your mother will have a talk to Lopakhin; he, of course, won’t refuse... And when you’ve rested you’ll go to Yaroslav to the Countess, your grandmother. So you see, we’ll have three irons in the fire, and we’ll be safe. We’ll pay up the interest. I’m certain. [Puts some sugar-candy into his mouth] I swear on my honour, on anything you will, that the estate will not be sold! [Excitedly] I swear on my happiness! Here’s my hand. You may call me a dishonourable wretch if I let it go to auction! I swear by all I am!

beat_states.desire_state: to reassure Anya and Varya (and himself) that he has a viable plan to save the estate
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve the family's dignity, legacy, and the cherry orchard without confronting the painful reality of his own ineffectiveness
beat_states.obstacle: he has no concrete, reliable plan — only vague conversations and wishful thinking; his nieces have already told him to stop talking

affect_state.rationale: He works himself into genuine excitement and hopefulness, but the escalation from calm promise to frantic oath-swearing reveals high arousal masking deep anxiety. He feels momentarily buoyed by his own rhetoric but has little actual control over the situation.

social_state.rationale: He is trying to reclaim patriarchal authority as the family problem-solver after being chastised by Varya. His warmth is genuine — he loves these young women and wants to protect them — but his status is compromised by the earlier rebuke to be quiet.

known_facts: The estate will be auctioned if the interest is not paid
He spoke with acquaintances at the District Court about a possible loan
Lopakhin is a potential source of help
Anya's grandmother the Countess in Yaroslavl is another potential source
hidden_secrets: He has no firm commitment from anyone for a loan
He likely knows his plans are far less solid than he presents them
false_beliefs: That having 'three irons in the fire' constitutes a reliable rescue strategy
That the Countess will necessarily help given the family's estrangement
That talking to people at the District Court again will yield a concrete loan
epistemic_state.rationale: Gaev inflates preliminary conversations into a multi-pronged strategy. He treats vague social encounters as near-commitments and assumes others' goodwill without evidence.

beat_states.defense_state: grandiosity and verbal inflation — escalating his promises to oaths and swearing on his honour to compensate for the flimsiness of his actual plans
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he swears with increasing fervor precisely because he senses his plans are insubstantial; the more uncertain he is internally, the more theatrically certain he performs
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Gaev may be entirely performing for the girls' benefit with no self-deception at all — purely trying to calm them while privately despairing — but the sugar-candy and escalating oaths suggest he is also convincing himself.

beat_states.desire_state: to hear something genuinely hopeful about saving the estate
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to hold the household together and preserve the family's home through practical action and faith
beat_states.obstacle: Gaev's plans are transparently flimsy, and she knows talk is his substitute for action

affect_state.rationale: Her single line is a prayer — the utterance of someone who feels powerless and uncertain. She is anxious, not reassured by Gaev's words, and exposed in her dependence on forces beyond her control.

social_state.rationale: She occupies a subordinate position as the adopted niece who manages the household but has no real authority or resources. Her warmth is present but muted by worry.

known_facts: The estate faces auction
Gaev talks more than he acts
Lopakhin has proposed cutting down the cherry orchard as a solution
hidden_secrets: She likely suspects none of Gaev's plans will work
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya is the most practically minded family member; she has few illusions, which is why her response is a prayer rather than enthusiastic agreement.

beat_states.defense_state: displacement into religious hope — 'If only God would help us' redirects her anxiety toward faith rather than confronting Gaev's inadequacy
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she wants to believe Gaev's plans but has already admonished him to stop talking, revealing she knows his words are unreliable
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Varya's 'If only God would help us' could be read as genuine hope sparked by Gaev's words rather than disguised skepticism, but the earlier beat where she tells him to be quiet suggests she doesn't trust his rhetoric.

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: [She is calm again and happy] How good and clever you are, uncle. [Embraces him] I’m happy now! I’m happy! All’s well!

beat_states.desire_state: to believe that uncle Gaev's plan will save the cherry orchard and her family
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to restore security and belonging in her childhood home and family
beat_states.obstacle: the fragility of Gaev's promises and the reality that his plan is almost certainly inadequate

affect_state.rationale: She has surrendered her earlier distress into a wave of relief and affection. The happiness is genuine but fragile — it depends entirely on trusting Gaev's vague assurances. High valence but low actual control; she has no agency in the financial plan. Her vulnerability remains high beneath the surface calm because she has staked her emotional equilibrium on a fantasy.

social_state.rationale: She positions herself as the grateful, admiring child looking up to her uncle, granting him elevated status through praise and physical embrace. Maximum warmth and affection.

known_facts: The cherry orchard is to be auctioned in August
Gaev has mentioned a plan involving a loan against a promissory note and appealing to the aunt in Yaroslavl
Gaev has been told repeatedly by others to talk less
hidden_secrets: She likely senses, at some unconscious level, that Gaev's plan is unreliable
false_beliefs: Uncle Gaev is clever and capable enough to save the estate
The crisis is essentially resolved now that a plan has been voiced
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya is a young woman desperate for reassurance. She seizes on Gaev's words as fact because she cannot bear the alternative. Her epistemic stance is shaped by emotional need rather than evidence.

beat_states.defense_state: idealization — she inflates Gaev's competence and plan to ward off anxiety, converting uncertainty into premature relief
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She calls Gaev 'good and clever' when moments earlier she witnessed him being told to keep quiet because he talks too much; her happiness is built on willful credulity rather than evidence
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Anya may be performing happiness partly to hold the family together emotionally — rewarding Gaev for committing to silence and action, functioning as a subtle form of behavioral conditioning rather than pure naïveté.

utterances.speaker: FIERS
utterances.text: [Reproachfully] Leonid Andreyevitch, don’t you fear God? When are you going to bed?

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: Soon, soon. You go away, Fiers. I’ll undress myself. Well, children, bye-bye...! I’ll give you the details to-morrow, but let’s go to bed now. [Kisses ANYA and VARYA] I’m a man of the eighties.... People don’t praise those years much, but I can still say that I’ve suffered for my beliefs. The peasants don’t love me for nothing, I assure you. We’ve got to learn to know the peasants! We ought to learn how....

beat_states.desire_state: to get Gaev to stop talking and go to bed immediately
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve the old order and take care of the family as he always has
beat_states.obstacle: Gaev's compulsive need to keep talking and performing for his audience

affect_state.rationale: Fiers is exasperated but certain in his authority as caretaker; his reproach carries the weight of decades of service and genuine concern for Gaev's well-being

social_state.rationale: Formally a servant and thus low status, but his long tenure and age grant him a unique moral authority to reproach Gaev; his scolding comes from genuine care

known_facts: Gaev stays up too late talking nonsense
Gaev's speeches are self-indulgent and unproductive
the household needs rest
epistemic_state.rationale: Fiers sees clearly through Gaev's bluster and knows the practical reality that talk solves nothing and rest is needed

beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He is a servant who exercises parental authority over his master

beat_states.desire_state: to reassure the family (and himself) that he is a man of substance and moral authority who will save the estate
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to maintain his self-image as a dignified, principled gentleman worthy of respect and love
beat_states.obstacle: Fiers's reproach punctures his performance; his own emptiness beneath the rhetoric; the estate crisis he cannot actually solve

affect_state.rationale: Gaev is buoyed by the warmth of Anya's embrace and the fantasy of being wise and capable, but beneath the bluster he is deeply uncertain and out of control; his rambling reveals anxiety and the need to convince himself as much as anyone

social_state.rationale: He performs warmth through kisses and endearments; he asserts status through appeals to his moral history, but Fiers's scolding and the overall family dynamic undermine his authority

known_facts: the estate is threatened with auction
he has promised to find a solution
Fiers wants him to go to bed
hidden_secrets: he has no real plan to save the estate
his confidence is entirely performative
false_beliefs: the peasants love him for his principled suffering
being 'a man of the eighties' gives him moral and practical authority
he will provide details tomorrow that will resolve the crisis
epistemic_state.rationale: Gaev's self-knowledge is severely limited; he genuinely seems to believe in his own narrative of principled suffering and peasant affection, which is Chekhov's ironic portrait of aristocratic self-delusion

beat_states.defense_state: intellectualization and self-aggrandizement — wrapping himself in the mantle of 'a man of the eighties' to avoid confronting his ineffectiveness
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He agrees to stop talking and then immediately launches into another self-glorifying speech; he claims peasant love and moral authority while being utterly disconnected from practical reality
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: A small possibility that Gaev is consciously performing confidence he doesn't feel specifically to comfort Anya, rather than being genuinely self-deluded

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: You’re doing it again, uncle!

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: Be quiet, uncle!

utterances.speaker: FIERS
utterances.text: [Angrily] Leonid Andreyevitch!

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: I’m coming, I’m coming.... Go to bed now. Off two cushions into the middle! I turn over a new leaf.... [Exit. FIERS goes out after him.]

beat_states.desire_state: to stop her uncle from rambling about billiards again and get him to go to bed
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve hope and stability for the family during this crisis
beat_states.obstacle: Gaev's compulsive, undisciplined verbal habits undermine any seriousness

affect_state.rationale: Mild frustration and fatigue; she's losing confidence in Gaev's capacity even as she wants to believe in him

social_state.rationale: Despite being the youngest, she takes on a parental corrective role with warmth still intact

known_facts: Gaev constantly reverts to billiard talk
The cherry orchard is in danger
Gaev just promised to be quiet
hidden_secrets: Her growing doubt that Gaev can actually save anything
false_beliefs: That Gaev's plans might still work out
epistemic_state.rationale: She recognizes the pattern but still clings to hope planted by Gaev's earlier reassurances

beat_states.defense_state: affectionate exasperation masks deeper anxiety about his reliability
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She just praised him as 'good and clever' moments ago, now must correct him like a child

beat_states.desire_state: to get Gaev to bed immediately, fulfilling his duty as servant and surrogate caretaker
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to maintain the old order and care for the family as he always has
beat_states.obstacle: Gaev's childish resistance and distractibility

affect_state.rationale: Genuinely angry — his high certainty comes from decades of knowing Gaev needs managing; arousal elevated by frustration

social_state.rationale: Nominally low status as servant, but the anger is rooted in deep familial warmth and protectiveness

known_facts: Gaev is old enough to know better
It is very late
Gaev's habits have never changed
hidden_secrets: His fear that this disintegrating family will be the end of his world
false_beliefs: That maintaining old routines can keep the old order intact
epistemic_state.rationale: Fiers operates from lifelong knowledge of the household but is blind to how irreversibly things have changed

beat_states.defense_state: righteous indignation channels his deep worry into anger
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: A servant asserting authority over his master, driven by love that inverts the social hierarchy

beat_states.desire_state: to escape the pressure of everyone correcting him and retreat into the comfort of his billiard metaphors
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve his dignity and sense of self-worth in a world slipping away from him
beat_states.obstacle: Everyone is calling him out; his compulsive billiard talk keeps exposing his inability to be serious

affect_state.rationale: Sheepish and embarrassed but unable to stop himself; very low control and certainty reflect his fundamental helplessness

social_state.rationale: Nominally the senior male of the family but treated like a child by everyone; maintains affable warmth as a shield

known_facts: Everyone finds his billiard talk irritating
He promised to stop talking
He needs to go to bed
hidden_secrets: He has no real plan to save the estate
His self-awareness about his own ineffectuality is deeper than he lets on
false_beliefs: That he can 'turn over a new leaf'
That his billiard metaphors are merely a quirk rather than a symptom of psychological avoidance
epistemic_state.rationale: Gaev half-knows he is failing but cannot confront it; the billiard talk is an involuntary tell of his dissociation

beat_states.defense_state: compulsive billiard language serves as dissociation from emotional and financial realities
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He declares he'll 'turn over a new leaf' in the very same breath as another billiard reference, revealing the impossibility of his self-reform
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Gaev's final billiard reference could be read as a deliberate, self-aware joke to lighten the mood before exiting, rather than purely compulsive

beat_states.desire_state: to silence Gaev and enforce order before the household descends further into chaos
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to hold the family and estate together through practical management
beat_states.obstacle: Gaev is incorrigible; she has no real authority over him

affect_state.rationale: Irritated and weary; her attempt at control reveals how little she actually has

social_state.rationale: She occupies a functionally higher domestic role but is socially subordinate to Gaev; warmth is low due to frustration

known_facts: Gaev's billiard talk is a nervous compulsion
The estate's financial situation is dire
Gaev's promises are unreliable
hidden_secrets: Her fear that no one in the family is capable of saving the estate
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya is the most clear-eyed about the family's dysfunction, though she may underestimate external options

beat_states.defense_state: stern managerial posture conceals her exhaustion and despair
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She needs Gaev to be a responsible patriarch but must constantly parent him

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: I’m quieter now. I don’t want to go to Yaroslav, I don’t like grandmother; but I’m calm now; thanks to uncle. [Sits down.]

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: It’s time to go to sleep. I’ll go. There’s been an unpleasantness here while you were away. In the old servants’ part of the house, as you know, only the old people live--little old Efim and Polya and Evstigney, and Karp as well. They started letting some tramps or other spend the night there--I said nothing. Then I heard that they were saying that I had ordered them to be fed on peas and nothing else; from meanness, you see.... And it was all Evstigney’s doing.... Very well, I thought, if that’s what the matter is, just you wait. So I call Evstigney.... [Yawns] He comes. “What’s this,” I say, “Evstigney, you old fool.”... [Looks at ANYA] Anya dear! [Pause] She’s dropped off.... [Takes ANYA’S arm] Let’s go to bye-bye.... Come along!... [Leads her] My darling’s gone to sleep! Come on.... [They go. In the distance, the other side of the orchard, a shepherd plays his pipe. TROFIMOV crosses the stage and stops on seeing VARYA and ANYA] Sh! She’s asleep, asleep. Come on, dear.

beat_states.desire_state: to find peace and rest after the emotional turmoil of returning home
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to feel safe and loved within a stable family home
beat_states.obstacle: emotional and physical exhaustion overtaking her

affect_state.rationale: She has achieved a temporary emotional equilibrium through Gaev's reassurances, but her immediate falling asleep reveals how drained she truly is. The calm is genuine but shallow, a child's trust rather than adult conviction.

social_state.rationale: She is the youngest, most dependent person in the room, gratefully acknowledging uncle's comfort. She radiates warmth but occupies a childlike, low-status position.

known_facts: The cherry orchard is threatened with sale
Uncle has promised to handle the situation
She doesn't like grandmother in Yaroslav
hidden_secrets: Her deeper fears about the family's ruin that she's suppressing
false_beliefs: Uncle's reassurances may actually resolve the crisis
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya has accepted Gaev's empty promises as genuine comfort. She doesn't yet see through the family's pattern of avoidance.

beat_states.defense_state: regression — retreating into childlike sleep as escape from adult anxieties
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: claims to be calm and resolved but is so fragile she falls asleep mid-conversation, suggesting the calm is a surface over deep exhaustion
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Anya's falling asleep could be read as a deliberate withdrawal — she senses the promises are hollow and unconsciously chooses not to stay awake for more

beat_states.desire_state: to take care of Anya and assert her competence as household manager
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to prove her worth and indispensability to the family she fears losing
beat_states.obstacle: the servants undermine her authority; the household is disintegrating around her; no one truly listens to her concerns

affect_state.rationale: She yawns — exhausted herself — and her story trails off when she notices Anya sleeping. There's tenderness mixed with weariness. She maintains a façade of practical authority but the undercurrent is loneliness and fatigue.

social_state.rationale: She occupies the caretaker/surrogate mother role — higher status than Anya in this moment as protector, but structurally marginalized as the adopted daughter who runs the house without owning it. Her warmth toward sleeping Anya is deeply genuine.

known_facts: Servants have been letting tramps sleep in the old quarters
Evstigney spread rumors about her ordering peas-only meals
The household is barely held together
Anya is exhausted from travel
hidden_secrets: Her own loneliness and desire for a life beyond household management
Her anxiety that all her labor may be for nothing if the estate is sold
false_beliefs: That maintaining strict household order can somehow stave off the larger catastrophe
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya is hyperaware of the domestic details but may be using that awareness to avoid confronting the existential threat. Her competence is real but ultimately beside the point.

beat_states.defense_state: displacement — channeling her deep anxiety about the estate's future into a story about disciplining servants
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She presents herself as firmly in control (disciplining Evstigney, managing the household) while the very story she tells reveals how little authority she actually commands — servants gossip about her, tramps are let in without her knowledge
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Varya's servant anecdote could be read as a bid for recognition — telling Anya (and the audience) about how hard she works, hoping someone will finally notice and validate her sacrifice

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: [Quietly, half-asleep] I’m so tired... all the bells... uncle, dear! Mother and uncle!

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: Come on, dear, come on! [They go into ANYA’S room.]

beat_states.desire_state: to surrender to sleep and feel safe and loved
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve the warmth and security of her family and home
beat_states.obstacle: exhaustion and the weight of anxieties about the family's future pulling her under

affect_state.rationale: Barely conscious, Anya drifts into sleep murmuring the names of her loved ones — bells echoing from the journey or from half-dream. She is maximally vulnerable, stripped of defenses by exhaustion, reaching out for connection as she loses consciousness.

social_state.rationale: She is a dependent child in this moment, low in status and authority, but radiating affection and need as she names her mother and uncle.

known_facts: The family is in financial trouble
The cherry orchard may be sold
She has just returned from a long, exhausting journey
hidden_secrets: The depth of her worry about her mother's spending and the family's future
false_beliefs: In this liminal state, the bells of the journey seem still present — reality and dream are blurring
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya possesses real knowledge of the crisis but in this moment her consciousness is dissolving; factual awareness gives way to sensory fragments and emotional attachment.

beat_states.defense_state: regression — retreating into a childlike state to avoid conscious worry
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she is aware of the family's dire situation but in this moment abandons adult awareness for the comfort of a child calling out for loved ones
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: The mention of 'mother and uncle' could be a half-conscious plea for them to resolve the crisis rather than pure affection

beat_states.desire_state: to get Anya safely to bed and take care of her
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to hold the household together and fulfill her role as caretaker in the absence of effective authority
beat_states.obstacle: she can only offer physical care — she cannot solve the larger problems weighing on Anya and the family

affect_state.rationale: Varya is tender and gentle here, finding genuine warmth in the act of caring for Anya, which gives her a small sense of purpose and control. But the calm is fragile — beneath it lies unresolved dread.

social_state.rationale: She assumes the quasi-maternal authority of an older sister guiding a younger one to bed, fully warm and nurturing.

known_facts: Anya is exhausted from the journey
The estate faces auction
She is responsible for day-to-day household management
hidden_secrets: Her own fears about her future if the estate is sold
Her unresolved feelings about Lopakhin and whether he will propose
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya is the most practically informed member of the household about its daily operations, but she keeps her personal anxieties hidden behind her caretaker role.

beat_states.defense_state: displacement — channeling her own anxiety into practical caretaking action
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she performs maternal competence and calm while internally carrying the weight of the estate's impending loss and her own uncertain future
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Varya's brisk 'come on, dear' might also contain a subtle desire to end the emotionally charged evening before more painful truths surface

utterances.speaker: TROFIMOV
utterances.text: [Moved] My sun! My spring! Curtain.

beat_states.desire_state: to cherish and protect the sleeping Anya, expressing his profound emotional connection to her
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to find meaning and hope for the future through idealistic love and intellectual purpose
beat_states.obstacle: Anya is asleep and cannot hear him; the social and economic gap between a perpetual student and a landowner's daughter; his own awareness that this tenderness may be unrequited or impossible

affect_state.rationale: Trofimov is deeply moved — the stage direction says so explicitly. He is overwhelmed by tenderness watching Anya fall asleep. High positive valence from love and hope, but low certainty and control because this love is unspoken and the future is uncertain. Extremely vulnerable because he is exposing his deepest emotional core to an unconscious listener, which is precisely what makes this safe enough to say.

social_state.rationale: Trofimov occupies low social status — he is the deceased son's tutor, a perpetual student with no money or position. But in this moment of private devotion over a sleeping Anya, he radiates maximum warmth and tenderness, almost parental/reverent in quality.

known_facts: Anya has fallen asleep exhausted from travel
Anya is troubled by her family's financial situation and her grandmother
He was tutor to Anya's deceased brother Grisha
The cherry orchard and estate face auction
Anya represents youth, future, and possibility to him
hidden_secrets: The depth of his romantic feeling for Anya
That he may see Anya as redemption for the loss of Grisha and his own failed life trajectory
false_beliefs: That his feelings can remain safely abstract and philosophical rather than personal
epistemic_state.rationale: Trofimov knows the practical realities of the family's decline but in this moment is consumed by private emotion. He speaks only because Anya cannot hear — revealing what he conceals from the waking world.

beat_states.defense_state: sublimation — channeling romantic desire into poetic, metaphorical language ('my sun, my spring') to avoid directly naming his love
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He is a self-declared rationalist and revolutionary idealist who in this private moment reveals himself to be deeply romantic and emotionally dependent on Anya as his source of hope
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Trofimov's exclamation could be read as purely paternal/avuncular — seeing in Anya the future of Russia rather than a personal romantic object — making 'my sun, my spring' ideological metaphor rather than love confession.

utterances.speaker: CHARLOTTA
utterances.text: [Thoughtfully] I haven’t a real passport. I don’t know how old I am, and I think I’m young. When I was a little girl my father and mother used to go round fairs and give very good performances and I used to do the _salto mortale_ and various little things. And when papa and mamma died a German lady took me to her and began to teach me. I liked it. I grew up and became a governess. And where I came from and who I am, I don’t know.... Who my parents were--perhaps they weren’t married--I don’t know. [Takes a cucumber out of her pocket and eats] I don’t know anything. [Pause] I do want to talk, but I haven’t anybody to talk to... I haven’t anybody at all.

beat_states.desire_state: to be witnessed and known by someone — to establish her own existence through telling her story
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to find belonging and identity in a world where she has neither roots nor permanent place
beat_states.obstacle: there is no one genuinely available to listen or connect with her; she lacks the basic facts of her own identity

affect_state.rationale: Deep sadness and existential loneliness pervade the speech, but the low arousal and studied nonchalance suggest a practiced melancholy. She has almost no certainty about herself — identity, age, origins are all unknown. She has little control over her circumstances or her need for connection.

social_state.rationale: As a governess with no passport, no family, and no confirmed identity, she occupies a marginal social position. Her warmth is muted but present — she reaches out to the void, willing to connect but unable to find a partner for genuine exchange.

known_facts: Her parents performed at fairs
She performed the salto mortale as a child
Her parents died
A German lady raised and educated her
She became a governess
She has no real passport
hidden_secrets: The depth of her anguish at having no identity or belonging
Possible shame about her parents' potentially unmarried status
epistemic_state.rationale: Charlotta's epistemic state is defined by absence — she knows fragments of her past but cannot construct a coherent identity. The repetition of 'I don't know' is not rhetorical but literal. Her knowledge is a series of disconnected episodes without a unifying narrative.

beat_states.defense_state: deflect — she uses casual physical action (eating a cucumber) and a tone of philosophical detachment to mask the depth of her loneliness
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she desperately wants to talk and connect yet announces her isolation as if it were an accepted, almost amusing fact; she performs vulnerability while simultaneously shutting down the possibility of receiving comfort
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Charlotta may be performing her loneliness partly as a circus-trained entertainer's monologue — a rehearsed routine she uses to test whether anyone in her environment will respond, making the speech as much a provocation or audition for connection as a genuine confession.

utterances.speaker: EPIKHODOV
utterances.text: [Plays on the guitar and sings] “What is this noisy earth to me, What matter friends and foes?” I do like playing on the mandoline!

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: That’s a guitar, not a mandoline. [Looks at herself in a little mirror and powders herself.]

utterances.speaker: EPIKHODOV
utterances.text: For the enamoured madman, this is a mandoline. [Sings] “Oh that the heart was warmed, By all the flames of love returned!”

beat_states.desire_state: to attend to her own attractiveness and feel desirable, not to be bothered by Epikhodov's clumsy courtship
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to rise above her station and be loved by someone refined and worthy — ultimately Yasha, not Epikhodov
beat_states.obstacle: Epikhodov is present and demanding attention she doesn't want to give him
