social_state.rationale: He has been publicly humiliated by a woman he respects, reducing his status drastically. His declaration that 'all is over between us' is cold and severing — an attempt to reclaim some dignity by cutting the relationship, but it reads as petulant rather than authoritative.

known_facts: Lubov has called him a 'schoolboy' and mocked his lack of romantic/sexual experience
Lubov is still emotionally entangled with the man in Paris
He has been lecturing Lubov about rising above love
hidden_secrets: He is deeply insecure about his perpetual-student status and possible virginity
Lubov's opinion matters far more to him than his philosophy of detachment would allow
false_beliefs: He believes his dramatic exit constitutes a dignified moral stand rather than an exposure of his own fragility
epistemic_state.rationale: Trofimov's intellectual framework cannot absorb the personal attack. His false belief that this is a principled rupture rather than a wounded retreat is central to Chekhov's comedy here.

beat_states.defense_state: dramatic self-righteous withdrawal — declaring 'all is over between us' to reclaim moral high ground and reframe his retreat as a principled break rather than humiliated flight
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he claims to be above petty emotions and romance, yet his reaction is wildly emotional, theatrical, and deeply wounded — revealing exactly the immaturity Lubov accused him of
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: A small possibility that Trofimov's reaction is partly performed — that he is consciously dramatizing to guilt-trip Lubov into apologizing, using his distress strategically to regain the upper hand.

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: [Shouts after him] Peter, wait! Silly man, I was joking! Peter! [Somebody is heard going out and falling downstairs noisily. ANYA and VARYA scream; laughter is heard immediately] What’s that?

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: Peter’s fallen downstairs! [Runs out again.]

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: This Peter’s a marvel. [The STATION-MASTER stands in the middle of the drawing-room and recites “The Magdalen” by Tolstoy. He is listened to, but he has only delivered a few lines when a waltz is heard from the front room, and the recitation is stopped. Everybody dances. TROFIMOV, ANYA, VARYA, and LUBOV ANDREYEVNA come in from the front room.]

beat_states.desire_state: to alert everyone and check that Trofimov is not hurt
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to protect and hold together the people she loves, especially as the family faces dissolution
beat_states.obstacle: she is caught between concern for Trofimov and loyalty to her mother who caused the scene

affect_state.rationale: High arousal from the screaming and sudden physical accident. She is frightened but quickly reassured. Her concern is genuine but she doesn't linger — she acts.

social_state.rationale: She occupies the position of concerned younger person — low status but high warmth, rushing to help without judgment.

known_facts: Trofimov has fallen down the stairs
Her mother and Trofimov had a terrible argument
Her mother was shouting after Trofimov to come back
hidden_secrets: Her deepening romantic attachment to Trofimov
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya is a relatively transparent character here — she sees what happened and responds with immediate caring action. Her feelings for Trofimov add urgency to her running out.

beat_states.defense_state: channels anxiety into action — running to check on Trofimov rather than confronting anyone
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she loves both her mother and Trofimov, and must navigate the aftermath of her mother attacking the man she admires
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Anya's scream and rush to Trofimov may betray the depth of her feeling for him more than she intends, making this a moment of inadvertent emotional exposure.

beat_states.desire_state: to repair the rupture with Trofimov and restore lightness after her cruel outburst
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve love, connection, and the feeling of being surrounded by people who care — to stave off abandonment and loss
beat_states.obstacle: Trofimov has stormed out in horror; her words cannot be taken back; she has revealed her cruelty and desperation

affect_state.rationale: Swinging from shouting apology to laughter — high arousal masking genuine distress. She's lost control of the interaction and knows she went too far. The laughter is a relief valve, not genuine joy.

social_state.rationale: She reclaims some social status by treating the incident with affectionate humor ('This Peter's a marvel'), performing warmth and maternal indulgence to erase the ugliness of what just happened.

known_facts: She wounded Trofimov deeply with her words about his lack of romantic experience
Trofimov has physically fallen downstairs in his dramatic exit
The party continues around her regardless of personal drama
She receives daily telegrams from her Paris lover
hidden_secrets: She knows her attachment to the man in Paris is self-destructive
Her cruelty to Trofimov came from a place of genuine pain about her own romantic failures
false_beliefs: That calling it a joke can undo the harm
epistemic_state.rationale: Lubov is aware she crossed a line but immediately deploys her social charm to rewrite the moment. She cannot fully face that her attack on Trofimov was a projection of her own shame about her lover.

beat_states.defense_state: converts a painful moment into comedy — laughing at Trofimov's fall to neutralize the emotional damage she caused
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she genuinely regrets hurting Trofimov but immediately reframes his dramatic exit (and literal fall) as charming and amusing rather than confronting her own cruelty
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: She may genuinely find the physical comedy of the fall funny enough to snap her out of her emotional spiral — the laughter could be partly authentic relief rather than purely defensive.

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: Well, Peter... you pure soul... I beg your pardon... let’s dance. [She dances with PETER. ANYA and VARYA dance. FIERS enters and stands his stick by a side door. YASHA has also come in and looks on at the dance.]

beat_states.desire_state: To repair the rupture with Trofimov and restore warmth and normalcy through the act of dancing together
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To hold onto love, beauty, and human connection as everything she cherishes slips away
beat_states.obstacle: Her own guilt at having cruelly wounded Trofimov; the lingering tension from their argument; the underlying dread about the auction happening at this very moment

affect_state.rationale: The tenderness in 'you pure soul' reveals genuine remorse and affection, but her emotional state is fragile — she has just shouted, then chased after him, and now must humble herself. The dancing is a desperate attempt to create lightness, but the vulnerability beneath is enormous.

social_state.rationale: Lubov deliberately lowers herself by begging pardon from a young student — an act of genuine humility unusual for someone of her social position. Her warmth is high as she extends herself generously to heal the wound she inflicted.

known_facts: She hurt Trofimov deeply with her comments about his romantic inexperience
The auction of the cherry orchard is happening right now while she dances
Trofimov is idealistic and sensitive beneath his intellectual posturing
She has organized this party as a distraction from the auction
hidden_secrets: She knows her cruelty to Trofimov was partly a projection of her own shame about her Paris lover
She is terrified about the auction result and uses social activity to suppress panic
false_beliefs: That dancing and socializing can genuinely hold catastrophe at bay
epistemic_state.rationale: Lubov is deeply self-aware emotionally but chronically unable to act on her knowledge. She understands she was wrong but the deeper self-knowledge — that all of this gaiety is a defense against ruin — remains half-conscious.

beat_states.defense_state: Displacement — channeling emotional distress into the physical ritual of dancing to avoid confronting deeper pain
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She genuinely seeks reconciliation and warmth, yet the very act of dancing at the party is itself a form of denial — she is performing joy while her estate is being sold out from under her
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lubov's apology could be more performative than sincere — a social reflex to smooth things over rather than genuine contrition — but the specificity of 'you pure soul' suggests real emotional recognition of Trofimov's innocence and her own transgression.

utterances.speaker: YASHA
utterances.text: Well, grandfather?

utterances.speaker: FIERS
utterances.text: I’m not well. At our balls some time back, generals and barons and admirals used to dance, and now we send for post-office clerks and the Station-master, and even they come as a favour. I’m very weak. The dead master, the grandfather, used to give everybody sealing-wax when anything was wrong. I’ve taken sealing-wax every day for twenty years, and more; perhaps that’s why I still live.

utterances.speaker: YASHA
utterances.text: I’m tired of you, grandfather. [Yawns] If you’d only hurry up and kick the bucket.

utterances.speaker: FIERS
utterances.text: Oh you... bungler! [Mutters.] [TROFIMOV and LUBOV ANDREYEVNA dance in the reception-room, then into the sitting-room.]

beat_states.desire_state: to be acknowledged, to assert the dignity of the old order and his place within it
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve the memory and continuity of the old estate and its way of life
beat_states.obstacle: the world has irreversibly changed; no one respects or listens to him; his body is failing

affect_state.rationale: Physically weak and spiritually bereft, Fiers speaks with the exhausted certainty of someone who has witnessed an entire world collapse. His low arousal reflects depleted energy, but his conviction about the superiority of the past remains firm.

social_state.rationale: Once a servant in a grand household attended by generals, he is now ignored or mocked by a young valet. His response to Yasha ('bungler') shows contempt rather than warmth, but it's defensive rather than aggressive.

known_facts: The household once hosted generals, barons, and admirals
Now only post-office clerks and station masters attend
He has taken sealing-wax daily for twenty years as medicine
He is physically very weak
hidden_secrets: He likely senses the estate is about to be lost permanently
false_beliefs: Sealing-wax is what has kept him alive
The old order was inherently superior and good
epistemic_state.rationale: Fiers operates on folk wisdom and aristocratic nostalgia. His medical belief in sealing-wax is a superstition that doubles as a spiritual tether to the dead master. His idealization of the past obscures the reality of serfdom.

beat_states.defense_state: nostalgic idealization — elevating the past to diminish the degraded present
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: clings to life through ritual (sealing-wax) while everything he lived for is dying around him
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Fiers may be performing his frailty and nostalgia partly to elicit care or guilt from those around him, not purely lamenting

beat_states.desire_state: to escape this conversation and this decrepit world entirely
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to leave Russia behind and return to Paris, to a life of sophistication and pleasure
beat_states.obstacle: he is trapped at this provincial party, forced to interact with relics of the old order like Fiers

affect_state.rationale: Bored and irritated rather than deeply affected. His yawn is genuine — he feels no threat from Fiers. The cruelty of wishing the old man dead is casual, reflecting emotional flatness rather than rage.

social_state.rationale: Yasha asserts dominance over the only person he can — the ancient, weakened Fiers. His coldness is extreme; telling someone to 'kick the bucket' is deliberately dehumanizing. He positions himself as modern and superior.

known_facts: Fiers is very old and frail
The party is happening but is a diminished affair
He considers this provincial life beneath him
hidden_secrets: His sophistication is a thin veneer over his servant origins
He has abandoned his mother without remorse
false_beliefs: He is fundamentally different from and superior to Fiers
Paris represents a life where he would have real status
epistemic_state.rationale: Yasha's worldview is built on self-serving contempt for his origins. He cannot see that he and Fiers are mirrors — servants defined by their masters — separated only by generational attitude.

beat_states.defense_state: contemptuous detachment — treating the old man as already dead neutralizes any emotional claim
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: performatively cosmopolitan yet still a servant; cruelty toward Fiers masks his own low status
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Yasha's cruelty could mask a suppressed fear of his own mortality or of ending up like Fiers — old, useless, forgotten

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: _Merci_. I’ll sit down. [Sits] I’m tired.

beat_states.desire_state: to rest and recover composure after the emotional turbulence of the evening
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve the beauty and grace of her life against the encroaching reality of loss
beat_states.obstacle: physical and emotional exhaustion from the party, the unresolved auction, and the series of confrontations

affect_state.rationale: The simple admission 'I'm tired' after the forced gaiety of dancing with Trofimov carries immense weight. The exhaustion is not merely physical — it's the weariness of maintaining a front. Valence is low, arousal has dropped after the dance, certainty about her future is minimal, and control is slipping. She is deeply vulnerable beneath the social pleasantries.

social_state.rationale: She maintains the manners of a hostess ('Merci') but her status is diminished — she is no longer animating the room but retreating to sit. The warmth is present but muted, a residue of social habit rather than genuine engagement.

known_facts: The cherry orchard is being auctioned today
No word has come yet about the auction result
She has just reconciled with Trofimov after their argument
Firs has just collapsed or been unwell nearby
hidden_secrets: She is terrified of the auction outcome
She suspects the estate may already be lost
false_beliefs: That maintaining the party atmosphere can somehow hold catastrophe at bay
epistemic_state.rationale: Lubov knows the auction is happening but has no news. She keeps the party going as a kind of magical thinking — as if normality can prevent disaster. Her tiredness is the body's honest admission of what her social self refuses to acknowledge.

beat_states.defense_state: performing gracious normalcy — the polite 'merci' masks deep depletion
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she hosts a party and dances while her estate is being auctioned; she performs social ease while internally collapsing
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: The tiredness could be read as purely physical after dancing, with no deeper psychological resonance — simply a moment of catching her breath before the next social interaction.

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: [Excited] Somebody in the kitchen was saying just now that the cherry orchard was sold to-day.

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: Sold to whom?

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: He didn’t say to whom. He’s gone now. [Dances out into the reception-room with TROFIMOV.]

beat_states.desire_state: to share the shocking news she's just heard about the cherry orchard's sale
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to help her mother face the future and move forward into a new life
beat_states.obstacle: she doesn't have complete information — she doesn't know who bought it

affect_state.rationale: Anya is agitated and excited — the news is enormous but incomplete. Her excitement contains both dread and a youthful readiness for change. She lacks certainty about the details and control over the situation.

social_state.rationale: As the daughter bringing bad news, she is in a subordinate position but approaches her mother with genuine concern and openness. Her quick departure with Trofimov suggests she gravitates toward her peer and future rather than staying to comfort Lubov.

known_facts: Someone in the kitchen said the cherry orchard was sold today
The person who said it has already left
She does not know who bought it
hidden_secrets: She may feel a secret sense of liberation at the prospect of the estate being sold
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya has only secondhand, incomplete information. She reports what she heard faithfully but cannot verify or elaborate.

beat_states.defense_state: deflection through movement — she dances away before having to process her mother's reaction or the full emotional weight
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she is 'excited' according to the stage direction, suggesting a mix of alarm and perhaps unconscious relief that the old life is ending, even as she knows this will devastate her mother
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Anya's 'excitement' could be read as pure anxiety rather than containing any element of relief — she may simply be a frightened daughter delivering terrible news and fleeing the scene.

beat_states.desire_state: to know immediately who bought the cherry orchard — to grasp the full reality of what has happened
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to hold onto the cherry orchard and the world of her past, her identity, her memories
beat_states.obstacle: the information is incomplete and Anya leaves before she can get answers; she is trapped in ignorance at the most critical moment

affect_state.rationale: This is a moment of existential shock. Her single urgent question ('Sold to whom?') reveals terror barely contained. She is left without answers, without control, maximally vulnerable. The party continues around her while her world collapses.

social_state.rationale: Her status is collapsing — the estate that defined her social identity is gone. Her warmth is muted by shock; she is focused entirely on extracting information rather than emotional connection.

known_facts: The cherry orchard has reportedly been sold today
The auction was happening today while she threw a party
No one present can tell her who bought it
hidden_secrets: She has been throwing the party partly to avoid facing the auction's outcome
She has spent recklessly and bears responsibility for the financial ruin
false_beliefs: She may still harbor a faint hope that the report is wrong or that Lopakhin's plan somehow saved things
epistemic_state.rationale: Lubov is thrust from willful ignorance into partial knowledge — the worst epistemic state. She knows the estate is sold but not to whom, leaving her in agonizing suspense.

beat_states.defense_state: her sharp, direct question is a desperate attempt to seize control of an uncontrollable situation
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she has known all along that the sale was likely, has been in denial and throwing a party during the auction, and now the reality crashes in — she both expected this and is utterly unprepared
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lubov's question could be read as already knowing the answer and testing whether it's Lopakhin, bracing for confirmation rather than genuinely ignorant.

utterances.speaker: YASHA
utterances.text: Some old man was chattering about it a long time ago. A stranger!

utterances.speaker: FIERS
utterances.text: And Leonid Andreyevitch isn’t here yet, he hasn’t come. He’s wearing a light, _demi-saison_ overcoat. He’ll catch cold. Oh these young fellows.

beat_states.desire_state: to ensure Leonid Andreyevitch's safety and well-being, expressing worry about his master's absence
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to maintain the old order and protect the family he has served his entire life
beat_states.obstacle: He is old and powerless; he cannot control his master's actions or the outcome of the auction

affect_state.rationale: Deep worry and helplessness; his anxiety is high but he channels it into habitual caretaking; deeply uncertain about the outcome and unable to affect anything

social_state.rationale: A devoted old servant whose warmth toward his masters is paternalistic despite his low social position; calling them 'young fellows' inverts the hierarchy with affectionate condescension

known_facts: Leonid Andreyevitch went to the auction
He wore a light overcoat inappropriate for the weather
He hasn't returned yet
hidden_secrets: His terror that the world he has known for 87 years is ending
false_beliefs: That worrying about the overcoat can somehow protect his master from the real danger
epistemic_state.rationale: Fiers focuses on concrete, manageable details because the larger truth — potential loss of the estate and the entire way of life — is too overwhelming to process directly

beat_states.defense_state: displacement — channeling his profound anxiety about the estate's sale into manageable concern about Gaev's overcoat
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He deflects from the catastrophic news about the orchard by worrying about a coat, suggesting he cannot face the larger existential threat
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Fiers may be deliberately redirecting the conversation away from the sale to protect Lubov from distress

beat_states.desire_state: to demonstrate his superior composure and indifference to the household's crisis
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to distance himself from the old estate world and assert his modern, cosmopolitan identity
beat_states.obstacle: Anya's excited report has created alarm that could disrupt the party atmosphere

affect_state.rationale: Yasha is emotionally uninvested in the cherry orchard's fate; his low arousal and casual dismissiveness reflect genuine indifference rather than suppressed concern

social_state.rationale: As a servant he holds lower formal status but asserts superiority through contemptuous dismissal of 'some old man' — cold and detached toward those around him

known_facts: Someone in the kitchen was talking about the orchard being sold
The auction was today
Lopakhin was the likely buyer
hidden_secrets: His fundamental indifference to the family's fate
epistemic_state.rationale: Yasha likely knows as much as anyone about the auction but strategically characterizes the source as unreliable to shut down the conversation

beat_states.defense_state: minimization — reducing the significance of the news by attributing it to an unreliable source
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He may actually know or suspect the sale is real, but his contempt for the old world makes him indifferent to what devastates others
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Yasha may genuinely not know and is dismissing it out of ignorance rather than strategy

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: I’ll die of this. Go and find out, Yasha, to whom it’s sold.

utterances.speaker: YASHA
utterances.text: Oh, but he’s been gone a long time, the old man. [Laughs.]

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: [Slightly vexed] Why do you laugh? What are you glad about?

utterances.speaker: YASHA
utterances.text: Epikhodov’s too funny. He’s a silly man. Two-and-twenty troubles.

beat_states.desire_state: to learn immediately who bought the cherry orchard
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve her identity, home, and the world the cherry orchard represents
beat_states.obstacle: no one present has the information; the messenger is gone; Yasha's flippancy blocks her from getting a serious response

affect_state.rationale: Existential dread about the auction result; she is exhausted, terrified, and unable to get answers. Yasha's laughter intensifies her sense of helplessness and insult.

social_state.rationale: She asserts mistress-to-servant authority by commanding Yasha and rebuking his laughter, but her desperation undermines her status. Warmth drops as she is vexed by his callousness.

known_facts: The auction has taken place
Someone in the kitchen said the cherry orchard was sold
She does not yet know to whom it was sold
hidden_secrets: She suspects the worst — that Lopakhin has bought it
Her financial ruin is nearly complete
false_beliefs: There may still be some hope that the sale went differently than feared
epistemic_state.rationale: She is in an agonizing epistemic gap — the most important fact of her life is unknown to her, and she cannot tolerate the uncertainty.

beat_states.defense_state: displacement of rage onto Yasha's laughter rather than confronting the terror of the sale
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She says 'I'll die of this' yet continues to function and give orders — her catastrophic language masks a still-operative will to act
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Her 'I'll die of this' could be read as performative aristocratic melodrama rather than genuine despair, but the accumulation of the evening's tension makes genuine anguish the stronger reading.

beat_states.desire_state: to avoid the burden of Lubov's anxiety and maintain his own comfort and detachment
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to escape provincial Russian life and return to Paris; to rise above his servant status through sophistication and indifference
beat_states.obstacle: Lubov directly confronts his laughter and demands accountability

affect_state.rationale: Emotionally disengaged from the family's crisis. His laughter is genuine amusement at others' foolishness, not anxiety. He feels relatively in control because he has no deep stake in the cherry orchard.

social_state.rationale: Nominally a servant but behaves with insolent familiarity. His laughter at a moment of his mistress's anguish shows cold disregard. He treats the household's suffering as entertainment.

known_facts: The old man who had information has already left
The auction has occurred
He does not know the buyer either
hidden_secrets: He likely cares very little about the outcome beyond how it affects his own plans
He despises provincial life and most of the household
epistemic_state.rationale: Yasha knows roughly what everyone else knows but is uninterested in pursuing the information. His epistemic stance is one of willful disengagement.

beat_states.defense_state: mocking deflection — redirecting attention to Epikhodov to avoid engaging with Lubov's distress or the seriousness of the situation
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He serves a household whose destruction would end his position, yet he treats the crisis with amusement — his contempt for provincial life makes him indifferent to what should alarm him
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Yasha's laughter and deflection to Epikhodov could be nervous laughter masking his own anxiety about job security, but his consistent characterization throughout the play as callously self-serving makes genuine indifference the stronger reading.

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: Fiers, if the estate is sold, where will you go?

utterances.speaker: FIERS
utterances.text: I’ll go wherever you order me to go.

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: Why do you look like that? Are you ill? I think you ought to go to bed....

utterances.speaker: FIERS
utterances.text: Yes... [With a smile] I’ll go to bed, and who’ll hand things round and give orders without me? I’ve the whole house on my shoulders.

beat_states.desire_state: to affirm his indispensability and his unbroken bond of service to Lubov and the household
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to maintain the old order and his place within it — his identity is inseparable from this house and this family
beat_states.obstacle: his body is failing him, and the estate may be sold, which would destroy the only world he knows

affect_state.rationale: He smiles but it masks deep fear; his pride in being needed is the only thing sustaining him against the terror of obsolescence and abandonment

social_state.rationale: Formally a servant, but his long tenure gives him an intimate familial standing; his warmth is expressed through gruff devotion rather than open affection

known_facts: He is old and physically declining
The estate may have been sold
He has served this household his entire life
No one else can manage the house as he does
hidden_secrets: He is terrified of being discarded or forgotten
He may know he is more seriously ill than he lets on
false_beliefs: He believes the household truly cannot function without him
He may believe the old way of life can somehow persist
epistemic_state.rationale: Fiers's worldview is rooted in the pre-emancipation order; he cannot conceive of life outside this household and constructs his identity entirely around his role

beat_states.defense_state: denial through duty — he deflects his physical fragility and existential terror by insisting on his irreplaceability
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he says he'll go wherever ordered, expressing total submission, but then immediately claims he holds the whole house on his shoulders — simultaneously subordinate and essential, powerless and indispensable
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: His insistence on staying up and working could be a form of gentle reproach to Lubov — reminding her that while she worries about the estate, the daily human infrastructure depends on him

beat_states.desire_state: to find comfort and connection by caring for Fiers while she awaits devastating news about the estate
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve the emotional bonds and world of the cherry orchard against the tide of change
beat_states.obstacle: she is consumed by anxiety about the auction results and cannot truly focus on anyone else; she also cannot protect those who depend on her

affect_state.rationale: Deep anxiety about the auction result spills into tenderness toward Fiers; she feels powerless and is grasping at small gestures of care to feel some sense of agency

social_state.rationale: She still occupies the role of mistress of the house, but her authority is crumbling; her warmth toward Fiers is genuine and maternal

known_facts: The auction has taken place
Someone said the cherry orchard was sold
Fiers is old and visibly unwell
She has sent Yasha to find out the results
hidden_secrets: She fears she has failed everyone who depends on her
false_beliefs: She may still believe there is a chance the estate was not sold
epistemic_state.rationale: She is in a liminal state of half-knowing — the news has been hinted at but not confirmed, and she oscillates between dread and denial

beat_states.defense_state: displacement — redirecting her unbearable anxiety about the estate sale onto concern for Fiers's health
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she asks Fiers where he will go if the estate is sold, acknowledging the reality she has been avoiding, while simultaneously trying to mother him as if she still has the power to care for anyone
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: She may be testing Fiers's loyalty — wanting to hear that he would follow her anywhere as reassurance of her worth

utterances.speaker: YASHA
utterances.text: [To LUBOV ANDREYEVNA] Lubov Andreyevna! I want to ask a favour of you, if you’ll be so kind! If you go to Paris again, then please take me with you. It’s absolutely impossible for me to stop here. [Looking round; in an undertone] What’s the good of talking about it, you see for yourself that this is an uneducated country, with an immoral population, and it’s so dull. The food in the kitchen is beastly, and here’s this Fiers walking about mumbling various inappropriate things. Take me with you, be so kind!

beat_states.desire_state: To secure Lubov's promise to take him to Paris when she goes
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To escape provincial Russian life and ascend to a cosmopolitan, sophisticated existence
beat_states.obstacle: Lubov is consumed by grief and anxiety over the estate sale; his request is trivially self-serving in the face of her crisis

affect_state.rationale: Urgency and desperation underlie his affected disdain; he senses the estate's collapse means his comfortable parasitic arrangement is threatened. His undertone and looking around suggest anxiety about being overheard, revealing his awareness that his contempt is socially dangerous.

social_state.rationale: He is a servant making a personal request of his mistress during her worst moment — low status despite his aspirations. His warmth is negative: he shows zero empathy for Lubov's anguish, coldly mocks Fiers behind his back, and dismisses the entire household.

known_facts: The estate auction has occurred and the result is imminent
Lubov has been to Paris before and may return
Fiers is old and increasingly incoherent
His own position depends entirely on Lubov's choices
hidden_secrets: He has been carrying on with Dunyasha and will abandon her without a thought
His loyalty to the family is purely transactional
false_beliefs: He believes himself genuinely superior to the Russian peasants and servants around him
He assumes Lubov will have the means and inclination to return to Paris
epistemic_state.rationale: Yasha knows the practical situation but lacks self-awareness about his own shallowness. He misreads the gravity of the moment, believing his personal desires are worth raising now.

beat_states.defense_state: Contempt for surroundings — by denigrating everything Russian, he elevates himself above his actual station and deflects from the fact that he is a servant begging for a favor
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He presents himself as too refined for this 'uneducated country' while being a valet who mocks an elderly servant — his sophistication is entirely superficial and borrowed from proximity to Paris
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Yasha may also be testing whether Lubov still has the resources and intention to go to Paris — using his request as a way to gauge the family's actual financial fate.

utterances.speaker: PISCHIN
utterances.text: I come to ask for the pleasure of a little waltz, dear lady.... [LUBOV ANDREYEVNA goes to him] But all the same, you wonderful woman, I must have 180 little roubles from you... I must.... [They dance] 180 little roubles.... [They go through into the drawing-room.]

utterances.speaker: YASHA
utterances.text: [Sings softly]   “Oh, will you understand My soul’s deep restlessness?” [In the drawing-room a figure in a grey top-hat and in baggy check trousers is waving its hands and jumping about; there are cries of “Bravo, Charlotta Ivanovna!”]

beat_states.desire_state: to extract 180 roubles from Lubov while maintaining the social cover of a dance
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to survive financially by borrowing from anyone available, always believing something will turn up
beat_states.obstacle: Lubov is distracted by grief over the estate auction; asking for money at this moment is socially awkward

affect_state.rationale: He is desperate for money but performs buoyancy and warmth; his repeated insistence on the exact sum reveals underlying anxiety beneath the jovial surface

social_state.rationale: He positions himself as a supplicant disguised as a gallant dance partner; genuine warmth toward Lubov even as he uses her

known_facts: The estate auction is happening today
Lubov is in emotional distress
He needs exactly 180 roubles urgently
hidden_secrets: The depth of his financial desperation
He likely cannot repay this loan either
false_beliefs: Something will always turn up to save him financially
epistemic_state.rationale: Pischin is aware of the crisis but his irrepressible optimism and survival instinct override any sense of inappropriateness

beat_states.defense_state: diminutization — calling it 'little roubles' and wrapping the request in gallantry to minimize its weight
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: genuinely affectionate toward Lubov yet shamelessly exploiting her emotional vulnerability to get money
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Could be read as genuinely trying to distract Lubov from her grief through the dance, with the money request being secondary

beat_states.desire_state: to perform his own sophistication and emotional depth while observing the party spectacle with detachment
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to escape provincial Russian life and return to Paris as a cultured, cosmopolitan figure
beat_states.obstacle: He is stuck at this provincial party among people he considers beneath him, his earlier request to Lubov was interrupted

affect_state.rationale: Mildly bored, self-amused, watching the chaos of the party with cool superiority; low emotional investment in the events around him

social_state.rationale: A servant who behaves as if he's above his station; cold toward the spectacle of Charlotta's antics and the general provincial merriment

known_facts: The estate is being auctioned today
Lubov sent him to find out the result
He wants Lubov to take him back to Paris
hidden_secrets: His contempt for nearly everyone at the party
His exploitation of Dunyasha
false_beliefs: He is genuinely sophisticated and above this world
epistemic_state.rationale: Yasha knows the practical situation but is fundamentally self-absorbed, viewing everything through the lens of his own desires

beat_states.defense_state: ironic detachment — singing a sentimental song as self-conscious performance rather than genuine feeling
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: Sings about soul's restlessness as if it's mere decoration, yet it genuinely reflects his frustrated desire to leave Russia
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: The song could be a genuine, unguarded moment of melancholy about his own trapped situation rather than pure affectation

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: [Stops to powder her face] The young mistress tells me to dance--there are a lot of gentlemen, but few ladies--and my head goes round when I dance, and my heart beats, Fiers Nicolaevitch; the Post-office clerk told me something just now which made me catch my breath. [The music grows faint.]

utterances.speaker: FIERS
utterances.text: What did he say to you?

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: He says, “You’re like a little flower.”

utterances.speaker: YASHA
utterances.text: [Yawns] Impolite.... [Exit.]

beat_states.desire_state: to share her romantic excitement and be validated in her attractiveness
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to elevate herself above her servant status through romantic attention and refinement
beat_states.obstacle: she is a maid performing duties (dancing because told to), not a lady of genuine social standing; her romantic interest (the Post-office clerk) is beneath Yasha

affect_state.rationale: Flushed and excited from dancing and the compliment, but dizzy and unsteady — her heart beating suggests both physical and emotional overwhelm. She is openly vulnerable, sharing intimate feelings with Fiers.

social_state.rationale: Low-status servant confiding warmly in the old butler; she performs a girlish intimacy with Fiers while hoping others (especially Yasha) overhear her desirability.

known_facts: The young mistress instructed her to dance
The Post-office clerk compared her to a flower
There are many gentlemen but few ladies at the party
hidden_secrets: She is more invested in Yasha's opinion than the clerk's
false_beliefs: She believes the clerk's compliment is extraordinary and meaningful
She believes performing refinement (powdering her face) makes her appear ladylike
epistemic_state.rationale: Dunyasha naively inflates a commonplace compliment into something breathtaking, revealing her inexperience and desire to be seen as romantically significant.

beat_states.defense_state: performing femininity (powdering face, breathlessness) to mask her low status
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she is physically overwhelmed by the dancing and socially out of place, yet she clings to a clerk's flattery as proof of her worth — while secretly preferring Yasha's attention
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: She may be deliberately performing this confession within Yasha's earshot to provoke his jealousy.

beat_states.desire_state: to listen and show paternal care for Dunyasha
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to maintain order, decency, and the old ways of the household
beat_states.obstacle: he is old, increasingly irrelevant, and the world around him is dissolving into chaos he cannot control

affect_state.rationale: Mildly warm but deeply fatigued; his gentle question masks an underlying helplessness about the larger catastrophe. He attends to small human exchanges because the big picture is beyond his power.

social_state.rationale: Once the senior servant, now diminished; still warm and grandfatherly toward Dunyasha, performing his accustomed role.

known_facts: The estate may be sold
The party is happening while the auction takes place
Dunyasha is excitable and impressionable
hidden_secrets: His deep fear about where he will go if the estate is sold
epistemic_state.rationale: Fiers knows the gravity of the situation but engages with Dunyasha's small world, perhaps as a distraction or simply out of ingrained duty.

beat_states.defense_state: habitual attentiveness — fulfilling his role as elder caretaker
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he engages gently with Dunyasha's triviality while the estate he has served his whole life may be sold that very night
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Fiers may be genuinely curious about Dunyasha's news, seeking any human connection amid his anxiety.

beat_states.desire_state: to assert his superiority over Dunyasha and the provincial world around him
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to escape Russia and return to Paris, confirming his cosmopolitan self-image
beat_states.obstacle: he is stuck at this provincial party surrounded by people he considers beneath him

affect_state.rationale: Bored, dismissive, and self-assured. The yawn is calculated disdain. He feels no vulnerability — or refuses to — and exits with total certainty of his own superiority.

social_state.rationale: He positions himself above both Dunyasha and the clerk by labeling the compliment 'impolite' (uncultured). Cold and withholding — his exit is a power move, withdrawing attention.

known_facts: Dunyasha is infatuated with him
The Post-office clerk flatters Dunyasha
He has been to Paris and considers himself refined
hidden_secrets: He has been intimate with Dunyasha and discarded her emotionally
His own origins are as humble as those he mocks
false_beliefs: He believes his time in Paris genuinely distinguishes him from everyone around him
epistemic_state.rationale: Yasha's single-word judgment ('Impolite') reveals he measures everything against a Parisian standard he only superficially absorbed. He knows Dunyasha's feelings but considers them beneath engagement.

beat_states.defense_state: contemptuous detachment — the yawn is a performed gesture of boredom and superiority
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he disdains Dunyasha's romantic excitement while having previously seduced her himself; his contempt masks his own vulgarity

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: Like a little flower. I’m such a delicate girl; I simply love words of tenderness.

utterances.speaker: FIERS
utterances.text: You’ll lose your head.

beat_states.desire_state: to be seen and admired as a refined, delicate woman worthy of romantic attention
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to transcend her servant class and live as a genteel lady deserving of love
beat_states.obstacle: her actual social position as a maid and Fiers' deflating presence as a reminder of reality

affect_state.rationale: She is in a pleasantly elevated, dreamy state, enjoying the party atmosphere and her self-image, but her certainty and control are moderate because her identity is performative and fragile

social_state.rationale: Low actual status as a servant, but she projects warmth and openness in her self-presentation, performing a higher status she doesn't possess

known_facts: She has been told to dance by Varya
Many gentlemen are asking her to dance
The post office clerk has been saying flattering things to her
hidden_secrets: Her romantic entanglement with Yasha
Her deeper insecurity about her class position
false_beliefs: That she is genuinely like a 'delicate flower' and a refined person
That romantic words from men reflect genuine regard for her
epistemic_state.rationale: Dunyasha has absorbed the manners of the gentry she serves and constructed a self-image based on borrowed refinement, mistaking flattery for real elevation

beat_states.defense_state: romanticization — wrapping herself in poetic self-description to avoid confronting her actual circumstances
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She is a servant at a party performing manual labor (powdering her face between dances ordered by her mistress) while narrating herself as a delicate flower deserving tenderness
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: She may be deliberately performing this naivety to charm Fiers or whoever is listening, rather than genuinely believing it

beat_states.desire_state: to warn Dunyasha and restore proper order and decorum among the servants
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve the old hierarchical order and protect the household from dissolution and foolishness
beat_states.obstacle: Dunyasha is lost in romantic self-absorption and unlikely to heed his warning; the old order he upholds is crumbling

affect_state.rationale: Disapproving and weary, highly certain of the danger he sees in her behavior, but with low control over the situation — he knows the young won't listen

social_state.rationale: As the eldest servant he carries moral authority but little actual power; his warmth is masked by gruffness and disapproval

known_facts: Dunyasha is getting swept up by flattery and romantic attention
Servants who forget their place come to ruin
The household is in crisis with the estate sale
hidden_secrets: His deep grief over the disintegration of the world he has served his entire life
false_beliefs: That maintaining old servant decorum can still protect people in this changing world
epistemic_state.rationale: Fiers sees clearly what Dunyasha cannot — that her romantic fantasies will lead to disaster — but his worldview is anchored in an obsolete social order

beat_states.defense_state: paternal authority — deploying his elder status to maintain emotional distance and moral superiority
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He cares enough to warn her, suggesting tenderness beneath his gruff dismissal, but his commitment to hierarchy prevents him from engaging with her as a full person
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: His warning could be read as pure cantankerous habit rather than genuine concern

utterances.speaker: EPIKHODOV
utterances.text: You, Avdotya Fedorovna, want to see me no more than if I was some insect. [Sighs] Oh, life!

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: What do you want?

utterances.speaker: EPIKHODOV
utterances.text: Undoubtedly, perhaps, you may be right. [Sighs] But, certainly, if you regard the matter from the aspect, then you, if I may say so, and you must excuse my candidness, have absolutely reduced me to a state of mind. I know my fate, every day something unfortunate happens to me, and I’ve grown used to it a long time ago, I even look at my fate with a smile. You gave me your word, and though I...

beat_states.desire_state: to get Epikhodov to leave her alone so she can return to the party and Yasha
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to elevate herself socially and romantically by attaching to someone she perceives as sophisticated (Yasha)
beat_states.obstacle: Epikhodov's persistent, guilt-laden appeals that she can't entirely ignore given their past understanding

affect_state.rationale: Mildly irritated rather than deeply affected. She feels some discomfort at his pleading but maintains enough control to deflect. Her certainty about wanting Yasha insulates her from Epikhodov's emotional pull.

social_state.rationale: She holds power in this exchange — she is the desired object, he the supplicant. Her curt 'What do you want?' is deliberately cold, asserting distance and superiority over him.

known_facts: She did give Epikhodov some form of romantic promise
She is now infatuated with Yasha
Epikhodov is emotionally volatile and accident-prone
Firs told her off for dancing
hidden_secrets: She knows she broke her word to Epikhodov and bears some responsibility for his suffering
false_beliefs: That Yasha's attention represents genuine romantic interest rather than casual exploitation
epistemic_state.rationale: She is aware of the moral imbalance but actively avoids reckoning with it, preferring to cast Epikhodov as merely tiresome rather than someone she wronged.

beat_states.defense_state: emotional detachment — treating him as an annoyance rather than a person she once encouraged
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she once gave him her word and likely knows she is being cruel, but suppresses guilt to protect her new romantic self-image
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: She may feel more guilt and conflict than she shows — her curtness could be overcompensation for genuine unease about how she's treated him.

beat_states.desire_state: to make Dunyasha acknowledge his suffering and recommit to her promise of romantic attachment
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to be recognized as worthy and significant despite his chronic misfortune
beat_states.obstacle: Dunyasha's indifference and her romantic fixation on Yasha; his own inability to articulate himself coherently

affect_state.rationale: Deeply wounded by Dunyasha's dismissal, he oscillates between self-pity and attempted stoicism. His sighs and meandering speech reveal high vulnerability and low control over both the situation and his own emotions.

social_state.rationale: He positions himself as beneath her — comparing himself to an insect — performing abjection to elicit sympathy. There is residual warmth in his devotion, but it reads as desperate rather than generous.

known_facts: Dunyasha gave him her word at some point
Dunyasha now avoids and dismisses him
He is chronically unlucky and aware of it
She is interested in Yasha
hidden_secrets: He may be contemplating drastic action (hints at suicide throughout the play)
false_beliefs: That verbose philosophical framing of his pain will move Dunyasha
That invoking her broken promise will restore the relationship
epistemic_state.rationale: He knows the facts of his rejection but cannot accept them, clinging to the earlier promise as evidence he still has a claim on her affections.

beat_states.defense_state: intellectualization — wrapping raw heartbreak in convoluted philosophical language to maintain dignity
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: claims to accept his fate with a smile while clearly begging for her attention and unable to let go
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: He may be performing misery more than feeling it — using the role of 'tragic sufferer' as an identity that gives him significance rather than genuinely seeking reconciliation.

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: Please, we’ll talk later on, but leave me alone now. I’m meditating now. [Plays with her fan.]

utterances.speaker: EPIKHODOV
utterances.text: Every day something unfortunate happens to me, and I, if I may so express myself, only smile, and even laugh.

beat_states.desire_state: to get rid of Epikhodov so she can focus on Yasha and her fantasy of romantic refinement
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to transcend her servant status and live a life of romantic delicacy and refinement
beat_states.obstacle: Epikhodov's persistent, clinging presence and emotional demands

affect_state.rationale: She feels in control of this interaction — Epikhodov is beneath her emotional attention. Mild irritation but mostly confident in her dismissal.

social_state.rationale: She is asserting superiority over Epikhodov by performing refined indifference. Cold and dismissive, treating him as a nuisance.

known_facts: Epikhodov is devoted to her
Yasha is the one she desires
She is a servant playing at being a lady
hidden_secrets: She has been intimate with Yasha and is emotionally consumed by him
false_beliefs: Her fan-waving meditation performance reads as genuinely refined rather than absurdly pretentious
epistemic_state.rationale: Dunyasha has absorbed the manners of her mistress without self-awareness about how comically she performs them.

beat_states.defense_state: performing aristocratic aloofness — 'meditating' with a fan to assert a class distance that doesn't exist
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She claims to be meditating and needing solitude, but she is actually just posturing and waiting for Yasha

beat_states.desire_state: to make Dunyasha acknowledge his suffering and feel something — guilt, pity, anything — for him
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to be recognized as a person of worth and feeling despite the universe's constant humiliations
beat_states.obstacle: Dunyasha's total indifference and active dismissal of him

affect_state.rationale: Deeply wounded by her dismissal. He has no power in this exchange. His attempt at philosophical composure barely conceals acute emotional pain and helplessness.

social_state.rationale: He is low-status, begging for crumbs of attention. His warmth is one-directional — earnest feeling met with nothing.

known_facts: Dunyasha does not want him
Something unfortunate happens to him every day
He is being dismissed
hidden_secrets: He likely suspects Yasha is his rival but cannot fully confront it
false_beliefs: His literary, philosophical manner of speaking makes him impressive or sympathetic rather than ridiculous
epistemic_state.rationale: Epikhodov is trapped in a self-narration that he believes elevates him but actually distances others further.

beat_states.defense_state: Intellectualized stoicism — reframing his misery as philosophical endurance ('I only smile, and even laugh') to mask devastation
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He claims to laugh at his misfortunes, but every word bleeds with self-pity and desperation for sympathy
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: There may be a veiled threat underneath the stoic mask — 'I only smile and laugh' could contain a darker implication about what he might do when he stops laughing

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: Haven’t you gone yet, Simeon? You really have no respect for anybody. [To DUNYASHA] You go away, Dunyasha. [To EPIKHODOV] You play billiards and break a cue, and walk about the drawing-room as if you were a visitor!

utterances.speaker: EPIKHODOV
utterances.text: You cannot, if I may say so, call me to order.

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: I’m not calling you to order, I’m only telling you. You just walk about from place to place and never do your work. Goodness only knows why we keep a clerk.

utterances.speaker: EPIKHODOV
utterances.text: [Offended] Whether I work, or walk about, or eat, or play billiards, is only a matter to be settled by people of understanding and my elders.

beat_states.desire_state: To defend his dignity against Varya's humiliation and assert that he is not beneath consideration
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To be recognized as a person of worth and sensitivity in a world that treats him as a comic nuisance
beat_states.obstacle: Varya's social authority over him and her open contempt, which he cannot directly challenge without risking his position

affect_state.rationale: Offended and wounded but trying to maintain a front of philosophical detachment. His arousal is moderate — he's activated but containing it. Low control and certainty reflect his fundamental position as someone perpetually acted upon by forces he can't manage.

social_state.rationale: Subordinate to Varya in the household hierarchy and being publicly reprimanded. His warmth is cool — he's defensive and bristling, withdrawing into formality rather than engaging with genuine hostility or connection.

known_facts: Varya is reprimanding him publicly
He did break a billiard cue
He has been walking about the drawing room
Dunyasha has been avoiding his romantic advances
hidden_secrets: His deep romantic despair over Dunyasha's rejection
His veiled references to suicide that others don't take seriously
false_beliefs: That his formal, educated manner of speaking elevates him above the other servants
That invoking 'people of understanding' will win the argument
epistemic_state.rationale: Epikhodov has accurate awareness of his situation but consistently misreads how others perceive him, believing his philosophical posturing commands respect rather than ridicule.

beat_states.defense_state: Intellectualization — retreating into formal, pseudo-philosophical language to maintain composure and assert superiority of mind over her authority of position
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He claims the matter should be 'settled by people of understanding and my elders' — simultaneously deferring to authority and implying Varya is neither understanding nor his legitimate elder
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Epikhodov's pushback could be read less as genuine challenge and more as reflexive wounded pride — a man so accustomed to humiliation that resistance is automatic rather than strategic.

beat_states.desire_state: To assert authority over the household servants and restore order during the party
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To maintain control over the estate and household as a way of proving her worth and securing her place in the family
beat_states.obstacle: Epikhodov refuses to submit to her authority and talks back, undermining her managerial role

affect_state.rationale: Frustrated and agitated by the disorder around her. She presents certainty in her reprimands but her control is slipping — Epikhodov pushes back, and the household is in chaos during a party happening while the estate is being auctioned.

social_state.rationale: She occupies a managerial position above Epikhodov but her status is ambiguous — she's an adopted daughter managing servants, not truly the mistress. Her warmth is very low; she's cold, dismissive, and contemptuous toward both Dunyasha and Epikhodov.

known_facts: Epikhodov broke a billiard cue
Epikhodov is wandering the drawing room instead of working
Dunyasha is flirting instead of attending to duties
The estate auction is happening today
hidden_secrets: Her deep anxiety about what will happen to her if the estate is sold
Her unresolved feelings about Lopakhin and the stalled proposal
false_beliefs: That enforcing household discipline can hold things together
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya sees the surface disorder clearly but her fixation on it is a displacement from the larger catastrophe she cannot control.

beat_states.defense_state: Channeling her deeper anxieties about the estate's fate into micromanaging servants
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She claims she's 'not calling him to order' while doing exactly that — her need for control wars with her awareness that her authority is precarious and possibly illegitimate

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: You dare to talk to me like that! [Furious] You dare? You mean that I know nothing? Get out of here! This minute!

utterances.speaker: EPIKHODOV
utterances.text: [Nervous] I must ask you to express yourself more delicately.

beat_states.desire_state: To be treated with dignity and to deflect Varya's attack without further escalation
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To be recognized as a person of consequence and refinement despite his lowly position and chronic bad luck
beat_states.obstacle: Varya's explosive fury and her superior social position, which make any pushback dangerous

affect_state.rationale: Marked as 'nervous' in the stage direction. He is humiliated and frightened but trying to maintain composure. High vulnerability — he is outmatched and knows it. Low control and certainty about how to navigate this.

social_state.rationale: He is socially inferior and being reminded of it violently. His formal language is a desperate bid to elevate his status through rhetoric. Slight coldness — wounded pride prevents warmth.

known_facts: Varya has authority over him in the household
He has overstayed and been told to leave
Dunyasha has rejected his attentions
hidden_secrets: His deep romantic despair over Dunyasha
His contemplation of suicide or dramatic gestures as expressions of his suffering
false_beliefs: That speaking in an elevated register actually commands respect from others
That he is a cultured, educated man wrongly placed in lowly circumstances
epistemic_state.rationale: Epikhodov consistently misreads social situations and overestimates the effectiveness of his literary self-presentation.

beat_states.defense_state: Retreating into formal, elevated diction ('express yourself more delicately') as a shield — intellectualization and pedantic language as armor against humiliation
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He wants to assert his dignity but is fundamentally nervous and intimidated; his request for 'delicacy' is itself an act of defiance wrapped in servility
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: There may be a thread of passive-aggressive provocation in his request — he knows telling an enraged person to be 'more delicate' will infuriate them further, suggesting a masochistic streak

beat_states.desire_state: To expel Epikhodov immediately and assert her absolute authority in the household
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To maintain order and control in a world that is slipping away from her — the estate, her role, her future
beat_states.obstacle: Epikhodov's continued presence and his implication that she lacks knowledge or authority

affect_state.rationale: White-hot fury — she is described as furious. High arousal, negative valence. She feels certain of her right to command but her volcanic reaction betrays deep vulnerability. The repetition of 'You dare' shows she is barely containing herself.

social_state.rationale: She is pulling rank as household manager, asserting hierarchical dominance over a clerk/worker. Zero warmth — pure aggression and contempt.

known_facts: Epikhodov has been lingering and causing disruption
He has not left as instructed
The auction is happening tonight and no one has heard results
hidden_secrets: Her anguish over Lopakhin's failure to propose
Her deeper fear that she is powerless to stop the family's decline
false_beliefs: That exerting control over servants can restore her sense of order and meaning
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya displaces her existential anxiety onto the manageable target of Epikhodov's insubordination.

beat_states.defense_state: Rage as a defense against feelings of powerlessness and irrelevance — she channels her deeper anxieties about losing the estate and Lopakhin into fury at a subordinate
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She is furious about being told she 'knows nothing,' which suggests she deeply fears this is true — that she is losing grip on everything and is fundamentally inadequate to the situation

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: [Beside herself] Get out this minute. Get out! [He goes to the door, she follows] Two-and-twenty troubles! I don’t want any sign of you here! I don’t want to see anything of you! [EPIKHODOV has gone out; his voice can be heard outside: “I’ll make a complaint against you.”] What, coming back? [Snatches up the stick left by FIERS by the door] Go... go... go, I’ll show you.... Are you going? Are you going? Well, then take that. [She hits out as LOPAKHIN enters.]

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: Much obliged.

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: [Angry but amused] I’m sorry.

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: Never mind. I thank you for my pleasant reception.

beat_states.desire_state: To defuse the awkward situation with humor while maintaining his dignity
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To rise above his peasant origins and prove his worth — the complicated relationship with Varya and the Ranevsky family is central to this
beat_states.obstacle: He has just been struck, creating an awkward charged moment with the woman everyone expects him to marry — a proposal he keeps avoiding

affect_state.rationale: He is surprised but recovers quickly. His wry humor gives him the upper hand. Low negative valence from the physical blow, offset by amusement. He maintains more control than Varya in this exchange, but the underlying tension about their unresolved relationship creates some vulnerability.

social_state.rationale: The accident paradoxically elevates his status — he is the gracious, witty victim while Varya is the flustered aggressor. His 'pleasant reception' line is warm enough to be charming but ironic enough to maintain distance.

known_facts: Varya was chasing Epikhodov and struck him by accident
He has just returned from the auction where the estate was sold
Everyone expects him to propose to Varya
hidden_secrets: He has bought the cherry orchard at auction
He cannot bring himself to propose to Varya despite everyone's expectations
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin carries the enormous secret of having purchased the estate. This accidental blow and comic exchange is a brief delay before the devastating revelation. His lightness here is partly the giddiness of a man sitting on explosive news.

beat_states.defense_state: Ironic understatement — 'much obliged' transforms a humiliating blow into a comedic exchange, maintaining emotional distance
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He uses humor to create intimacy and distance simultaneously — the moment could be a doorway to genuine connection with Varya, but his irony keeps her at arm's length
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lopakhin's ironic response could mask genuine hurt or irritation — the blow from Varya might unconsciously confirm his sense that he doesn't truly belong in this household and will never be accepted as an equal

beat_states.desire_state: To expel Epikhodov completely and assert her authority over the household
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To maintain order and control in a world that is slipping away from her, and to secure her place through marriage to Lopakhin
beat_states.obstacle: She has accidentally struck Lopakhin — the very man she hopes to marry — in a moment of uncontrolled rage, catastrophically undermining herself

affect_state.rationale: She was at peak fury, then the accidental strike on Lopakhin plunges her into humiliation. Her arousal remains extremely high but now mixed with shame. She has lost complete control of the situation. The 'angry but amused' stage direction masks deep embarrassment and vulnerability — she has just physically hit the man she wants to propose to her.

social_state.rationale: Her status collapses from domineering authority figure to someone apologizing for a ridiculous accident. She tries to project warmth through her apology and shared laughter, but the power dynamic has shifted entirely to Lopakhin.

known_facts: She has just struck Lopakhin with a stick meant for Epikhodov
Lopakhin has entered at the worst possible moment
Everyone expects Lopakhin to propose to her
She was in an undignified rage
hidden_secrets: She longs for Lopakhin to propose and fears this incident has further damaged her chances
Her rage at Epikhodov is partly displaced anxiety about the estate sale and her uncertain future
false_beliefs: That laughing it off can neutralize the damage of this moment
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya is painfully aware of the social catastrophe of the moment but cannot acknowledge its full significance — that she has physically assaulted the man she needs to see her as a desirable partner.

beat_states.defense_state: Converts mortification into humor — laughing at herself to minimize the damage of the moment
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She has been performing dominance and fury, but the accidental blow to Lopakhin instantly collapses her into vulnerability and shame — she is both furious and desperate to repair
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: The 'angry but amused' direction could indicate genuine relief — the absurdity of the situation breaks her tension and she momentarily feels closer to Lopakhin through shared comedy

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: It isn’t worth any thanks. [Walks away, then looks back and asks gently] I didn’t hurt you, did I?

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: No, not at all. There’ll be an enormous bump, that’s all.

beat_states.desire_state: to defuse the awkwardness and reassure Varya while maintaining his composure
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to prove his worth and rise above his peasant origins, which includes an ambivalent relationship with the possibility of marrying into this family
beat_states.obstacle: his own inability to engage emotionally with Varya's tenderness — he deflects into humor rather than meeting her vulnerability
beat_states.tactic_state: reassure — minimizing the injury with self-deprecating humor to ease her guilt

affect_state.rationale: He's been physically struck but is not angry. There's a slight warmth in his response, but he keeps emotional distance. He's slightly bemused and faintly touched by her concern.

social_state.rationale: He holds a slight status advantage — he's the injured party being solicitous toward — and his humor is warm but distancing, maintaining a genial but non-committal rapport.

known_facts: Varya struck him accidentally while aiming at Yepikhodov
Varya's concern for him is genuine and possibly romantic
Everyone expects him to propose to Varya
hidden_secrets: He may not truly intend to propose to Varya despite everyone's expectations
He has just bought the cherry orchard at auction — news he has not yet shared
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin is aware of the social expectation around their relationship and of Varya's feelings but remains evasive about his own intentions.

beat_states.defense_state: deflection through humor ('enormous bump, that's all') prevents the moment from becoming emotionally intimate
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He is physically hurt and could use this moment to draw closer to Varya, yet he instinctively retreats into joking, revealing his deep discomfort with emotional intimacy — especially with her
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lopakhin's humor could be read as genuinely warm and an attempt to comfort rather than deflect, suggesting he does care but simply lacks the emotional vocabulary to go further.

beat_states.desire_state: to repair the damage of accidentally hitting Lopakhin and to reconnect with him tenderly
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to secure love, stability, and a proposal from Lopakhin while maintaining her moral authority in the household
beat_states.obstacle: her own embarrassment at having just struck the man she loves, and her inability to express her feelings directly

affect_state.rationale: She has just accidentally hurt the man she hopes will propose to her. Her fury has collapsed into mortification and tender concern. She is exposed and uncertain about how he perceives her.

social_state.rationale: She has lost the high-status position of the raging household manager and is now in a lower position, gently soliciting Lopakhin's well-being, showing rare warmth and vulnerability.

known_facts: She accidentally struck Lopakhin with a stick meant for Yepikhodov
She was in a rage moments ago and now must face the consequences
Lopakhin has not yet proposed to her despite widespread expectation
hidden_secrets: She is deeply in love with Lopakhin and terrified she has ruined any chance with him
Her rage at Yepikhodov was partly fueled by frustration over her unresolved relationship with Lopakhin
false_beliefs: That walking away casually and then looking back will disguise the depth of her concern
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya knows she hit Lopakhin and is anxious about physical and emotional damage. She tries to appear nonchalant but her gentle return reveals everything.

beat_states.defense_state: deflection through minimizing ('it isn't worth any thanks') to mask the intensity of her concern
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: moments ago she was in a fury of righteous anger and dominance; now she is soft, vulnerable, and desperate not to have alienated Lopakhin — the whiplash reveals how much she cares beneath her disciplinarian exterior
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Varya's gentleness could be purely about physical concern with no romantic subtext, but the stage history and context strongly suggest this is a moment of romantic vulnerability.

utterances.speaker: VOICES FROM THE DRAWING-ROOM
utterances.text: Lopakhin’s returned! Ermolai Alexeyevitch!

utterances.speaker: PISCHIN
utterances.text: Now we’ll see what there is to see and hear what there is to hear... [Kisses LOPAKHIN] You smell of cognac, my dear, my soul. And we’re all having a good time.

beat_states.desire_state: to greet Lopakhin warmly and learn the outcome of the auction
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to attach himself to whoever has money and power so he can secure his own financial survival
beat_states.obstacle: he doesn't yet know the auction results and anxiety lurks beneath his jovial exterior

affect_state.rationale: Outwardly buoyant and celebratory, but underneath uncertain about what Lopakhin's return means; the cognac comment and kiss suggest performative warmth covering anxiety about the auction outcome

social_state.rationale: Pischin positions himself below Lopakhin, kissing him and using endearments ('my dear, my soul'), signaling deference to the man who may now hold enormous power; his warmth is genuine but also strategic

known_facts: Lopakhin has returned from the auction
Lopakhin smells of cognac suggesting he has been drinking
The party has been going on during the auction
The estate was being sold today
hidden_secrets: His own debts are crushing and he needs to borrow from whoever emerges wealthy
false_beliefs: The evening's merriment suggests things may have gone well
epistemic_state.rationale: Pischin knows Lopakhin attended the auction but does not yet know the result; he reads the cognac as a possible sign of celebration and rushes to position himself favorably

beat_states.defense_state: uses effusive warmth and physical affection to mask his desperate financial dependency
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: performs carefree merriment while desperately needing to know if the estate sale affects his own debts and borrowing prospects
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Pischin may be genuinely oblivious to the gravity of the moment, simply enjoying the party and greeting an old friend without strategic calculation

beat_states.desire_state: to announce Lopakhin's arrival and learn the auction results
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: the household and guests collectively need to know the fate of the cherry orchard
beat_states.obstacle: the answer has not yet been spoken; the moment is charged with suspense

affect_state.rationale: High arousal and excitement at Lopakhin's return, but deep uncertainty and lack of control over the outcome; the chorus-like announcement carries both hope and dread

social_state.rationale: The collective voices represent the household looking to Lopakhin as the bearer of decisive news, placing him in a position of authority

known_facts: Lopakhin went to the auction
He has now returned
The cherry orchard was up for sale
epistemic_state.rationale: The crowd knows only that Lopakhin has returned; the auction result remains unknown to them

beat_states.defense_state: communal excitement diffuses individual dread
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: the excited announcement masks collective terror about what news Lopakhin brings
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: The voices may represent a range of emotions from genuine excitement to anxious dread, not a unified sentiment

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: Is that you, Ermolai Alexeyevitch? Why were you so long? Where’s Leonid?

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: Leonid Andreyevitch came back with me, he’s coming....

beat_states.desire_state: to find the right moment and way to announce that he has bought the cherry orchard
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to triumph over his peasant origins by acquiring the estate where his ancestors were serfs
beat_states.obstacle: he is facing the woman he respects and cares for, knowing his news will devastate her; he must manage the contradiction between his triumph and her loss

affect_state.rationale: He knows the full truth and is elated at his purchase but emotionally overwhelmed and struggling to contain himself; his control is low because the enormity of what he's done is still sinking in

social_state.rationale: He has just become the new owner of the estate, fundamentally altering the power dynamic, but his warmth is muted by guilt and the difficulty of the moment

known_facts: He bought the cherry orchard at auction
He outbid Deriganov
Leonid is coming behind him
Lubov does not yet know the outcome
hidden_secrets: He is the new owner of the cherry orchard — the central secret of this moment
epistemic_state.rationale: He holds all the information and is strategically withholding it, buying time before the devastating announcement

beat_states.defense_state: delaying the revelation by answering only the surface question about Leonid's whereabouts
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he is bursting with the triumphant news of his purchase but simultaneously dreads delivering the blow to Lubov, whom he genuinely loves and respects
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: He may not be deliberately withholding but simply hasn't found his footing emotionally to speak the truth yet — the delay is psychological paralysis rather than strategy

beat_states.desire_state: to learn immediately what happened at the auction — whether the cherry orchard was sold and to whom
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve the cherry orchard and the life/identity it represents
beat_states.obstacle: she has been waiting in agonizing suspense and Lopakhin has not yet delivered the news

affect_state.rationale: Extreme anxiety beneath a veneer of hostess-like normalcy; she doesn't know the outcome yet and her entire world depends on it

social_state.rationale: She addresses Lopakhin with familiarity and politeness befitting her role as landowner/hostess, but Lopakhin's economic power is shifting the dynamic

known_facts: The auction was today
Lopakhin and Leonid went to the auction
They have returned very late
Leonid is not yet in the room
hidden_secrets: She suspects the worst but cannot bring herself to ask directly
false_beliefs: She may still hold out hope that Leonid secured the estate or that a miracle occurred
epistemic_state.rationale: She knows only that they're back late, which itself is an ominous sign, but she has no confirmed information about the sale
