known_facts: He told Egor about Fiers this morning
Fiers was supposed to go to the hospital
hidden_secrets: He likely did not follow through to confirm Fiers was actually sent
He does not genuinely care about Fiers's fate
false_beliefs: He assumes telling Egor is equivalent to the task being completed
epistemic_state.rationale: Yasha operates on the assumption that passing a message equals completing a responsibility. His 'I suppose they've sent him' reveals he has no actual knowledge of the outcome.

beat_states.defense_state: indignation — performing offense to shut down further questioning and avoid accountability
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He claims to have handled Fiers's transfer but likely did so carelessly or not at all; his defensiveness masks negligence
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: It's possible Yasha did earnestly try to arrange Fiers's transfer and his offense is genuine hurt at being distrusted, but the text's 'I suppose' strongly suggests negligence.

utterances.speaker: EPIKHODOV
utterances.text: The aged Fiers, in my conclusive opinion, isn’t worth mending; his forefathers had better have him. I only envy him. [Puts a trunk on a hat-box and squashes it] Well, of course. I thought so!

utterances.speaker: YASHA
utterances.text: [Grinning] Two-and-twenty troubles.

beat_states.desire_state: to appear philosophically authoritative and competent while handling the packing
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to be taken seriously as an intelligent, capable person despite constant humiliations
beat_states.obstacle: his own clumsiness continually undermines his pretensions to competence and wisdom

affect_state.rationale: He tries to project certainty and worldly wisdom but the physical mishap immediately deflates him; his resigned 'I thought so' reveals a man accustomed to failure, caught between self-importance and self-pity

social_state.rationale: Low status despite aspirations; his dismissal of the elderly Fiers as not worth saving is cold and self-serving, and Yasha's mockery confirms his low standing among servants

known_facts: Fiers is very old and ill
The estate is being packed up and everyone is leaving
He has been tasked with packing duties
hidden_secrets: He envies Fiers partly because even death would be a resolution to his own endless misfortunes
false_beliefs: He believes his philosophical pronouncements make him sound educated and impressive
epistemic_state.rationale: Epikhodov has a surface grasp of the situation but filters everything through his pretentious self-regard; his 'envy' of Fiers may be more genuine than he lets on

beat_states.defense_state: intellectualization — wraps callous dismissal of Fiers in grandiose pseudo-philosophical language to mask his own insignificance
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: claims superiority of judgment while physically demonstrating incompetence by crushing the hat-box
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: The envy of Fiers could be read as a genuine death-wish or existential despair rather than mere rhetorical posturing

beat_states.desire_state: to enjoy his superiority over Epikhodov by mocking him
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to escape provincial Russian life and return to Paris, affirming his sophistication over those around him
beat_states.obstacle: he is still stuck among these provincial servants during the tedious departure

affect_state.rationale: Genuinely amused at Epikhodov's predictable clumsiness; feels in control of the social dynamic between them; low vulnerability because he distances himself from any emotional stakes

social_state.rationale: Holds himself above Epikhodov with sneering amusement; the nickname 'Two-and-twenty troubles' is a deliberate status-reducing label, delivered with cold pleasure

known_facts: Epikhodov is notoriously clumsy and accident-prone
The packing is underway and departure is imminent
Fiers has supposedly been sent to the hospital
hidden_secrets: He likely has not confirmed or cared whether Fiers was actually sent to the hospital
false_beliefs: He believes his own worldliness places him categorically above the other servants
epistemic_state.rationale: Yasha is observant about others' weaknesses but willfully negligent about responsibilities like Fiers' welfare; his sophistication is a self-flattering construct

beat_states.defense_state: contemptuous detachment — maintains emotional distance from the sadness of departure by turning everything into a joke
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: his casual cruelty toward both Fiers (indifference) and Epikhodov (mockery) masks his own anxiety about whether he will actually escape this world

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: [Behind the door] Has Fiers been taken away to the hospital?

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: Yes.

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: Why didn’t they take the letter to the doctor?

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: It’ll have to be sent after him. [Exit.]

beat_states.desire_state: to reassure Varya and move on quickly from these logistical concerns
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to leave the old life behind and embrace the future with hope and forward motion
beat_states.obstacle: the unresolved detail of the unsent letter forces her to acknowledge that things haven't been handled properly

affect_state.rationale: Anya is relatively calm and matter-of-fact; she's emotionally detaching from the household but the forgotten letter creates a small pang of guilt or discomfort

social_state.rationale: Responding dutifully to her older sister's questions but not lingering — her exit signals she's already emotionally moving away from this caretaking dynamic

known_facts: Fiers has been sent to the hospital
The letter to the doctor was not sent with him
The letter will need to be sent separately
false_beliefs: she assumes the letter being sent after him will be sufficient and that Fiers will be properly cared for
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya knows the facts but doesn't grasp the full weight of what a forgotten letter might mean for an old man in a hospital — her youth shields her from fully understanding the stakes

beat_states.defense_state: minimization — treating the forgotten letter as a minor issue that can be easily fixed rather than a symptom of the household's collapse
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she wants to be free of the old world's burdens but still feels the pull of responsibility when directly questioned by Varya
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Anya's quick exit might also signal avoidance — she doesn't want to be drawn into Varya's world of anxious caretaking because it threatens her optimistic vision of the future

beat_states.desire_state: to confirm that proper care has been arranged for Fiers before the household disperses
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to maintain order and fulfill her duty as the responsible caretaker of the household even as everything falls apart
beat_states.obstacle: she is behind a door, physically separated from the action, unable to directly manage the situation herself

affect_state.rationale: Varya is anxious and worried; her follow-up question about the letter reveals she suspects things are being mishandled, which feeds her sense of losing grip on the household she's managed for years

social_state.rationale: Still operating in her managerial role, asserting her authority by checking on tasks, but her concern for Fiers shows genuine warmth and care

known_facts: Fiers is old and ill
Fiers was supposed to be sent to the hospital
A letter to the doctor was supposed to accompany him
hidden_secrets: her deep fear that no one else will take care of these details and people will be abandoned
false_beliefs: she may believe that if she keeps checking, the household will function properly until the end
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya's second question reveals she already suspected the letter wasn't sent — she knows from experience that tasks fall through the cracks in this household

beat_states.defense_state: displacement — channeling her grief and anxiety about losing the estate into micro-managing practical logistics
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she needs to feel in control of domestic details precisely because she has lost control of everything that matters to her
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Varya's questions could also be a way of indirectly criticizing the others' negligence, expressing resentment at always being the one who has to think of everything

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: [In the next room] Where’s Yasha? Tell him his mother’s come and wants to say good-bye to him.

utterances.speaker: YASHA
utterances.text: [Waving his hand] She’ll make me lose all patience! [DUNYASHA has meanwhile been bustling round the luggage; now that YASHA is left alone, she goes up to him.]

beat_states.desire_state: to locate Yasha and ensure he sees his mother before departure
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to maintain order and fulfill her caretaking duties until the very end
beat_states.obstacle: Yasha is avoiding responsibility and his mother; the household is in chaos

affect_state.rationale: Varya is stressed and task-oriented, channeling anxiety into practical duties. Her tone is brisk and commanding but underneath she is losing control of the situation and her own future.

social_state.rationale: She still occupies the role of household manager but her authority is waning. Her warmth is limited — she is issuing directives, though the act of relaying the mother's wish carries some residual care.

known_facts: Yasha's mother has come to say goodbye
Yasha is somewhere in the house
The household is being packed up and everyone is leaving
hidden_secrets: Her deep pain over Lopakhin's failure to propose
false_beliefs: That keeping busy with duties will hold her together emotionally
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya knows the practical logistics but uses them to avoid confronting her own emotional devastation.

beat_states.defense_state: displacing her own grief about leaving into managing others' affairs
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She is managing everyone else's goodbyes while her own emotional farewell (to the house, to Lopakhin) remains unresolved

beat_states.desire_state: to avoid his mother and any sentimental obligation that would tie him to his provincial roots
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to escape Russia entirely and live a sophisticated life in Paris, shedding his peasant origins
beat_states.obstacle: His mother has arrived and Varya is broadcasting her presence, forcing a confrontation with the world he wants to leave behind

affect_state.rationale: Genuine irritation and impatience dominate. The waving hand is a gesture of exasperated dismissal. He feels threatened by sentiment but masks vulnerability with contempt.

social_state.rationale: Yasha postures as above his station but is fundamentally a servant. His warmth is aggressively negative — he refuses emotional connection with his own mother, displaying cold self-centeredness.

known_facts: His mother has come to say goodbye
He is leaving for Paris with Ranevskaya
Dunyasha is nearby and attached to him
hidden_secrets: He has been exploiting Dunyasha's affections with no intention of commitment
He is ashamed of his peasant mother
false_beliefs: That rejecting his mother proves his sophistication rather than his shallowness
epistemic_state.rationale: Yasha is fully aware of the situation but chooses willful emotional avoidance. His self-concept as a Parisian sophisticate is a constructed fiction he defends fiercely.

beat_states.defense_state: contemptuous dismissal — treating familial bonds as irritating inconveniences to maintain his self-image as cosmopolitan and above sentiment
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He wants to be seen as refined and above provincial feeling, yet the very vehemence of his irritation reveals how much his origins still have a hold on him
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: A small possibility that his irritation masks genuine pain at leaving his mother, but Chekhov's stage direction ('waving his hand') strongly suggests dismissive contempt rather than suppressed grief.

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: If you only looked at me once, Yasha. You’re going away, leaving me behind.

utterances.speaker: YASHA
utterances.text: What’s the use of crying? [Drinks champagne] In six days I’ll be again in Paris. To-morrow we get into the express and off we go. I can hardly believe it. Vive la France! It doesn’t suit me here, I can’t live here... it’s no good. Well, I’ve seen the uncivilized world; I have had enough of it. [Drinks champagne] What do you want to cry for? You behave yourself properly, and then you won’t cry.

utterances.speaker: DUNYASHA
utterances.text: [Looks in a small mirror and powders her face] Send me a letter from Paris. You know I loved you, Yasha, so much! I’m a sensitive creature, Yasha.

beat_states.desire_state: to make Yasha acknowledge their love and promise to maintain connection after he leaves
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to be seen as worthy of love and elevated above her station through romantic attachment
beat_states.obstacle: Yasha is indifferent to her feelings and entirely focused on his own departure

affect_state.rationale: She is deeply hurt and desperate, openly exposing her need for Yasha. Her crying and pleading show high arousal and very low control. She identifies as 'sensitive' — both genuine and a performance of femininity she's learned from novels.

social_state.rationale: She is in a supplicant position, begging for attention from someone who considers himself above her. She radiates warmth and emotional openness that goes entirely unreciprocated.

known_facts: Yasha is leaving for Paris tomorrow
Yasha has been physically intimate with her
Yasha does not reciprocate her depth of feeling
hidden_secrets: She may sense this is truly the end but cannot admit it to herself
false_beliefs: That calling herself a 'sensitive creature' will move Yasha to tenderness
That a letter from Paris would signify continued connection
epistemic_state.rationale: Dunyasha has enough evidence to know Yasha doesn't love her, but her romantic self-image prevents her from accepting this fully.

beat_states.defense_state: self-beautifying — powdering her face to reassert attractiveness and composure after emotional exposure
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she knows Yasha doesn't care yet she continues to perform devotion, oscillating between genuine grief and theatrical self-presentation
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: She may be performing grief partly for herself — rehearsing the role of the abandoned lover she's read about in novels, finding some pleasure in the melodrama even amid real pain.

beat_states.desire_state: to enjoy his last moments here drinking champagne and savor the anticipation of returning to Paris without emotional encumbrance
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to escape provincial Russian life permanently and live as a cosmopolitan man in Paris
beat_states.obstacle: Dunyasha's emotional display is an unwanted intrusion on his self-congratulatory mood

affect_state.rationale: He is in high spirits about leaving, certain of his trajectory. The champagne fuels his expansiveness. He feels entirely in control of the interaction and shows zero vulnerability — Dunyasha's feelings are an inconvenience, not a wound.

social_state.rationale: He positions himself far above Dunyasha — and above Russia itself. His condescending advice ('behave yourself properly') is cold and paternalistic. No genuine warmth, only patronizing tolerance.

known_facts: He is leaving for Paris tomorrow via express train
Dunyasha loves him
His mother has come to say goodbye and he has avoided her
hidden_secrets: His cosmopolitan identity is a shallow performance built on proximity to employers rather than genuine cultivation
He has avoided saying goodbye to his own mother
false_beliefs: That Paris represents his true home and identity
That his disdain for Russia marks him as sophisticated rather than self-hating
epistemic_state.rationale: Yasha has constructed an identity around being 'Parisian' that requires constant denigration of his origins. He cannot see that he is performing a borrowed identity.

beat_states.defense_state: contemptuous detachment — reframing the entire situation as beneath him by invoking Paris and 'civilization' to create emotional distance
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He is himself a servant from this 'uncivilized world' he scorns, performing sophistication while drinking champagne that isn't his
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: A small reading exists that his repeated champagne-drinking masks some suppressed anxiety about the future — but the text gives very little evidence for genuine feeling beneath his posturing.

utterances.speaker: YASHA
utterances.text: Somebody’s coming. [He bustles around the luggage, singing softly. Enter LUBOV ANDREYEVNA, GAEV, ANYA, and CHARLOTTA IVANOVNA.]

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: We’d better be off. There’s no time left. [Looks at YASHA] Somebody smells of herring!

beat_states.desire_state: to hurry the departure and avoid the pain of lingering in the house they've lost
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve his dignity and sense of aristocratic identity despite total material and emotional loss
beat_states.obstacle: The overwhelming grief of leaving the cherry orchard; his inability to face the moment with real composure

affect_state.rationale: Deeply sad and anxious about the departure; the snide remark about herring betrays agitation and loss of composure; underneath the bluster is profound vulnerability about losing everything

social_state.rationale: Asserts remnant aristocratic superiority by sneering at Yasha's smell — a desperate grasp at class distinction when his actual material status has collapsed

known_facts: The estate is sold
They must leave immediately
Their old life is over
hidden_secrets: The depth of his grief and shame at having lost the family estate
His awareness of his own uselessness in preventing the loss
false_beliefs: That maintaining aristocratic fastidiousness somehow preserves his dignity
epistemic_state.rationale: Gaev knows the facts of the situation completely but uses petty social observations to avoid reckoning with them emotionally

beat_states.defense_state: Displacement — channeling his distress into a petty, snobbish observation about herring to avoid the unbearable emotion of departure
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He urges practical urgency ('no time left') but immediately deflects into trivial aristocratic disdain, revealing he cannot actually face the gravity of the moment
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: The herring comment could be a genuine moment of comic relief rather than displacement — Gaev may be so superficial that the smell genuinely bothers him more than the departure

beat_states.desire_state: to dismiss Dunyasha and appear composed and busy when the masters arrive
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to escape this provincial life and return to Paris as a polished, worldly man
beat_states.obstacle: Dunyasha's emotional demands threaten to expose his callousness; the arrival of the family requires him to perform competence

affect_state.rationale: Relieved that the family's arrival interrupts Dunyasha's emotional scene; mildly excited about the imminent departure; feels in control of the social performance

social_state.rationale: Occupies servant status but performs as though above it; cold toward Dunyasha and indifferent to the arriving family except as audience for his competence

known_facts: The family is departing imminently
Dunyasha is emotionally attached to him
He is going away, likely to Paris
hidden_secrets: His contempt for the provincial household and most people in it
His complete indifference to Dunyasha's feelings
false_beliefs: That his Parisian veneer makes him genuinely sophisticated
epistemic_state.rationale: Yasha is socially aware enough to shift behavior when the masters enter, knows the departure timeline, but his self-image is inflated

beat_states.defense_state: performing busyness and nonchalance to avoid emotional engagement
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He is a servant pretending to be above his station, busying himself with luggage while affecting casual sophistication through singing

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: We needn’t get into our carriages for ten minutes.... [Looks round the room] Good-bye, dear house, old grandfather. The winter will go, the spring will come, and then you’ll exist no more, you’ll be pulled down. How much these walls have seen! [Passionately kisses her daughter] My treasure, you’re radiant, your eyes flash like two jewels! Are you happy? Very?

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: Very! A new life is beginning, mother!

beat_states.desire_state: to reassure her mother that the loss is not a tragedy but a liberation — to prove the future is bright
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to break free from the decaying old world and build a meaningful new life through work and purpose
beat_states.obstacle: her mother's visible grief threatens to destabilize Anya's own conviction that leaving is the right thing

affect_state.rationale: Her 'Very!' is emphatic, almost too emphatic — she is performing certainty as much as feeling it. High positive valence from genuine youthful hope, but arousal is elevated because the emotional stakes of this goodbye are immense. Moderate vulnerability — she is young and untested.

social_state.rationale: Anya is in the position of the child being blessed, slightly lower status, but meets her mother with deep warmth and reciprocal love. She is also subtly taking on the role of the one who comforts.

known_facts: The cherry orchard has been sold and the house will be demolished
She and her mother are leaving this place for good
Trofimov has inspired her with ideas about work and a new life
false_beliefs: That a 'new life' of work and purpose will be straightforward and fulfilling
That leaving the old world behind is an uncomplicated good
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya's beliefs are sincere but shaped by youthful idealism and Trofimov's rhetoric; she has no experience of the hardships that await.

beat_states.defense_state: idealization — transforming the pain of loss into an abstracted narrative of renewal and progress
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she is genuinely excited about the future but is also a young woman watching her mother's heart break and her childhood home be destroyed
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Anya's emphatic 'Very!' could be read as slightly desperate self-persuasion — she needs to believe this in order to not collapse alongside her mother.

beat_states.desire_state: to absorb and memorialize this final moment in her ancestral home while finding consolation in her daughter's happiness
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to hold onto what she loves even as everything slips away — to feel fully alive through emotional connection
beat_states.obstacle: the house is being destroyed, the life she knew is over, time cannot be stopped

affect_state.rationale: The passionate kissing, the direct address to the house, the awareness of demolition — she is overwhelmed with grief barely transmuted into tenderness. High arousal from the emotional intensity of farewell. Low control as she surrenders to the moment. Near-total vulnerability as all defenses have fallen.

social_state.rationale: She has lost her estate, her status is diminished, but her warmth toward the house and especially Anya is at its peak — this is Lubov at her most emotionally generous and open.

known_facts: The house will be demolished after they leave
They must leave in ten minutes
Anya claims to be happy about the new life ahead
This is the final moment she will ever spend in this house
hidden_secrets: She plans to return to her lover in Paris and will likely squander what little money remains
false_beliefs: That Anya's radiance and optimism guarantee a genuinely better future
epistemic_state.rationale: Lubov knows the material facts clearly but may be willfully choosing to believe in Anya's optimism as a way to bear the loss.

beat_states.defense_state: displacement — channeling unbearable grief over the house into passionate adoration of Anya
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she is saying goodbye to everything she loves while celebrating her daughter's joy at leaving it all behind
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lubov's passionate focus on Anya's beauty and happiness could be read as a deliberate performance of maternal blessing designed to give Anya courage, masking even deeper despair than shown.

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: [Gaily] Yes, really, everything’s all right now. Before the cherry orchard was sold we all were excited and we suffered, and then, when the question was solved once and for all, we all calmed down, and even became cheerful. I’m a bank official now, and a financier... red in the middle; and you, Luba, for some reason or other, look better, there’s no doubt about it. LUBOV Yes. My nerves are better, it’s true. [She puts on her coat and hat] I sleep well. Take my luggage out, Yasha. It’s time. [To ANYA] My little girl, we’ll soon see each other again.... I’m off to Paris. I’ll live there on the money your grandmother from Yaroslav sent along to buy the estate--bless her!--though it won’t last long.

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: You’ll come back soon, soon, mother, won’t you? I’ll get ready, and pass the exam at the Higher School, and then I’ll work and help you. We’ll read all sorts of books to one another, won’t we? [Kisses her mother’s hands] We’ll read in the autumn evenings; we’ll read many books, and a beautiful new world will open up before us.... [Thoughtfully] You’ll come, mother....

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: I’ll come, my darling. [Embraces her.]

beat_states.desire_state: to reassure her mother and herself that their separation is temporary and that a better future awaits them both
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to break free from the past and build a meaningful, self-determined life
beat_states.obstacle: the pain of separation from her mother and the uncertainty of whether Lubov will actually return or whether the hopeful future she envisions is realistic

affect_state.rationale: She is genuinely hopeful and energized by her vision of the future, but the stage direction 'thoughtfully' and the repeated plea reveal underlying doubt and fear of abandonment. Kissing her mother's hands shows both tenderness and need.

social_state.rationale: She is the child trying to become the caretaker — offering to work and help her mother. High warmth driven by love and the urgency of parting.

known_facts: Her mother is going to Paris
The grandmother's money won't last
She needs to pass exams and find work
They are leaving the estate
hidden_secrets: She may suspect her mother won't easily return from Paris
false_beliefs: That books and study will straightforwardly open a 'beautiful new world'
That the reunion will come 'soon'
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya's youthful idealism is her most consistent characteristic, but Chekhov gives her the 'thoughtfully' moment — a crack in the optimism where she senses the truth of how precarious everything is.

beat_states.defense_state: idealization — transforming an uncertain and painful separation into a narrative of self-improvement and beautiful reunion
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: her confident vision of studying, working, and reading together shifts to 'thoughtfully' — the doubt creeps in; her repeated 'you'll come, mother' is as much a plea as a promise
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Anya's optimism may be entirely sincere and not defensive — she may genuinely represent the generation that can move forward without the weight of nostalgia.

beat_states.desire_state: to convince himself and others that the loss of the cherry orchard has actually improved their lives
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve his dignity and sense of self-worth despite repeated failures
beat_states.obstacle: the underlying grief and loss he is suppressing; the absurdity of his optimism given the circumstances

affect_state.rationale: His gaiety is performed rather than felt. He's trying to maintain a brave face but has no real control over his future. The billiards reference is a nervous verbal tic revealing his underlying anxiety.

social_state.rationale: His social status has diminished — from estate owner to bank employee. He shows warmth toward Lubov by complimenting her appearance, trying to lift the family mood.

known_facts: The cherry orchard has been sold
He now works at a bank
They are leaving the estate for the last time
Lubov is going to Paris
hidden_secrets: He likely knows his bank position is precarious and his nature unsuited to it
false_beliefs: That the resolution of the sale has genuinely calmed everyone
That his new career as a 'financier' represents real stability
epistemic_state.rationale: Gaev's self-narrative about becoming a banker is a fragile construction. He reframes dispossession as resolution.

beat_states.defense_state: reaction formation — converting grief into gaiety, loss into liberation
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: claims everything is 'all right now' and that he's become a financier, but the forced cheerfulness and billiards metaphor ('red in the middle') reveal habitual deflection from real emotion
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Gaev may genuinely feel some temporary relief — the anxiety of the auction is over, and having any structure (the bank job) provides momentary comfort, even if it won't last.

beat_states.desire_state: to hold herself together during the departure and maintain connection with Anya despite the separation
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to be loved and to feel that life still holds beauty and meaning despite constant loss
beat_states.obstacle: the pain of leaving the estate and separating from Anya; the knowledge that the Paris money won't last and she's returning to a destructive relationship

affect_state.rationale: Beneath the composed exterior, she is deeply vulnerable. The embrace of Anya is where real feeling breaks through. She knows the Paris money won't last. Her 'I'll come' is a promise she may not be able to keep.

social_state.rationale: She has lost her estate and social standing but pours warmth into her daughter. The embrace is genuine maternal tenderness in a moment of dispossession.

known_facts: The grandmother's money won't last long
She is going to Paris
She is leaving Anya behind
The estate is lost forever
hidden_secrets: She is likely returning to her exploitative lover in Paris
She knows the money will run out and has no real plan
false_beliefs: That going to Paris represents a viable future
epistemic_state.rationale: Lubov is lucid about the money's insufficiency but refuses to confront the full implications. Her acknowledgment that it 'won't last long' is as far as she allows herself to go.

beat_states.defense_state: compartmentalization — focusing on practical departure details (luggage, coat, hat) to manage overwhelming emotion
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: agrees her nerves are better and she sleeps well, but is returning to Paris and a man who has ruined her financially — she frames flight as recovery
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lubov's calm may be genuine exhaustion-based acceptance rather than suppression — she may have truly passed through grief into a kind of resigned peace.

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: Charlotta is happy; she sings!

utterances.speaker: CHARLOTTA
utterances.text: [Takes a bundle, looking like a wrapped-up baby] My little baby, bye-bye. [The baby seems to answer, “Oua! Oua!”] Hush, my nice little boy. [“Oua! Oua!”] I’m so sorry for you! [Throws the bundle back] So please find me a new place. I can’t go on like this.

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: We’ll find one, Charlotta Ivanovna, don’t you be afraid.

beat_states.desire_state: to express her existential abandonment and secure a future position
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to find belonging and identity in a world where she has always been rootless and alone
beat_states.obstacle: no one truly understands her pain; she is a servant-entertainer whose feelings are dismissed as performance

affect_state.rationale: Deeply distressed and uncertain about her future. The shift from tenderness with the 'baby' to throwing it away and directly pleading reveals desperation breaking through the performance

social_state.rationale: As a governess without a family to govern, she has almost no social standing. Her warmth is directed at an imaginary baby — she cannot direct it at real people

known_facts: She has no place to go
The household is dissolving
No one has arranged for her future
She has no family and no roots
hidden_secrets: The depth of her loneliness and fear of being completely alone
epistemic_state.rationale: Charlotta sees her situation with painful clarity — she knows she is disposable and must advocate for herself

beat_states.defense_state: theatricalization — channeling genuine anguish through her ventriloquist act so it can be dismissed as entertainment if it becomes too raw
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: uses comedic performance to express real grief — the baby she cradles and discards is her own loneliness and childlessness made visible

beat_states.desire_state: to maintain the illusion that everyone is fine and the departure is cheerful
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve his sense of dignity and normalcy despite the collapse of his world
beat_states.obstacle: Charlotta's performance immediately undercuts his forced cheerfulness by revealing deep pain

affect_state.rationale: Gaev is performing gaiety as he did in the previous beat, but his certainty and control are low — he is grasping at any sign that things are okay

social_state.rationale: His status has diminished; he no longer owns the estate. His warmth is genuine but shallow — he notices Charlotta but completely misreads her

known_facts: The cherry orchard has been sold
They are leaving the estate
Charlotta is performing something
hidden_secrets: His own terror about the future
false_beliefs: Charlotta is happy
Everything is truly all right now
epistemic_state.rationale: Gaev consistently misreads emotional reality, projecting optimism as a defense against despair

beat_states.defense_state: denial — projecting happiness onto others to avoid confronting collective grief
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: claims Charlotta is happy when she is performing her despair through grotesque vaudeville
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Gaev may be knowingly performing cheerfulness for Lubov's sake rather than genuinely misreading Charlotta

beat_states.desire_state: to reassure Charlotta and manage the practical fallout of the transition smoothly
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to prove himself a capable, generous man who has risen above his origins — worthy of respect
beat_states.obstacle: his reassurance is vague and possibly empty; he may not actually have a plan for Charlotta

affect_state.rationale: Moderately calm and in control as the new owner, but his reassurance lacks real conviction — 'don't you be afraid' is formulaic rather than substantive

social_state.rationale: He has the highest practical status in the room as the new owner. His warmth toward Charlotta is genuine but somewhat patronizing

known_facts: He now owns the estate
The household staff need new positions
Charlotta is distressed
hidden_secrets: He likely has no concrete plan for Charlotta's placement
false_beliefs: That a practical solution will adequately address Charlotta's existential crisis
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin understands logistics but not the emotional depth of what people are losing

beat_states.defense_state: managerial efficiency — treating emotional crises as logistical problems to be solved
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: positions himself as a caretaker of the old household's people while being the person whose purchase destroyed their world
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lopakhin may feel genuine guilt about Charlotta's displacement and his reassurance may be an attempt to assuage his own conscience

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: Everybody’s leaving us. Varya’s going away... we’ve suddenly become unnecessary.

utterances.speaker: CHARLOTTA
utterances.text: I’ve nowhere to live in town. I must go away. [Hums] Never mind.

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: Nature’s marvel!

beat_states.desire_state: to have someone take responsibility for her future or at least register her plight
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to find belonging and a place in the world after a life of rootlessness
beat_states.obstacle: no one is offering her a home or employment; she is fundamentally alone

affect_state.rationale: Beneath the casual humming is existential dread. She has no home, no family, no plan. The low arousal masks the depth of her distress through habitual performance of nonchalance.

social_state.rationale: As a governess without a position, she occupies the lowest status in this scene. Her 'never mind' is slightly cold — distancing herself from the people who failed to secure her future.

known_facts: she has no home arranged in town
she must leave the estate
no one has made provisions for her
hidden_secrets: the depth of her lifelong loneliness and lack of identity
epistemic_state.rationale: Charlotta is perhaps the most clear-eyed character here about her own situation — she has always known she is alone.

beat_states.defense_state: dismissal — 'never mind' shuts down her own vulnerability before others can ignore it
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she states a desperate fact — homelessness — then immediately deflects it with humming and 'never mind,' as though it doesn't matter
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: She may be deliberately guilting Lopakhin or the family into offering her employment or lodging.

beat_states.desire_state: to have someone acknowledge the pain of dissolution and abandonment he feels
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve his identity and dignity as the world he knew collapses
beat_states.obstacle: the irreversible dispersal of the household leaves him powerless to stop the loss

affect_state.rationale: The false bravery of the previous beat has collapsed into quiet despair. He recognizes his own obsolescence and speaks it plainly, with deflated energy but high vulnerability.

social_state.rationale: He includes himself in the collective 'we' of the unnecessary, acknowledging his diminished place. His warmth toward Varya and the departing household remains.

known_facts: Varya is leaving
the household is dispersing
he has a bank position waiting
the cherry orchard is sold
hidden_secrets: his deeper terror that the bank job won't last and he'll end up destitute
false_beliefs: that his earlier cheerfulness resolved his grief
epistemic_state.rationale: Gaev sees clearly in this moment that the old world is finished and its people are scattering, but he may still underestimate how permanent his displacement is.

beat_states.defense_state: intellectualization — framing personal devastation as a general observation about everyone
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: moments ago he claimed everything was 'all right now' after the sale; now he confesses to feeling unnecessary
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: He may be fishing for reassurance from Lopakhin that they are still needed or valued.

beat_states.desire_state: to deflect the emotional weight of the moment and avoid feeling guilty for others' displacement
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to prove his worth and transcend his origins through material success
beat_states.obstacle: the suffering of people he's connected to confronts him with the human cost of his triumph

affect_state.rationale: Mildly positive on the surface — he's performing ease — but uncertain underneath. He controls the social register by joking, yet the vulnerability seeps through his failure to actually help Charlotta.

social_state.rationale: As the new owner, he holds the highest social status. His remark has a warmth — he genuinely finds Charlotta remarkable — but it's insufficient, sidestepping her need.

known_facts: Charlotta has nowhere to go
the household is dispersing
he is the new owner of the estate
hidden_secrets: his unease about whether his success has made him a better person or just a richer one
false_beliefs: that a witty remark can adequately respond to genuine homelessness
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin knows the facts but chooses not to act on them in this moment, possibly because confronting them would undermine the triumphant narrative he needs.

beat_states.defense_state: sarcasm/ironic commentary — calling Charlotta 'nature's marvel' redirects the tone away from grief
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he is the cause of their displacement yet feels affection for them; his quip simultaneously admires Charlotta's stoic eccentricity and avoids engaging with her homelessness
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: His comment may be genuine, uncomplicated admiration for Charlotta's resilience and oddness, with no defensive motive at all.

utterances.speaker: PISCHIN
utterances.text: [Puffing] Oh, let me get my breath back.... I’m fagged out... My most honoured, give me some water....

utterances.speaker: GAEV
utterances.text: Come for money, what? I’m your humble servant, and I’m going out of the way of temptation. [Exit.]

beat_states.desire_state: to escape the social obligation and emotional discomfort of being asked for money he doesn't have
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to preserve his sense of gentlemanly dignity despite total financial ruin
beat_states.obstacle: Pischin's arrival forces a direct confrontation with his powerlessness and lack of resources

affect_state.rationale: His cheerful mask cracks — the reflexive assumption that Pischin wants money reveals Gaev's deep shame about his financial ruin. He flees rather than face the interaction, showing how thin his composure really is.

social_state.rationale: His self-description as 'your humble servant' is ironic deflection, not genuine warmth. He literally exits to avoid engagement, which is a withdrawal of social connection masquerading as humor.

known_facts: He has no money to give
The estate is sold
He is about to leave for a bank position
Pischin has borrowed money from the family before
hidden_secrets: His deep shame at being unable to fulfill his former role as generous landowner
false_beliefs: That Pischin has come to ask for money — this may be wrong
epistemic_state.rationale: Gaev assumes based on pattern that Pischin wants money. This assumption reveals more about Gaev's anxious self-consciousness than about Pischin's actual purpose. He doesn't wait to find out the truth.

beat_states.defense_state: preemptive self-deprecating humor to avoid the humiliation of being asked and having to refuse
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: Just moments ago he was claiming everything was 'all right now,' but Pischin's arrival instantly exposes his fragility and desire to flee
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Gaev may be using Pischin's arrival as a convenient excuse to exit a scene that was becoming too emotionally raw for him — the money excuse is cover for emotional flight.

beat_states.desire_state: to recover physically and request financial help or discuss money matters
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to survive financially despite constant debt and the precariousness of his estate
beat_states.obstacle: physical exhaustion and the social awkwardness of always being the one who needs money

affect_state.rationale: Physically depleted and socially exposed — he arrives in a state of need, panting, which strips away his dignity. His request for water is both literal and a stalling mechanism before addressing the real purpose of his visit.

social_state.rationale: He is the perpetual borrower, always in a lower position. His use of 'my most honoured' is formulaic deference, maintaining a veneer of warmth while occupying a supplicant role.

known_facts: The cherry orchard has been sold
The family is leaving
He likely has some financial news or need to discuss
hidden_secrets: He may actually be coming to repay money rather than borrow — an unexpected reversal
false_beliefs: That Gaev might still be in a position or mood to help
epistemic_state.rationale: Pischin arrives late in the act, possibly unaware of the full emotional toll the departure is taking. In some readings, he actually comes to repay debts, which would be a dramatic reversal of his usual pattern.

beat_states.defense_state: using physical distress as a pretext to delay the embarrassing request
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: arrives breathless and desperate but tries to frame the encounter as casual
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Pischin may actually be arriving with good financial news (the Englishmen finding clay on his land) and is breathless from excitement rather than desperation — Gaev's assumption about money is a projection.

utterances.speaker: PISCHIN
utterances.text: I haven’t been here for ever so long... dear madam. [To LOPAKHIN] You here? Glad to see you... man of immense brain... take this... take it.... [Gives LOPAKHIN money] Four hundred roubles.... That leaves 840....

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: [Shrugs his shoulders in surprise] As if I were dreaming. Where did you get this from?

utterances.speaker: PISCHIN
utterances.text: Stop... it’s hot.... A most unexpected thing happened. Some Englishmen came along and found some white clay on my land.... [To LUBOV ANDREYEVNA] And here’s four hundred for you... beautiful lady.... [Gives her money] Give you the rest later.... [Drinks water] Just now a young man in the train was saying that some great philosopher advises us all to jump off roofs. “Jump!” he says, and that’s all. [Astonished] To think of that, now! More water!

beat_states.desire_state: to understand what just happened — to process the unexpected repayment and make sense of Pischin's sudden appearance
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to prove his worth and mastery in a world where the old order is collapsing — being repaid by the old gentry confirms his position
beat_states.obstacle: genuine disorientation; this moment of unexpected honesty from Pischin disrupts Lopakhin's mental preparation for the departures and his own complicated emotions about the estate

affect_state.rationale: Mildly pleased but mostly bewildered; the shrug and dreamlike comparison suggest Lopakhin is in a destabilized emotional state on this departure day, and this unexpected event further loosens his grip on the controlled narrative he's been maintaining

social_state.rationale: Lopakhin is the powerful figure here — the creditor being repaid — but his surprise softens his usual dominance; warmth is moderate, more bemused than cold

known_facts: Pischin owed him money and he likely never expected repayment
The estate sale is final and everyone is departing
He is the new owner of the cherry orchard
hidden_secrets: His deeper emotional turmoil about the departures and his failure to propose to Varya
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin is pragmatic and well-informed but emotionally opaque to himself; the repayment genuinely catches him off guard, suggesting he had written off the debt

beat_states.defense_state: ironic detachment — 'as if I were dreaming' distances him from emotional engagement
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: The man who bought the cherry orchard and orchestrates everything is genuinely surprised by a simple act of financial honesty — reveals his deep cynicism about human nature coexisting with a wish to be surprised
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lopakhin's 'as if I were dreaming' could connect to his broader sense of unreality about the entire day — owning the estate, the departures — and Pischin's repayment is just one more surreal element in a day that doesn't feel real

beat_states.desire_state: to repay his debts and restore his honor and dignity before everyone leaves
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to maintain his place in the social world despite his perpetual financial precariousness
beat_states.obstacle: his physical exhaustion and the chaotic timing of his arrival as everyone is departing

affect_state.rationale: Genuinely elated and physically overwhelmed — the unexpected Englishmen windfall has given him a burst of manic joy, but his breathlessness and scattered speech reveal a man barely holding himself together, aware that fortune is arbitrary

social_state.rationale: Pischin has always been the lowest-status borrower in the room, but repaying money temporarily elevates him; his warmth is genuine and indiscriminate — he greets everyone with affection and generosity

known_facts: Englishmen discovered white clay on his land, generating unexpected income
He owed Lopakhin and Lubov money
He still owes 840 roubles to Lopakhin
Everyone is leaving and the estate is being dissolved
hidden_secrets: The depth of his past desperation about the debts
His awareness that this windfall may not last
false_beliefs: That repaying these debts meaningfully restores his social standing in a world that is already ending
epistemic_state.rationale: Pischin operates with a mix of genuine information about his land deal and a comic obliviousness to the larger emotional catastrophe unfolding around him

beat_states.defense_state: deflection through breathless chatter and comic anecdotes to avoid the emotional weight of the moment
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: pride in finally repaying debts collides with awareness that this windfall is pure luck, not competence — and that the social world he's repaying into is dissolving
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Pischin's manic energy could be read as a desperate performance — he senses the dissolution of this social circle and is rushing to settle accounts before it's too late, driven more by anxiety than joy

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: Who were these Englishmen?

utterances.speaker: PISCHIN
utterances.text: I’ve leased off the land with the clay to them for twenty-four years.... Now, excuse me, I’ve no time.... I must run off.... I must go to Znoikov and to Kardamonov... I owe them all money.... [Drinks] Good-bye. I’ll come in on Thursday.

beat_states.desire_state: to understand the specifics of Pischin's deal and assess its legitimacy
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to validate his own worldview that business acumen and seizing opportunity is the path to survival
beat_states.obstacle: Pischin is in a rush and only gives partial information

affect_state.rationale: Mildly intrigued and skeptical — the Englishmen detail is unexpected and he wants to verify it. He's calm, in control, but slightly caught off guard by this turn of events.

social_state.rationale: As the new estate owner, Lopakhin holds high status. His question is businesslike rather than warm — he's assessing rather than celebrating Pischin's good fortune.

known_facts: Pischin has suddenly come into money
Pischin is repaying debts he owes to Ranevskaya and others
The estate sale is complete and everyone is departing
hidden_secrets: His own complicated guilt about displacing the family
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin is a shrewd businessman who recognizes the improbability of Pischin's story and wants concrete details.

beat_states.defense_state: intellectual distancing — treats the moment as a business inquiry rather than engaging emotionally with the departures happening around him
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: curious about Pischin's unexpected windfall while simultaneously aware that his own purchase of the cherry orchard has displaced everyone around him
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lopakhin may be genuinely happy for Pischin as a fellow self-made man, and his question is one of collegial interest rather than skepticism.

beat_states.desire_state: to pay back his debts, demonstrate his restored solvency, and rush off to continue repaying others
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to maintain his dignity and social standing despite a lifetime of financial desperation
beat_states.obstacle: he is physically exhausted, time-pressed, and still owes money to multiple people — the windfall is not infinite

affect_state.rationale: He's euphoric but winded, genuinely pleased to be repaying debts but barely in control of the situation — rushing, puffing, listing names. The high arousal masks underlying anxiety that this good fortune might not last.

social_state.rationale: Despite having money for once, Pischin's habitual social position as the bumbling borrower persists. He's warm and open but still deferential — he owes these people not just money but years of patience.

known_facts: Englishmen discovered clay on his land
He has leased the land for 24 years
He still owes money to Znoikov and Kardamonov
He has enough to begin repaying everyone
hidden_secrets: The full terms of the deal may not be as favorable as he presents them
false_beliefs: That this lease deal resolves his financial problems permanently
epistemic_state.rationale: Pischin knows the basic facts of his deal but his lifelong pattern of financial mismanagement suggests he may be overly optimistic about its sufficiency.

beat_states.defense_state: manic busyness — uses the rush of activity to avoid deeper emotional engagement with the fact that this household is dissolving
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: experiencing genuine relief and pride at finally repaying debts, yet the frantic pace and breathlessness suggest he knows this windfall is precarious and the habit of crisis is deeply ingrained
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Pischin's rush may partly be an avoidance tactic — he doesn't want to witness the final emotional dissolution of this household he's been connected to for years.

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: We’re just off to town, and to-morrow I go abroad.

utterances.speaker: PISCHIN
utterances.text: [Agitated] What? Why to town? I see furniture... trunks.... Well, never mind. [Crying] Never mind. These Englishmen are men of immense intellect.... Never mind.... Be happy.... God will help you.... Never mind.... Everything in this world comes to an end.... [Kisses LUBOV ANDREYEVNA’S hand] And if you should happen to hear that my end has come, just remember this old... horse and say: “There was one such and such a Simeonov-Pischin, God bless his soul....” Wonderful weather... yes.... [Exit deeply moved, but returns at once and says in the door] Dashenka sent her love! [Exit.]

beat_states.desire_state: to deliver the news of her departure simply and move forward
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to hold onto love and beauty even as everything she cherishes is stripped away
beat_states.obstacle: Pischin's emotional reaction mirrors and amplifies her own grief, making composure harder

affect_state.rationale: She has accepted the facts but the emotional weight is enormous; her brief, restrained delivery suggests she is holding back a flood of feeling

social_state.rationale: Diminished status as she leaves her home dispossessed; warmth toward Pischin as a fellow relic of the old world

known_facts: She is leaving for town today
She goes abroad tomorrow
The estate is sold and the cherry orchard will be destroyed
hidden_secrets: She is returning to her lover in Paris despite everything
epistemic_state.rationale: She is fully aware of her situation and states it plainly; the hidden dimension is the moral complexity of her return to Paris

beat_states.defense_state: compression — reducing the enormity of her loss to a flat, factual statement
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she states her departure matter-of-factly while the act of leaving is destroying her
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: She may be deliberately understating to spare Pischin, or to spare herself from his sympathy cracking her composure

beat_states.desire_state: to say a proper farewell and be remembered as someone who mattered
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to maintain his dignity and social bonds despite his perpetual financial humiliation and awareness of mortality
beat_states.obstacle: the shock of the departure and his own overwhelming emotions make coherent expression impossible

affect_state.rationale: Deeply agitated, crying, overwhelmed by the finality of the farewell; his fragmented speech reveals a man losing emotional control; the request to be remembered after death shows existential vulnerability

social_state.rationale: He positions himself as humble ('this old horse') and deeply affectionate; his kissing of her hand and Dashenka's love convey intense warmth and deference

known_facts: Lubov is leaving for abroad
The estate has been sold
He has just repaid his debt thanks to the Englishmen
He is old and his end may come soon
hidden_secrets: His awareness that this is likely the last time he will ever see Lubov
false_beliefs: 'Wonderful weather' — a desperate non-sequitur suggesting he half-believes normalcy can be maintained
epistemic_state.rationale: He grasps the situation instantly from the trunks and furniture but cannot fully process it; his fragmented consciousness jumps between topics as a coping mechanism

beat_states.defense_state: deflection through repetition of 'never mind' — frantically trying to minimize the catastrophe to manage his grief
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he tries to comfort Lubov while he himself is devastated; he says 'never mind' while clearly minding enormously
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: His speech about being remembered could be partly performative — the comic old man playing his final role — but the stage directions ('deeply moved', 'crying') suggest genuine devastation

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: Now we can go. I’ve two anxieties, though. The first is poor Fiers [Looks at her watch] We’ve still five minutes....

utterances.speaker: ANYA
utterances.text: Mother, Fiers has already been sent to the hospital. Yasha sent him off this morning.

beat_states.desire_state: to reassure her mother and remove an obstacle to their departure
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to help her mother move forward into the new life and leave the old one behind without guilt
beat_states.obstacle: her mother's chronic anxiety and tendency to cling to worries

affect_state.rationale: Anya is in forward-looking mode, relatively composed, eager to move on. She takes on a quasi-parental role of soothing her mother's worries with practical information.

social_state.rationale: Warmly supportive daughter, but in this moment she steps into a caretaking role that subtly elevates her above her mother's helplessness.

known_facts: Yasha told her Fiers was sent to the hospital this morning
They need to leave soon
false_beliefs: Fiers has actually been sent to the hospital — this is almost certainly false, as the play's ending reveals Fiers was forgotten and locked in the house
epistemic_state.rationale: Anya trusts Yasha's report without verification. This false belief is one of the play's most devastating dramatic ironies — she unknowingly participates in the abandonment of Fiers.

beat_states.defense_state: rationalization — presenting the situation as handled and closed, possibly without full verification
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She is confident in relaying information she received secondhand from Yasha, a character known for unreliability, yet presents it as settled fact

beat_states.desire_state: to ensure Fiers is taken care of before she leaves forever
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to maintain her sense of moral goodness and connection to the old life even as she abandons it
beat_states.obstacle: time is running out and she cannot verify Fiers' welfare herself

affect_state.rationale: On the verge of permanent departure, she surfaces a nagging guilt about the old servant. Her checking the watch reveals agitation — she knows time is almost gone and she's grasping at last responsibilities.

social_state.rationale: Still the matriarch voicing concern for her household, but her authority is hollow — she depends on others to have handled things she should have overseen.

known_facts: They must leave very soon
Fiers is old and ill
She has five minutes before departure
hidden_secrets: She suspects deep down that she has failed Fiers by not attending to him personally
false_beliefs: She may believe there is still time/opportunity to rectify the situation if Fiers hasn't been sent
epistemic_state.rationale: Lubov raises Fiers as an anxiety precisely because she is uncertain of his fate — she hasn't confirmed it herself, revealing her characteristic detachment from practical matters.

beat_states.defense_state: displacement — channeling her grief about leaving into a manageable, concrete worry
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She is anxious about Fiers but has done nothing to personally ensure his care, revealing the gap between her emotional concern and her capacity for practical action
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lubov may be using the worry about Fiers as a pretext to delay departure, unconsciously looking for reasons not to leave

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: The second is Varya. She’s used to getting up early and to work, and now she’s no work to do she’s like a fish out of water. She’s grown thin and pale, and she cries, poor thing.... [Pause] You know very well, Ermolai Alexeyevitch, that I used to hope to marry her to you, and I suppose you are going to marry somebody? [Whispers to ANYA, who nods to CHARLOTTA, and they both go out] She loves you, she’s your sort, and I don’t understand, I really don’t, why you seem to be keeping away from each other. I don’t understand!

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: To tell the truth, I don’t understand it myself. It’s all so strange.... If there’s still time, I’ll be ready at once... Let’s get it over, once and for all; I don’t feel as if I could ever propose to her without you.

beat_states.desire_state: To agree to propose to Varya while simultaneously needing external scaffolding to actually do it
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To prove his worth and transcend his peasant origins, yet he remains psychologically trapped by class anxiety around the Ranevsky family
beat_states.obstacle: A deep internal paralysis — possibly rooted in class inadequacy, ambivalence about the match, or fear of emotional intimacy — that prevents him from acting autonomously on this matter

affect_state.rationale: Genuinely confused by his own inaction, uncomfortable being exposed, slightly anxious. His 'let's get it over' language reveals this feels more like an obligation than a desire, yet he's willing. Low certainty and control reflect his bewilderment at himself.

social_state.rationale: Despite being the new landowner, he's subordinate here — deferring to Lubov as the authority who must facilitate his proposal. Moderate warmth but not passionate.

known_facts: Lubov wants him to marry Varya
Varya loves him
He has been avoiding the proposal
Time is running out
hidden_secrets: He may not truly love Varya or may be ambivalent about the marriage
His inability to propose may reflect deeper feelings about his relationship to this family and class
false_beliefs: That having Lubov present will solve his inability to propose
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin admits he doesn't understand his own behavior — a rare moment of genuine epistemic opacity about himself. His request for Lubov's presence suggests he believes the problem is situational rather than psychological.

beat_states.defense_state: Intellectualizing the situation as 'strange' to avoid confronting his own emotional avoidance
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He is a decisive, ruthless businessman who bought an entire estate, yet he literally cannot bring himself to propose to a woman without her mother's presence as a crutch
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lopakhin may secretly know he doesn't want to marry Varya but agrees to try out of guilt and obligation to Lubov, expecting it to fail

beat_states.desire_state: To secure Varya's future by pressuring Lopakhin to finally propose before it's too late
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To ensure her loved ones are cared for as she loses everything and departs
beat_states.obstacle: Lopakhin's mysterious reluctance and the rapidly closing window of time before departure

affect_state.rationale: Anxious and sorrowful about Varya's deterioration, frustrated by the incomprehensible delay, but summoning directness in a last-ditch effort. Her vulnerability shows through the tenderness with which she describes Varya's suffering.

social_state.rationale: She positions herself as the matriarch brokering a match, wielding residual social authority over Lopakhin despite her reduced circumstances. Deep warmth for both Varya and Lopakhin.

known_facts: Varya loves Lopakhin
Varya is suffering physically and emotionally without purpose
Lopakhin has been avoiding proposing
They are leaving imminently — this is the last chance
hidden_secrets: She may suspect Lopakhin will never actually propose but needs to try one final time
false_beliefs: That directly confronting Lopakhin will overcome whatever internal resistance he has
epistemic_state.rationale: Lubov sees the situation clearly — the love, the compatibility, the inexplicable avoidance — but cannot penetrate Lopakhin's actual psychology around the proposal.

beat_states.defense_state: Deflecting her own grief about leaving by focusing on solving Varya's problem
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She orchestrates others' futures with maternal authority while being utterly powerless over her own disintegrating life

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: Excellent. It’ll only take a minute. I’ll call her.

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: The champagne’s very appropriate. [Looking at the tumblers] They’re empty, somebody’s already drunk them. [YASHA coughs] I call that licking it up....

utterances.speaker: LUBOV
utterances.text: [Animated] Excellent. We’ll go out. Yasha, allez. I’ll call her in.... [At the door] Varya, leave that and come here. Come! [Exit with YASHA.]

beat_states.desire_state: To avoid being cornered into proposing to Varya while appearing willing
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To prove his worth and transcend his peasant origins — but marriage to Varya doesn't serve this ambition and terrifies him emotionally
beat_states.obstacle: Lubov is actively engineering the proposal situation, leaving him no graceful exit

affect_state.rationale: Nervous, cornered, and avoidant. The champagne comment is classic anxiety displacement. He feels the walls closing in as Lubov takes charge of the situation he's been dodging for years.

social_state.rationale: Despite owning the estate, in this moment he's being managed by Lubov like a reluctant child. His comment about the champagne is socially disconnected, almost cold in its irrelevance.

known_facts: Lubov is about to leave him alone with Varya
Everyone expects him to propose
He has the social and financial standing to marry Varya
He has never been able to bring himself to propose
hidden_secrets: He does not truly want to marry Varya
He may still harbor feelings for Lubov herself
false_beliefs: That he will rise to the occasion when the moment comes
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin knows exactly what is expected. His deflection reveals he knows he probably won't do it, but he hasn't admitted this to himself or anyone else.

beat_states.defense_state: Displacement — he fixates on the empty champagne glasses and Yasha's behavior, filling the space with trivial commentary to avoid engaging with the real subject
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: He has tacitly agreed to propose ('Excellent' from Lubov implies his consent) yet he immediately pivots to irrelevant small talk about champagne, revealing his deep resistance to the very thing he's consenting to
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lopakhin may genuinely intend to propose but is simply processing normal pre-proposal nerves, with the champagne comment being innocent small talk rather than avoidance

beat_states.desire_state: To orchestrate Lopakhin's proposal to Varya before departure — to accomplish this final act of caretaking
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: To preserve love, connection, and the emotional bonds of her world even as everything dissolves
beat_states.obstacle: Time is running out; she senses Lopakhin's reluctance and must act quickly before the moment passes

affect_state.rationale: Animated and purposeful on the surface, but underneath she's anxious and uncertain. She's performing control she doesn't feel, rushing to create the conditions before Lopakhin can deflect.

social_state.rationale: She positions herself as the matchmaker and social arranger, exercising what little authority she has left, driven by genuine warmth and concern for Varya's future.

known_facts: Lopakhin has never proposed despite years of expectation
Varya loves Lopakhin and is waiting
They are all leaving imminently
This is the last possible opportunity for the proposal
hidden_secrets: She suspects Lopakhin may not propose even now
false_beliefs: That creating a private moment between them will be sufficient to produce a proposal
epistemic_state.rationale: Lubov knows the history of non-action but chooses to believe that direct intervention at this final moment can force the outcome. Her optimism is willful.

beat_states.defense_state: Busyness and animated energy mask her anxiety about whether this will actually work
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: She performs cheerful confidence ('Excellent!') while deeply uncertain Lopakhin will follow through — she's rushing precisely because she fears delay will kill the moment

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: [Looks at his watch] Yes.... [Pause.] [There is a restrained laugh behind the door, a whisper, then VARYA comes in.]

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: [Looking at the luggage in silence] I can’t seem to find it....

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: What are you looking for?

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: I packed it myself and I don’t remember. [Pause.]

beat_states.desire_state: to propose to Varya as everyone expects, but struggling to initiate the intimate conversation
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to prove his worth and transcend his peasant origins through success and social acceptance
beat_states.obstacle: his own emotional paralysis and inability to be vulnerable in this decisive romantic moment

affect_state.rationale: Nervous and uncomfortable. The restrained laugh behind the door signals everyone knows what's supposed to happen, increasing pressure. He checks his watch — a displacement behavior signaling anxiety and a desire to escape.

social_state.rationale: He holds economic power but is emotionally subordinate to the situation. His warmth is muted — polite but distant, unable to bridge the gap to intimacy.

known_facts: Lubov has arranged this moment for him to propose to Varya
Everyone behind the door is listening and waiting
Time is running out before departure
hidden_secrets: He may not actually want to marry Varya
He is relieved by any excuse to avoid the proposal
false_beliefs: He tells himself he would propose if the moment were right
epistemic_state.rationale: Lopakhin knows the script — he's supposed to propose — but his behavior reveals he may not truly want to follow through. The watch-checking betrays his awareness that he's running out the clock.

beat_states.defense_state: deflection through mundane small talk — asking about the luggage rather than addressing the elephant in the room
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he agreed to propose and knows this is the arranged moment, yet he retreats into banal conversation as if unaware of the purpose
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lopakhin genuinely wants to propose but is simply too socially awkward and emotionally inarticulate to find the words, and the presence of eavesdroppers makes it impossible.

beat_states.desire_state: to hear Lopakhin propose, while desperately protecting herself from the humiliation of wanting it openly
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to find security, love, and a defined place in the world after losing her role as caretaker of the estate
beat_states.obstacle: Lopakhin's failure to initiate and her own pride which prevents her from making the first move

affect_state.rationale: Deeply exposed emotionally. She heard the laughter behind the door — she knows everyone knows. The fake search is a transparent cover for devastating vulnerability. Her pause after 'I don't remember' is the sound of hope beginning to die.

social_state.rationale: She is in the supplicant position — the one who has been brought to the man. Her warmth is slightly withdrawn as a protective measure, her manner deliberately neutral to avoid appearing eager.

known_facts: Lubov arranged this meeting for a proposal
Lopakhin has never actually proposed despite years of expectation
The estate is lost and her future is uncertain
hidden_secrets: She loves Lopakhin and has been waiting for this moment for years
She already senses from his demeanor that the proposal won't come
epistemic_state.rationale: Varya enters with full knowledge of the situation's stakes. Her body language — looking at luggage in silence — suggests she reads Lopakhin's discomfort instantly and begins to understand what's about to not happen.

beat_states.defense_state: displacement activity — pretending to search for something in the luggage to justify her presence and avoid the unbearable silence
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she enters knowing this is the arranged proposal moment yet immediately pretends she came in for a completely different reason, simultaneously hoping and refusing to hope
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Varya has already accepted that Lopakhin will never propose and enters the scene in a state of resigned grief rather than active hope.

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: Where are you going to now, Barbara Mihailovna?

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: I? To the Ragulins.... I’ve got an agreement to go and look after their house... as housekeeper or something.

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: Is that at Yashnevo? It’s about fifty miles. [Pause] So life in this house is finished now....

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: [Looking at the luggage] Where is it?... perhaps I’ve put it away in the trunk.... Yes, there’ll be no more life in this house....

beat_states.desire_state: to connect with Varya and possibly propose, but finding himself unable to bridge the emotional gap
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to prove his worth and secure lasting belonging, which marriage to Varya would symbolize
beat_states.obstacle: his own emotional paralysis and inability to be direct about his feelings; the awkwardness of the arranged encounter

affect_state.rationale: Internally agitated and exposed by the setup, yet outwardly performing calm detachment. The pause after 'fifty miles' reveals his inability to fill the silence with what matters. Melancholy creeps in with 'life in this house is finished.'

social_state.rationale: He is technically the new owner and socially dominant, yet his warmth is restrained and formal — addressing her by patronymic, keeping polite distance rather than intimacy

known_facts: Lubov has set up this encounter for him to propose
Varya expects or hopes for a proposal
The household is dispersing and this is the last chance
Varya is planning to leave for the Ragulins
hidden_secrets: He is terrified of emotional exposure and commitment
He may not truly want to marry Varya but cannot admit it
false_beliefs: That making small talk can somehow lead naturally to a proposal without direct emotional risk
epistemic_state.rationale: He knows exactly what is expected of him in this moment but cannot or will not deliver it, hiding behind factual conversation

beat_states.defense_state: intellectualization — retreating into safe, factual small talk about geography and the house ending rather than addressing the real subject
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: he has agreed to propose and knows this is the moment, yet he steers the conversation toward impersonal topics, sabotaging the very thing he ostensibly wants
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Lopakhin may genuinely want to propose but is so conditioned by class shame and emotional illiteracy that he literally cannot form the words — his deflection is not strategic but involuntary

beat_states.desire_state: to hear Lopakhin propose marriage, to be chosen and given a future with dignity
beat_states.superobjective_reminder: to find security, love, and a sense of home — everything the cherry orchard once represented to her
beat_states.obstacle: Lopakhin's refusal to move beyond small talk; her own pride prevents her from initiating or pleading

affect_state.rationale: She is in acute emotional pain, sensing the proposal will not come. Her line about 'no more life in this house' is a barely disguised expression of grief about her own future. She is at maximum vulnerability — exposed, waiting, powerless.

social_state.rationale: She has been reduced to seeking a housekeeper position — her status is plummeting. Her warmth is suppressed by hurt pride; she answers his questions factually and coolly rather than reaching toward him

known_facts: Lubov arranged this private moment for a proposal
Lopakhin is not bringing up marriage
She has secured a housekeeper position as fallback
The household is ending permanently
hidden_secrets: She loves Lopakhin and has waited years for this
She is humiliated by having to announce her housekeeper plans in front of the man who should be proposing
false_beliefs: That perhaps if she stays quiet and available long enough, he will finally speak
epistemic_state.rationale: She reads the situation accurately — the proposal is not coming — but clings to a fading hope, covering her devastation with practical activity

beat_states.defense_state: displacement — busying herself with luggage to avoid confronting the devastating silence where a proposal should be
beat_states.psychological_contradiction: she desperately wants him to speak but actively avoids eye contact and redirects her attention to luggage, making it harder for him to propose
beat_states.alternative_hypothesis: Varya may already have fully given up and is performing composure not out of hope but as a final act of dignity before permanent separation

utterances.speaker: LOPAKHIN
utterances.text: And I’m off to Kharkov at once... by this train. I’ve a lot of business on hand. I’m leaving Epikhodov here... I’ve taken him on.

utterances.speaker: VARYA
utterances.text: Well, well!

beat_states.desire_state: to escape the unbearable intimacy of the moment by retreating into business talk
